content.question:
Thanks.



Slide 5

   - Does the projection of 132% RR reflect the expectation of how we will
   end the month?

We probably need to improve on our accuracy if so..



Slide 14

   - Which category is VN strong in for SIP?

Does it make sense to explore
   direct selling to other markets?
For eg. MY?

Slide 18

   - Does this issue impact SBS sellers?
- How does it impact TT in their business?

Slide 19

   - How do we use this chance to push sellers to use SBS?

Slide 20

   - @Yuna Kwon <yuna.kwon@shopee.com> is the impact here aligned with your
   thinking?

Feels lower than what you were saying



Slide 21

   - Seems like TT does not need the custom declaration?

Which means that
   they are more lax than us?

Slide 23

   - Typically even if they convert to “local”, it depends on whether the
   decision maker sits. For most KR sellers and certain CN sellers, decision
   maker still sits in home country, so account management will need to be in
   home market.



Slide 24

   - Even if we go with consignment model for CB sellers, it is still
   acceptable legally right?
Just need additional HC to support this?

- There is probably revenue implication given higher comms for CB
   sellers, so think we can probably argue that consignment is probably ok
   net-net



Others

   - @Ning Li <ning.li@shopee.com> any issue regarding Lovito that we would
   like to bring up?

Handover on SLS pnl from this week (from CB SLS Team to KRJP Team)
New P&L target for each market also need alignment with local markets

content.answer:
it will only have 0.08-day reduction as the leadtime of subsequest
      process cannot be shortened(e.g. KR airport do not receive cargo after
      10pm, WH scan for 2nd pick up likely to be after 12am etc)  and 2) will
      incur 30~50% increase of operation cost(e.g. After hour labor
cost) of TWH
      leading to SHP cost increase



Slide 46

   -

   Does it make sense to push Video for JP?
   -

      Yes I think we can push for video in JP, need to look more in to
      details

Out of total video orders only 30% of Shopee video order is generated by
AMS-Shopee Videos, meaning 70% are seller generated videos
* However need to double check the definitions

Live stream’s ado is 50% AMS and 50% non AMS, meaning if platform
focuses more on video KOL sourcing we may also see potential by +20% ratio
for AMS Video orders


So in conclusion, I need to deep dive into JP sellers more however as the
baseline of the JP is very low, it would make more sense to push videos
rather than pushing for LS which is significantly for Mall sellers



Slide 53

   -

   Has this already been budgeted for this year?
   -

      Not yet. Feb SLS budget just submitted to CBBI team with current LH
      quotation(not the new LH quotation previously we got from 3PLs for ‘25).
      Once the ‘25 LH RFQ is resumed and awared(target date Q1), KRJP
will submit
      the budget accordingly for that month onward as from Feb KRJP logi will
      absorb SG SLS scope for KRJP



Slide 56

   -

   Do we need to make so much from JP while we are just starting? Shall we
   aim to make prices more competitive instead?
   -

      Yes we should revisit the rates, now that JPTW SLS launch date has
      been accelerated due to local team’s concern on compliance/fraud
risks from
      non-SLS channel. Current rates already had been set to be lower than
      competitors’ (mainly JP Post) but we’ll review together and
re-nego on JPVN
      rates with the 3PL. Below is reference for our last competitor rate
      analysis (TH also lower), but will update.



Slide 59

   -

   How are doing on LFF targets? Joanne Lee <joanne.lee@shopee.com>
   -

      (As of Jan 16) Updated RR at 97% after 15 Jan campaign
      -

      (As of Jan 13) Jan LFF dropped 27% MoM (RR 83%), with all markets
      regressing except VN. LFF penetration now at 23.2%, -2.4%p MoM
same period
      M-1).
      -

         SG beauty brands contributed to 37% of the decline, 23% due to
         OOS, while TH beauty brands contributed 24%, with 40% of
regression from 1
         beauty brand seller transitioning to local shop)

Apologies. Yes will send at least 1day before the meeting

Pls find answers below




Slide 5

   -

   Missing RR by so much? Is it caused by any particular market? Think all
   markets are hitting topline ADO
   -

      When including Jan 15th data (just got data today), KR ADO/ADG EoM RR
      projection is updated to 100%/95%. The previous 95%/91% ADO/ADG RR was
      projection based on actual Jan 1~14th data, and our projection tracker
      could not fully reflect Jan 1

Thanks.



#2 – I think we wanted to have a view of existing KR sellers and their
coverage across markets right now?

That should be a low hanging fruit that
we should engage



#3 – I was referring more to the seller centre that we are building for
MYSG/IDMY etc. Would this help JP sellers migrate their SKUs easier to
these new markets?

On deadline, can see if we get some of these more action points done
earlier?

content.answer:
Hi Ian,

Please find the weekly SIP update for your review (TB updates in
attachment). Key highlights for this week are


   -

   Performance
   -

      Topline 98% RR, slightly missing target due to 1) raya ramp up slower
      than expected for IDMY SIP; 2) MY CB Choice LFF low RR due to revision of
      CB choice LFF IB criteria and target. Catch-up plan in place for 1)
      to back on track by end month, and Feb target revised down for 2)
      -

      P&L P+SA achieved breakeven per management direction; combined P&L at
      Shopee level healthy at $87k daily, +17% MoM
      -

      Logistics/ops/product performance on track. CB BPM started requesting
      IDMY seller center (similar to MYSG seller center); target go-live Q1
      (same time as MYSG seller center)
      -

   Key projects
   -

      BN successfully launched on 17 Jan. MTD ADO 4,224. Reactive PR
      pending Reg marketing feedback by 21 Jan
      -

      In progress of preparing for IDMY Direct selling pilot to be launched
      by mid-Feb. The ID acquisition team targets 50-200 pilot sellers from
      ST managed muslim/men’s/women’s fashion/BKF. Target acquisition
completion
      by 24 Jan
      -

      Taobao fashion MTD ADO 3.9k (+32% MoM), VN/TH/TW to start marketing
      push next week
      -

      Taobao furniture to complete 1st round pitching for listing migration
      with 48 GGPs before CNY



Performance Dashboard (as of  15 Jan)

A/ Topline/P&L


Highlights

   1.

   [Topline] Jan proj ADO 155,441 (98% RR, -3% MoM, ADO as %CB +0.50pp
   MoM); proj ADG $1.6M (100% RR, -1% MoM, ADG as %CB +0.83pp MoM)
   1.

      Local SIP ADO proj 99% RR, ADO as % CB +0.5pp MoM. Missing target due
      to 1) MY Raya trends slower to ramp up than expected; 2)
marketing spending
      running conservatively to control P&L. Expect to catch up target in 2nd
      half of the month
      2.

      CNSIP ADO proj 105%

content.answer:
#2 (MYSG seller center) PRDing. Target to be reviewed on 20 Jan. Target
   Q1 Live

#3 (IDMY seller center) Kicked off with biz team. Target to align high
   level solution with ID local by late-Jan



When will the seller centre go live? There will be further development work
required for this?

   -

   MYSG SC: target 31 Mar
   -

   IDMY SC: target 30 Apr, concrete ETA to be provided by early Feb
   -

   For IDMY SC, we will request to support configurable systems to any new
   lanes which shortening subsequent lane launch time


How about VNMY?

   -

   VNMY SC: target 30 May
#6 (FBS x SIP) PRD review by PM teams to be arranged post-CNY
#7 BRD review kickoff with PM ETA 17 Jan

#8 BRD reviewed with LATAM product team on 14 Jan, PRD review kickoff on
   16 Jan. To finalise PRD w/e 24 Jan

#9 RSX approved as P1 as of 17 Jan



Key projects updates



F/[Special Project] Brunei Launch

Brunei officially launched on 17 Jan, with 1) SHP MY app published on BN
   Google Play Store and Apple Store; 2) allowing BN phone number registration
Reactive PR: awaiting feedback from Reg Marketing [ETA: 21 Jan]

Performance (as of 16 Jan）

* BN users defined as 1) users registered with BN phone number; 2) all MY
orders with BN IP address



Have we seen a surge in downloads so far?

   -

   Total App Downloads from Jan 16 onwards is ~1.2k
   -

      App Store: 668 downloads
      -

      Google Play Store: 530 downloads (Jan 16 only; Jan 17-19 data pending)


   -

   305 users registered with BN phone numbers
   -

   We will track app downloads and user registration in weekly dashboard
   going forward

 How do we seed the idea that BN users can now download the Shopee App?

   -

   Per Reg Comms guidance, we will use reactive PR (instead of proactive
   PR) at this stage, as we are not officially serving BN market yet



G/ [Special project] Direct selling expansion

IDMY: target pilot launch 17-Feb

a.              In progress of seller pitching by ID RM team; target to
acquire 50-200 pilot sellers among ST managed local sellers from muslim
fashion/men’s fashion/women’s fashion/BKF categories

b.              In progress of finalizing incentive packages and marketing
support from MYBD team [ETA: 20 Jan]



I thought we are going to use Taobao Fashion package?

   -

   Yes, seller incentive package confirmed to be Taobao Fashion package;
   also confirmed ID sellers will be included in MY’s Raya marketing exposure



c.              In progress of setting up logistics channel and designing
shipping rate card

d.              Next step to finalize the pilot sellers by 24 Jan, and
start shop creation on MY platform by 31 Jan

Other lanes interested in the Direct program

a.              SGMY

i.9/10 (90%) top SGMY SIP sellers interested in opening SGMY Direct shop

ii.In progress of pilot planning, target pilot launch by end-Feb,
contingent on logistics channel timeline



Do we need a SGMY seller centre?

   -

   SGMY SC: target 30 May go-live
   -

   Pilot run and scale-up: from 16 Jun onwards


b.              MYPH

i.65/85 (76%) managed MYSG Direct sellers interested to operate additional
markets

ii.In progress of pilot planning, target pilot launch by end-Feb,
contingent on logistics channel timeline



Do we need a MYPH seller centre? Can they be price competitive?

   -

   MYPH SC: target 30 Apr go-live
   -

   Pilot run and scale up: from 12 May
   -

   Overall price competitiveness on this lane is not strong. However, MYPH
   is strong in 2 categories in terms of unique assortment - (a) motorcycle;
   (2) sports and outdoor. We can start with these 2 categories to build
   success case in pilot run




c.              VNMY

i.21/30 (70%) local managed sellers interested

ii.Updated findings to Anh in VN monthly CB meeting; next step for Anh to
feedback on pilot go/no-go decision

d.              TWMY

i.39/64 (61%) local managed sellers interested

ii.Next step to update the findings to William in the next TWCB monthly
management meeting on 23 Jan, for pilot go/no-go decision



Do we need a TWMY seller centre? How about TWSG?

   -

   TWSG SC: target 30 May go-live
   -

   TWMY SC: target 30 May go-live


Taobao Furniture

content.answer:
Hi Ian/JH,

Please find attached deck for upcoming CB Management Meeting with Terence
for your review. A couple of slides WIP has yellow stickers, will be ready
by 20th Jan before 2nd review.

Let me know if you have any questions.

Target timeline to meeting as per below:

   - 1st Review Reply - 20th Jan 6pm SGT
   - 2nd Review Reply - 21st Jan, 6pm SGT
   - 3rd Review Reply (if needed) - 22nd Jan, 12pm SGT
   - Send to TP - 22nd Jan, Wed, 6pm SGT


-- 
*Jason YU*
*Regional CB*
*Mobile*: +65 90284825
*Email*: jason.yu@shopee.com

Hi JH,

Please find responses to questions below. Have also attached V2 of the deck
here.

Slide 51: Is there any reason why ADO% over platform increased but managed
ratio dropped (Jan vs Dec)? It means our managed CNLS grew less than the
unmanaged pool during Dec campaign season?
CNLS managed ratio dropped mainly as a number of previously managed shops
have dropped to unmanaged status during the local re-tiering process.
As seen from table below, managed shops no. remained relatively stable
while unmanaged shop no. grew from 42,653 to 43,154 from Nov to Dec’24.
(Note that the table on Slide 51 is based on Nov and Dec’24 full month
data). Specifically,

   -

   1,182 shops with 87k ADO moved from managed shop list to unmanaged,
   which is the main reason for the drop in managed ratio.
   -

      The majority of these shops come from TH (557 shops with 40k ADO). We
      have confirmed with TH local that these are the sellers who we have lost
      contact with, i.e.sellers who have been not been in contact for
at least 3
      months
      -

      Another 225 shops with 22k ADO come from PH, which we are pending

*Subject:* Re: [For Review] Jan CB Monthly Management Meeting



Hi JH,



Please find responses to questions below. Have also attached V2 of the deck
here.

Slide 51: Is there any reason why ADO% over platform increased but managed
ratio dropped (Jan vs Dec)?

It means our managed CNLS grew less than the
unmanaged pool during Dec campaign season?

CNLS managed ratio dropped mainly as a number of previously managed shops
have dropped to unmanaged status during the local re-tiering process.
As seen from table below, managed shops no. remained relatively stable
while unmanaged shop no. grew from 42,653 to 43,154 from Nov to Dec’24.
(Note that the table on Slide 51 is based on Nov and Dec’24 full month
data). Specifically,

   - 1,182 shops with 87k ADO moved from managed shop list to unmanaged,
   which is the main reason for the drop in managed ratio.


   - The majority of these shops come from TH (557 shops with 40k ADO). We
      have confirmed with TH local that these are the sellers who we have lost
      contact with, i.e.sellers who have been not been in contact for
at least 3
      months
      - Another 225 shops with 22k ADO come from PH, which we are pending
      Local seller performance team feedback. Will follow up and share findings
      once hearing back


   - With another 667 shops of 57k ADO moving from unmanaged to managed,
   overall ~30k net ADO moved from managed to unmanaged, roughly 1% of CNLS
   universe ADO, i.e. 1% drop in managed ratio.



Slide 66: Who is accountable for this?

Local CB SBS warehouse manager or
Shaowen's Reg team?

The owner of this is Local WH Ops; They will plan for capacity using the
inbound forecast sent by CN LFF Ops on a weekly basis and approve to adjust
W+1 quota to cater to incoming demand spikes reasonably.

Slide 82: Have we considered the "removal of VAT" and "SPX discount"
factors for TW in this version?

Yes, we have considered the two factors and updated the data already in
this version. The main reason for 1% YoY increase is due to XDD ramp up in
TW and XDD LM rate is the most expensive (45 NTD) in TW 店配 now.

Shourou Li <shourou.li@shopee.com> Could you add one slide to summary our
initiative under "Cost", "Speed", "Expansion" and "Long Term Capability" 4
pillars?

content.question: probably more important we grow TB sellers during the starting period?

and to let buyers know that we have more fashionable sellers on the
platform too?

content.answer:
Hi Ian, happy new year!


probably more important we grow TB sellers during the starting period?

and to let buyers know that we have more fashionable sellers on the
platform too?



   -

   Marketing front, SG team plans to
   -

      Affiliate: Push more TB assortment to our fashion affiliates (incl
      XHS Affiliates)
      -

      Livestream: Daily 8pm live fashion try-on, providing top KOL match to
      sellers in exchange for upsized AMS and exclusive LS deals [ETA 2.15 to
      start daily fashion stream]
      -

      Shopee fashion microsite: feature these assortments more prominently
      -

   From BD side, SG team will supplement support with PRM, paid ads credit
   and seller management


Thank you.
cuiwei

content.question:
Thanks.



Slide 8

   - Who is managing this right now?

I think measurement of CS is more than
   just CRR
   - I do not know how we are managing for upselling/conversion, how we are
   managing for encouraging of buyers to rate well/change rating etc.



Slide 9

   - On expanding of seller centre to other lanes, what is the latest
   timeline around this?

Slide 13

   - For each export market, what is the charging mechanism?

Shall we have
   something that is standardized across the markets?

So we can properly
   measure impact (what is it compared to now, what is the decrease/increase
   in orders, what is the net increase/decrease, impact to overall platform?)
   Instead of going market by market to negotiate
   - In my mind, it should be COGS +, the true cost to serve plus some
   margins..



Slide 15

   - The acceptance rate is probably too low to be even meaningful?

Slide 23

   - What is the ROI that we should be aiming for?
- How do we scale this meaningfully?

Slide 24

   - How do I understand this table?
- We probably should not be spending Ads on PC2 negative SKUs?

Slide 25

   - How does this impact overall PC2 of this lane?

Does PC2 include paid
   ads spending here?

Slide 35

   - Why would top few GGPs stop investing over CNY period in MY?

That does
   not sound right?

Slide 36

   - What is the % of TB fashion as total CB fashion in these markets after
   this year?

Not quite sure very meaningful yet



Slide 39

   - How about other markets?

Slide 40

   - Is there anything we are doing for MY?

Slide 44

   - Nothing seemed to have changed much?

Slide 48

   - These sellers will migrate and sell via SLS?
- Pitching status feels very slow?

Slide 53

-          IDMY cannot launch earlier?

Slide 54

-          So we will not have pilot sellers to sell during Raya period?

Others

-          For SIP, what is the % of sales via LS/Video right now?

How do
we measure vs top CB sellers?
-          For SIP, what is the % of sales via AMS right now?

How do we
measure vs top CB sellers?

-          Are we using the above to further improve our conversion of
sales?

content.answer: [ID/VN/MY export] RM price nego

[All lanes] Regional seller discount product

P MP fees (seller priced into price)

Passed back from P MP to SIP standalone P&L (w.e.f. Mar’25)

Yes

None (completed)

P BPSF

Same as local order

No

Propose to continue nego by markets following regional guidance to charge
based on true costs
VN: already fair charge
MY: aligned with MY, pending Ian approval
ID: ongoing alignment with Christin

Rest markets: ETA end Q1




Slide 15

   -

   The acceptance rate is probably too low to be even meaningful?
   -

      The 3% acceptance rate is for the 20% price discount program which is
      expected
      -

      MY is in-progress of 10% price reduction pitching, including 73%
      unresponsive to 20% tier pitching; ETA end-Feb



Slide 23

   -

   What is the ROI that we should be aiming for?
   -

      We will target the CNCB ads ROI for their new SKU and old SKU first,
      and continuously improve from there. For reference



   -

   How do we scale this meaningfully?
   -

      For new SKUs, we have a tool to automate the new SKU ads adjustment
      process and will roll out gradually from IDMY to other lanes to prevent
      significant topline impact
      -

         From biz side, we are constrained by budget (there is only so much
         we can release from old skus, if we want to have more budget
we need to
         raise price which will hurt price competitiveness)
         -

      For old SKU, we will raise a product tool to automate multiple cycles
      for ads cut



Slide 24

   -

   How do I understand this table?
   -

      After we reduced daily ads expenditure for existing ads SKU group
      from $386 to $302 (-22%), this group of SKU’s ADO didn’t change much
      -

         Indicating these SKUs have low dependency on ads
         -

         PC2 for these SKU improves by 1% due to ads cut



   -

   We probably should not be spending Ads on PC2 negative SKUs?


   -

   Yes, our goal is to eventually remove ads on PC2-ve SKUs; only keeping a
   very limited number of selected loss leaders to drive shop traffic/build up
   sold count
   -

   Execution wise, we will do by batches to minimize significant topline
   impact


Slide 25

   -

   How does this impact overall PC2 of this lane? Does PC2 include paid ads
   spending he

Hi Ian,

Slide 8

   -

   Who is managing this right now? I think measurement of CS is more than
   just CRR
   -

      SIP CS management:
      -

         Local CS agents operate SIP chats, shared with other internal biz
         (e.g., SBS)
         -

         A local SIP CS TL is assigned in each local team to manage
         SIP-related agent matters
         -

         SIP Ops team to 1) monitor the local CS performance, 2)
         collaborate with local CS team

For Direct Selling sellers forecast, we aim to have ~8K to 12K selling
sellers by the end of 2025, driven by top SIP shop conversions and new
seller acquisitions.

Slide 37

   -

   Why is this seller so strong in VN? Are they selling our other markets
   as well? Does it make sense to push them?

Why is this seller so strong in VN?


   -

   KK (LazBao) launched as Seller-own-fleet with Shopee.
   -

   2 main strengths: (1) cheaper price as result of logs cost saving from
   self-handled E2E logistics (2) sourcing capacity over other CB sellers with
   diversified assortments, able to cover assortment gaps for CB in FA, HL

Are they selling our other markets as well?


   -

   Yes, they are selling in Vietnam and Thailand as Seller-Own-Fleet on
   other platforms (LZD, TTS) with total SOB 49% from SHP VN, 20% from SHP
   TH, followed by LZD TH and TTS VN
   -

   KK officially integrated under CB SLS due to poor operation performance.
   After integration, TH saw a -90% drop in ADO, and VN -65% drop

Does it make sense to push them?


   -

   After integration, we maintain similar push for KK as other strategic CB
   sellers due to (1) KK performance on par with other sellers and (2) KK
   is maintaining good pricing and assortment strength in categories i.e:
   Women Shoes and Bags, Home & Living.
   -

   From competitor perspective, KK launched self-delivery in TTS from
   Aug'24, achieving 18-20% MoM growth in TTS VN.
   -

   We will observe KK topline/P&L performance and level of cooperation to
   engage them by a dedicated seller program if needed in Q2'25


Regards,
Lisa

Slide 7

   - We outperformed P&L by so much?

- VNCB outperformed PnL because: (1) MP PnL target RR 132%, as a result
      of conservative target setting to buffer CB VAT impacts, and (2) SLS PnL
      target RR 109% due to better profit/order from channel mix change (shift
      from lower profit margin channel to higher profit margin channel) and
      higher liquidation revenue.

Given VAT impact is less than expected, do we need to reproject for the
rest of the year?

Slide 12

   - What is the LFF% for Lovito right now?

LFF here refers to NN warehouse
   right?
Is there any value in going into VN local WH for Lovito?

- MTD LFF ado% for Lovito 9.4%, referring to shipped from Nanning
      Warehouse
      - Lovito shipping to local WH cost will be higher, because last mile
      logistics cost + warehouse handling fee is high, unable to cover
the saving
      of the first mile. Also blackstock clearance in NN can get
higher pay from
      vendor because CN vendor black stock quotation is higher than
local vendor.

What is required for Lovito VN to be fulfilled from VN local FBS WH?

content.answer:
Hi Ian,

Please kindly find our reply below:

Given VAT impact is less than expected, do we need to reproject for the
rest of the year?

   - We propose no adjustment in March topline target yet due to (1) VAT
   impact needs longer time to observe the impact since effective date is just
   from 18 Feb, (2) in March, platform direction is to push high abs order &
   optimize %PC2 further which will impact CB ADO contribution. We will keep
   monitoring and adjust target if needed in the next cycle.

What is required for Lovito VN to be fulfilled from VN local FBS WH?

I think we should explore having some SKUs fulfilled locally on top of NN

   - Similar to our conclusion for pushing KR CB local FBS WH, Lovito needs
   to have a local entity in VN to open a NEW LOCAL SHOP. As such, Lovito will
   strictly follow white channel import flow which will incur higher costs
   compared to NNWH model and complexity in tax/payment flow

If you need further information, please let me know.

Regards,
Lisa

content.answer:
Hi Ian,

SIP weekly update for your review please. Thank you.

Performance Dashboard (as of 19 Feb)

A/ Topline/P&L


Highlights

   1.

   [Topline] Feb proj ADO 177k, 100% RR; ADO as %CB +0.23pp MoM; proj ADG
   $2.0M, 108%RR; ADG as %CB +0.34pp MoM
   1.

      Local SIP proj ADO 99% RR, ADO as % CB +0.03pp MoM; slight gap in
      target due to 1) IDMY slow recovery in 2H with low platform RR; 2) VNSIP
      prolonged Tet holiday impact
      2.

      CNSIP proj ADO 112% RR, ADO as % CB +0.10pp MoM; above target driven
      by 1) price reduction initiative in PH and VN; 2) LPP push in SG
      2.

   [P&L]
   1.

      Both Local SIP P+SA and CN SIP SA MTD daily PC2 breakeven; rest of
      Feb focus to ramp up marketing spending to maximise topline growth
      2.

      Overall combined PC2 healthy at Shopee level at $115.5k daily, +29%
      MoM. PC2 MoM growth much higher than ADG growth due to 1) ADG has
      higher base; 2) PC2 expected to be lower towards end of month to release
      more budget to marketing


B/ ADO breakdown (multiple BU/lanes)


Highlights

   1.

   IDMY proj 95% RR; Due to 1) 1H budget cut from profit passback VAT
   removal and OPEX issues; slower recovery in 2H with low platform RR. 2)
   Lower than projected Raya uplift with platform raya categories
   underachieving vs last year. Ramp up plan kicked in with 1) new SKUs ads
   push 2) high traffic SKUs product promo set up; observed improvement in ADO
   platform % by 0.16pp on D-1 vs last week
   2.

   MYSG proj 106% RR; high RR driven by successful 2.2 push, overachieving
   target with 128% R we R while ADO CB% grew 1.31pp vs 1.1 spike; expect to
   maintain with Raya assortment push
   3.

   VNMY proj 81% RR; low RR due to 1) slower Tet recovery 2) limited SLS
   rate reduction impact 3) Plt WC/MC ADO decline; GMV RR higher at 96% with
   less rebate discount vs M-1. Ramp up initiatives observed good uplift wit

content.question: ·  PC2 is probably too high?
How does it benchmark to SLS furniture sellers?

- For SG, Taobao furniture PC2 is 5.9% which is lower than CNCB
      furniture PC2 (7.1%).
      - For the other markets, we will update it and update in this
      Friday’s weekly



If we wish to grow this category, maybe our margins are probably too high,
both on CNCB and Taobao front..maybe we should see how we can reinvest into
the ecosystem (maybe via rate table especially on heavier items)

      (~5%). As a next step, we will explore more investment from platform
      together with MY/SG CB team.

      optimize in the weekly update this week

How about other markets?

      in MY before the program kick-off. Please see the summary of
other markets

      - [Free ads fully expense] Target by end of month:


   - SG: 46/69 shops (currently 46 shops complete, vs 12 in email update)
         - MY: 63/86 shops (currently 54 shops complete, vs 30 in email
         update)


   - As discussed in the MY/SG CB monthly management meeting, we will focus
      on pushing 1) SKU migration; 2) seller hitting seller-level ADO targets,
      and push for co-investment if ADO targets not hit



·  #4 – Similar?
Seems very slow?

- Yes, we have called out this issue for Derek’s attention and we’re
      behind target
      - [Fully compliant] Target by end of the month:


   - SG: 14/69 shops (currently 6 shops complete, vs 2 in email update)
         - MY: 29/86 shops (currently 15 shops complete, vs 0 in email
         update)


   - As discussed in the MY/SG CB monthly management meeting, we will focus
      on pushing 1) SKU migration; 2) seller hitting seller-level ADO targets,
      and push for co-investment if ADO targets not hit





Slide 6

·  Only 20-35% by end Feb?
Should it not be 100%?

- 57%: supply chain fully recovered, half of them promise to complete 4
      requirements, and the other half can only complete 2 to 3 due to high
      requirements of LTD/DD/DDV nomination, expect to achieve higher compliant
      rate if MY/SG change to monthly reverse nomination from 3.15
(now 3-4 times
      per month)
      - 29%: supply chain not fully recovered or high PP, which is not
      eligible for campaign exposures
      - 14%: supply chain fully recovered but not cooperative, ok to opt
      out from the program in Mar



Slide 11

·  Not much growth/movement in general?

- Due to CNY break, many sellers reduced commitment in terms of 1) ads
      investment 2) uploading new SKUs and 3) stock replenishment
      - W-1 ads investment has recovered to ~70% of pre-CNY level
      - We expect to recover and catch up in Mar, for 1) SG: top sellers to
      upload 6k wishlist SKUs and ramp up push 2) MY: to migrate
40-50k SKUs from
      SG top sellers

content.answer:
-0.4mil: from Topline missing target: despite overall topline runrate
   okay, MP (3P SLS) runrate at ~93% runrate, this is due to higher runrate
   for Choice and 3PF which has lower/no SLS profit

-0.2mil: from channel mix diff vs target: XDD channel lower than target
   by ~10%, which XDD channel has a higher profit margin than standard channel
   (~0.5usd/pc vs 0.4usd/pc PPO difference)

-0.3mil: from SIP SLS ASF rate reduction (email name: [For your info]
   P&L impact in Feb due to SIP SLS ASF adjustment _PH Lane), suggest to SIP
   to obtain approval from CBBI and PHCB beforehand in future on decisions
   impacting local CB P&L



Slide 22

   - What is the ABS of Local SCS right now? USD 2.64 feels too low. What
   is the item/order ratio?


   -

   Local SCS ABS is $2.72, IPO is 1.64. CN SCS ABS is $2.69, with an IPO of
   1.40. CN SCS focuses on short tail, hot selling items, bringing the ABS
   lower than CB Non-mall at $5.6.
   -

   The low ABS is primarily determined by the low MBS of Platform FSV,
   which are $0, $0.85, and $1.71 for ~90% of the platform FSVs
   -

   CN SCS UE is healthy and on track. The overall PC3% is around 10-15% in
   Feb, which is well above our break-even target of 9-10%.

Slide 24

   - Probably need to watch out for our P&L here, seems like we overspent
   by quite a bit
   - If I look at TB sellers, their ABS is about USD 11 in PH? (Slide 61).
   Our ABS probably need to increase here, it is probably still too low

For Lovito Feb PC3 miss target, mainly due to increasing CPAS Ads KOL
investment, to catch up with S&R imp lost (due to guidelines and
initiatives updates, incl. PID criteria update, cheapest and replacement
experiment, etc).

For ABS

   -

      Lovito has been working on the high ABS assortment series Refine (ASP
      $6+) with MTD ADO 3k; Overall ABS has been increasing for the past 3
      months: Feb MTD $6.25 vs Jan $6.24 and Dec $6.19
      -

      TB WA ABS is $8 ($11 for all categories incl. Lifestyle Shoes top
      brands Anta, XTEP, 361, Lining and Fashion Watches - Daniel Wellington)



Slide 28

   - What is PH exporting out? The ABS looks high

PH exporting out mainly in Fashion, Home & Living and Beauty. High ABS due
to pricing markup and driven up by selling higher ABS price range products
such as sports & outdoor equip

Hi Teams,

Here refer to the meeting minutes and next steps for last week's THCB
monthly meeting.
Sorry for a bit delay on this summary due to several further alignment with
CN LFF side till this week.
No. Topic Action Items PIC ETA
1 THCB SBS ADO Target Setting Discussion Overall:
TH side wants to push more on TH local SBS ADO with 70K~100K ADO by Dec
2025 which will be highly appreciated.
CN side proposed a version with TH local SBS ADO increased from 18.9K into
33.9K dec 2025 via several further initiatives support needed before the
THCB monthly meeting.

5 keys discussion alignment during the meeting:
--Handling Fee Decrease (Rate card): Remain no discount but go with
commission instead
--Commission: Remain commission gap 5% between 3PF and FBS
--Upgraded incentives: remain the same, but consolidate ref requests.
--Bulky inbound restriction: TH ok to go with bulky orders
--TISI: TH cross check with local CMI on the deep summary on TISI CB 3PF
sellers existing SKU

Next Step Actions:
CN LFF Team recalulation 2025 THCB SBS target and resources alignment with
TH side (ETA get back on Mar 14) CN LFF Team : Bufei/Derek
THCB Team: Winnie/Jack 14 Mar
2 CB SOB SHP/TTS Direction: Top CB sellers who lost over TTS, we want to
maintain SOB with compromising PC2 meanwhile THCB side will need to recheck
with local CMI on the top CB seller if it matches with the overall TH
Shopee seller focus pool.

Next Step Action:
To align with TH CMI on the top seller list if top CB seller is actually
matter to plat.
To align on the PC2 bottom line that platform can accept
To Increase mapping coverage and map top sellers with different incentives
of each program >> make sure they don’t get duplicated benefits eg SSP or
other programs.
Review on CNCB new proposal of counter TTS program seller pool
THCB Team: Tiffany 14 Mar
3 Regional SSP update Done and Dhika already approval email for execution
and budget request THCB Team: Tiffany Done



Yu C

content.question: - IDMY should be growing MOM?
Given that it is Raya season?

Any reason
   why it is not the case?

- The key reason is Raya trends this year kicked off much later than
      expected (25 Feb this year vs 15 Feb last year, 10 days late)
      - From 1-24 Feb, MY fashion cluster ADO saw -11.2% MoM. IDMY SIP Raya
      related categories (Muslim Fashion, Women Clothes, Men Clothes) performed
      slightly better than platform despite much lower platform-sponsored ads
      budget than last year, but overall impacted by low platform traffic.
      - From 25 Feb onwards, we saw significant growth in Raya categories.
      MY platform fashion cluster ADO +16.7% MoM, IDMY SIP fashion +45.8% MoM.
      The growth is Driven by
         - Platform level: Extra platform PRM has driven more Raya buyers
         to purchase on Shopee
         - For IDMY SIP specifically
         - Ramped up paid ads for new Raya SKUs
         - Sourced 10K trendy SKUs to push (Hijab, traditional wears, etc)
         - Revived 6 mil SKU into MY since 28 Feb





How else can we grow IDMY more during this period?
- Do we need to teach them on how to handle delivery?

Or expect them to
   figure out themselves?

- We believe they can figure out themselves because
         - Runner services are popular in Brunei
         - Plenty of tutorial videos/advertisements on social media
         - Promoting runner service is risky due to legal concerns over
         their unofficial status



Are we sure about this?

*From:* Cuiwei Sun <cuiwei.sun@shopee.com>
*Sent:* Wednesday, 5 March 2025 10:28 am
*To:* Ian Ho <hoi@sea.com>
*Cc:* Jianghong Liu <jianghong.liu@shopee.com>; Shuning Wang <
shuning.wang@shopee.com>; Keljin Chua <keljin.chua@shopee.com>; Jason Yu <
jason.yu@shopee.com>; Yu Zhang <yu.zhang@shopee.com>; Ailin Xie <
ailin.xie@shopee.com>; Wei Qiang <wei.qiang@shopee.com>; Momo Chen <
momo.chen@shopee.com>; Huang Wan-Jung <wanjung.huangwj@shopee.com>; Chiong
Zhi Ann <zhiann.chiong@shopee.com>; Steven Liu <steven.liusk@shopee.com>;
Wei Loong Yong <weiloong.yong@shopee.com>; Nicholas Lim <
nicholas.lim@shopee.com>; Keith Huang <keith.huang@shopee.com>; Joe Li <
joe.liyc@shopee.com>; Johan Liew <johan.liew@shopee.com>; Ren Jiahui <
jiahui.ren@shopee.com>; Eveline Seah <eveline.seahyl@shopee.com>; Jules
Zhao <jules.zhao@shopee.com>; Tina Tai <tina.tai@shopee.com>; Tzong Meng
Ong <tzongmeng.ong@shopee.com>; Mingkit Tan <mingkit.tan@shopee.com>; Lim
Wei <wei.lim@shopee.com>
*Subject:* Re: SIP Weekly Update - Week ending 28 Feb



Hi Ian,



   - Can we always display P&L for the month?

And we should probably have
   some targets?
Instead of leaving it blank?

- Noted will show P&L on monthly basis, attached below with dashboard
      MTD as of 26 Feb ($2.9M)
      - For SIP P&L, our OKR target is P MP PC1 + SIP standalone PC2
      breakeven, which we are hitting target since Jan
      - The combined PC2 on Shopee level is mainly the MP PC2 collected by
      the A markets


   - We did not set the target as it is a passive result of A market
         platform take rate minus PRM based on the topline we generate
in each A
         market
         - We do have monthly projection - we will include it from next
         update



   - IDMY should be growing MOM?
Given that it is Raya season?

Any reason
   why it is not the case?

- The key reason is Raya trends this year kicked off much later than
      expected (25 Feb this year vs 15 Feb last year, 10 days late)
      - From 1-24 Feb, MY fashion cluster ADO saw -11.2% MoM. IDMY SIP Raya
      related categories (Muslim Fashion, Women Clothes, Men Clothes) performed
      slightly better than platform despite much lower platform-sponsored ads
      budget than last year, but overall impacted by low platform traffic.
      - From 25 Feb onwards, we saw significant growth in Raya categories.
      MY platform fashion cluster ADO +16.7% MoM, IDMY SIP fashion +45.8% MoM.
      The growth is Driven by


   - Platform level: Extra platform PRM has driven more Raya buyers to
         purchase on Shopee
         - For IDMY SIP specifically
         - Ramped up paid ads for new Raya SKUs
         - Sourced 10K trendy SKUs to push (Hijab, traditional wears, etc)
         - Revived 6 mil SKU into MY since 28 Feb



*On Brunei*

   - How are the deliveries being done right now?

How does the
   registration/downloads and buyers/downloads compare with new users in MY?

- Brunei buyers arrange their own runner service. Parcel will be
      delivered to a Malaysian address near Brunei via shopee MY logistics, and
      then the runner collects it and delivers it to Brunei within four days.
      - Pending MY BI provides new user conversion data for BN-MY
      comparison. ETA 7 Mar.
      - Also heads up that we will review the BN entry assessment in end
      Mar as planned, to see any changes on market potential, buyer sentiment,
      and logistics solutions. Will provide review update in April’s
SIP monthly
      meeting



   - Do we need to teach them on how to handle delivery?

Or expect them to
   figure out themselves?

- We believe they can figure out themselves because


   - Runner services are popular in Brunei
         - Plenty of tutorial videos/advertisements on social media
         - Promoting runner service is risky due to legal concerns over
         their unofficial status



*On counter TB*



Slide 6

   - Why so many sellers opt out?

- To clarify, 8 and 9 self-run sellers opted out from SG and MY
      respectively
      - Sellers opted out from SG/MY mainly because they want focus
      capacity on bigger markets like VN/TH, as they still believe SG/MY has
      limited growth potential
      - The ADO impact of these sellers to the program is ~7% for MY, and
      ~10% for SG



Slide 10

   - What is the expectation around growth for March?

- Mar target is 22% ADO growth for SG and 20% ADO growth for MY
      - Target to grow SKU count (+16k for SG, 14% MoM; +52k for MY, 480%
      MoM), with 1) local wishlist SKUs uploaded by sellers; 2) SKUs
migration of
      top GGP to MY; 3) onboarding newly acquired top TB sellers (not on SHP)
      - Maintain/Grow SKU efficiency in MY/SG by pushing the customised
      incentive package with top sellers, which includes sellers sourcing and
      investing in ads/vouchers, in exchange for RM support, comms waiver (SG
      only), PRM support, and other exposure points



   - For MY, how are we intending to show that we have more furniture SKUs
   now?

- MY will increase the marketing exposure to the furniture SKUs via 1)
      Shopee Home Microsite; 2) AMS; 3) SS slots



Slide 14

   - What are next steps then?
If it is not attractive?

content.answer:
Hi Teams,

Here refer THCB weekly update- Week of 28 Feb 2025

1) THCB Budget update

Budget highlights

   -

   Overall CB: Gross ADO lower than target mainly from CNY holiday which
   saw the same trend with LY impact on CNY around -10% ADO and -9% in ADG in
   Feb’24.
   -

   CBMP P&L: Expect PC2 to land at -0.6%. PRM over target mainly from  DDVC
   during campaign period; peak day and payday,
   -

   CBWH P&L: WH ADO MoM -4% mainly from drop in SCS orders fulfilled from
   TH Local warehouse, and drop in NNWH MP orders. WH Revenue MoM -13% due to
   increase in proportion of orders fulfilled from NNWH vs TH Local warehouse,
   where NNWH RPO (0.19) is lower than Local warehouse RPO (0.39). WH P&L MoM
   -23% due to increase in % costs allocated to CB from TH Local warehouse.
   -

   CB Lovito: Feb Seller PnL MoM drop as we self-rebate more on voucher,
   and invested more in ads to drive traffic; ADO and ADGMV missed target
   mainly as traffic loss(SR traffic loss and platform downtrend) and platform
   FSV reduction and OOS which both impacted our CR.

2) THCB OKR update

OKR Highlights

   -

   %ADO and ADG: Overall ADO improved from last week mainly from the
   outperform during Payday period (+105% to ADO target). %ADG still remain
   the same trend as last week behind target mainly from growth of plat.
   -

   LS ADO/ ADG: Shopee Choice and Lovito are the main drivers of both ADO
   and ADG contributing over 14% ADO and 6.4% ADG. For MP sellers, the top 3
   contributors are Mossdoom (+26% MoM ADG), JisuLife (1.2x MoM ADG), and
   Papafeel (+1% MoM ADG) mainly from the view boost initiative.
   -

   VDO ADO/ ADG: Shopee Choice and Lovito are the top contributors of both
   ADO and ADG. MP sellers that show a strong growth and contribute
   significantly are Mossdoom (+16% MoM ADG), Goojoodoq (+32% MoM ADG), and
   Ugreen (+17% MoM ADG).


3) Key updates/ topics for approval

   -

   [For Up

content.answer:
Seasonal downtrend: the downtrend of winter trendy SKUs (e.g. hoodie,
   long-sleeved base shirts, full-coverage crocs) resulted in a noticeable
   decline in CBWH ADO. Meanwhile, sellers’ delay to publish and inbound new
   spring SKUs caused by CNY further slowed down ADO recovery of these
   sellers. Fashion sellers were mainly impacted, with ~1k target gap, account
   for 55% of total ADO gap, key sellers as follows:
Kuadu, target gap 264, accounting for 14% of total ADO gap;
Posee, target gap 239, accounting for 12% of ADO gap;

content.question: *Seasonal downtrend:* the downtrend of winter trendy SKUs (e.g.
Kuadu, target gap 264, accounting for 14% of total ADO gap;
Posee, target gap 239, accounting for 12% of ADO gap;
*OOS issue:* mainly caused by factory shutdown during CNY and sea
Rockbros, target gap 217, 11% of ADO gap;
Mobi Garden, target gap 134, 7% of ADO gap.
Sellers with downtrend issues already published and inbound new
OOS sellers already replenished by the end of Feb;
In Mar, will allocate more free ads and item rebate resources to help
TH encounters the biggest impact of winter-spring trend changes
Key sellers mentioned above are not ranked top in other sites.

content.answer: Projection of the OOS and the volume to replenish

Push the sellers to replenish

*For 1), MP FBS replenishment SOP is established based on the OOS forecast:
*

MTD OOS ADO = Σ((OOS daysa * model-level OOS orders on the OOS dayb) / MTD
days of the month) for all FBS models in this site. (This definition are
aligned with Reg PMO)

*For 2), we will replenish based on the seller tiering: *

[OOS ADO coverage 75%] Each week, Top 100 OOS ADO shop by site will be
   pushed to do replenishment by RM team 1 by 1

[OOS ADO coverage 25%] Every 2 weeks, mass seller communication to do
   replenishment push through TS tool


However, the current OOS data have some visibility issues that Reg PMO is
fixing together with us, we expect to resume by EOW.



Best,
Derek


Notes on the formula:
OOS days is defined as # of Days FBS SKU stock is 0

model-level OOS orders on the OOS day is the total orders for the model
   over the L60D /sellable days in the L60D * adjustment factorc

adjustment factor is to normalize the model’s OOS orders impact with L3
   cat level for ADO movement of L60D of "D0" last year, to reflect the
   seasonality and eliminate the outliner impact.

content.answer:
Hi Ian,

How does sellers send to us right now?


Sellers handle FBS first mile by themselves. Shopee will recommend managed
3PLs with more stable lead time performance for sellers’ reference, but
does not actually involve in first mile.

   -

   How seller chooses 3PL: values most on convenience (does not want to
   spend much effort in first mile selection as it takes up only a small
   portion of the total cost). More big sellers use unmanaged while mid-long
   tail sellers use managed 3PL as Shopee recommends.
   -

   3PL solution: seller ship to 3PL’s CN warehouse, 3PL will then complete
   the shipment to CBWH including custom clearance.



 Does it make sense to arrange regular linehauls every week? So we create a
habit for sellers to replenish?


Yes, the direction is to more proactively manage sellers to replenish and
to support weekly linehauls shipments.  SLS team is pushing integrated B2B
channel (Shopee official first mile solution) with shorter and more stable
E2E leadtime, also saving sellers from scheduling inbound after custom
clearance, expected to go live in end of Q2


Attached please find the plan for the Shopee offical first mile plan from
our SLS team for more details.




Best,

Derek

3PLs with more stable lead time performance for sellers’ reference, but

content.question:
Thanks.



Slide 5

   - Does this take into account increase in comms rate across various
   markets?
- Heard there is some VAT issue, how will that change the picture?
- Will we be able to maintain this profitability April onwards?

- For SG&A budget, we should probably adjust future months budget so
   that it is more accurate?
- When will we have the view that includes SLS cost?

Where did we land
   on how much discount SLS should give us as a big seller?

If we benchmark
   against what they charge other big sellers?

Slide 7

   - Do not understand the BR part
   - ADO >5 includes all markets right?

What is stopping us from reducing
   the ADO requirements to get a wider assortment?

Will we be able to get
   higher ASP items?
- Can explain the bundle situation a bit better?

We can probably play
   with variations a bit better?

For eg. 1 item is $1 but 3 items is $2.50 but
   it is all in 1 listing?

Slide 9

   - How are we measuring backlog rate % right now?
- For pre-sales, how do we measure the conversion rate?

- For the productivity metrics, how do we compare with local scs/SIP
   right now?
- For Taiying, which markets do they serve?
- For BSV, which markets do they serve?
- We wanted to split pre-sales and post-sales?

Is our system able to
   differentiate this right now?

I am not quite sure..



Slide 10

   - So who exactly are we using from march onwards?

The thinking feels
   very haphazard, the backlog so bad, not sure we manage this very well right
   now..



Slide 14

   - For each of this category, how are we thinking about suppliers that we
   source from?

How are we thinking about our allowance in terms # of  stock
   that we bring in as well as blackstock allowance?
- For #3 – How exactly will we identify these SKUs?
- For #4-  How exactly will we identify these SKUs?

Slide 16

   - Can we have ADO numbers as well for this table?

- I need to be able to reconcile the Not in SPU with the #1- #4 on Slide
   14
   - How about Not in CSPU but with Similar Match, where do I find them?
- If we look at contribution of ADO of not in CSPU it is non-existent?
Why is that the case?
- What is the blackstock % of the models Not in CSPU?
- What is the difference in UE of models in CSPU and Not in CSPU?

- In general, we need to probably go much deeper on why we want to grow
   this
      - Is it increasing in orders (right now I do not see it)
      - Is it better margins (right now I do not see it)
      - How this will impact our BS (right now it feels like it is going to
      be very high because the ADO is very low). But maybe it is not
because the
      qty for each model not in CSPU may be low?

Slide 18

   - So should we increase our LFF ADO %?

- For VN and TH, think it is important for us to differentiate between
   NN and Local WH



Slide 19

   - This is very odd, how does the CR numbers compare with SLS sellers who
   use FBS?
They will have the same problems that you are talking about?
- If this was indeed the case, they probably will not want to use FBS?

Slide 28

   - Where did we land on BS consideration?

Slide 29

   - How come SG&A fluctuate so much from Jan >> Feb >>  March?

Slide 31

   - Can we also break it down into listings of various ASPs?

- How do I tell if the ADO % (based on category and ASP) is going
   according to plan?

Slide 32

   - How does the ADO % mix compare with listing % mix right now?

How do we
   tell if it is the right mix that we should be aiming for?

Slide 33

   - 43% is still lower than the Malay buyer base on Shopee
   - Can check with Saovanee on this?

I think it is probably ok as long as
   we are not going into Muslim wear?
And we are not stealing the designs?

- But things like bags/shoes we can definitely push more to them



Slide 35

   - What is the language right now that we are using for MY LS?
- What is the language that we are using for SG LS?

Will we be testing
   for English?

Slide 39

   - So we cannot get in-app assets from FF?

- What is the ADO expectation that we are anticipating from this
   collaboration?

Slide 45

   - Our inbound productivity seems quite low?

And lead time to inbound
   seems long as well?
- How do we define % idle time here?
Feels quite high?
- Can remind me if our WH Ops is 24/7 right now?

Slide 47

   - Not sure I understand this table very well. What is the difference
   between Base Scenario and FBS Scenario?
- Can remind me what our previous projections was?

Slide 52

   - On rate card, where did we land on this in the end?
- How many sellers were we thinking off and the ADO previously?

Is 7
   good/bad?

Slide 54

Slide 55

That cannot be right?

Slide 14

because the

content.question: Where did we land on #5?

content.answer:
Hi Ian,

I am asking team to build a holistic performance tracking, including volume
and quality of chat bot, human agents, and split by pre-sale and
after-sales etc, and to compare with SIP and local SCS to identify our gaps
and come up with improvement plans.

The tracking will have more data than any other CS trackers that you have
seen, but it take a bit longer time than expected, and we aim to update you
by this week, and to have more discussion on our next week biweekly catchup.

Thanks!


*Best regards,*

*LI Ning*
SCS

Ian Ho <hoi@sea.com>于2025年3月19日 周三下午7:15写道：

Hi Ian,

After a comprehensive performance benchmark to local SCS & SIP (by
chatbot/agent, presales/aftersales, etc.), here are our preliminary
findings of our strengths/gaps and improvement plan:

In general, our human agent performance (CSAT, ART, Cost per chat, working
hour scheduling, order conversion rate) is better than local SCS and SIP.
However due to recent low productivity of new hires and lack of sufficient
agents, buyer average queuing time is longer than local SCS and SIP, which
we will expedite the new hire training progress and adding new agents in PH
as per communicated in another email thread. On the other hand, our chatbot
deflection rate is much better than local SCS, however we found there is
some gap in conversion between chatbot and human agent performance, which
we will work with BPM to see how to improve chatbot conversion, thus to
further grow the sales.

Moreover, we found comparing to SEA, our BR CS performance isn't as good.
Besides BR using a different chatbot model, we think the time zone,
language and culture differences are also the reasons while hosting BR CS
in Asia. We will study more and update in next step.

More detailed findings as following:

   -

   CB SCS performs better at: 1) Agent CSAT; 2) ART (avg. response time);
   3) Cost per chat; 4) Working hour scheduling; 5) Agent order conversion rate
   -

      Agent CSAT
      -

         Performance: CB SCS (SEA+TW) 80.0%, local SCS 76.9%, SIP 70.3%
         -

         Reason: CB SCS sets agent CSAT as core KPI of external vendor
         (weight 50%), which linked to monthly bonus paid to them
         -

         Next step: We observed the CSAT of new hires (70%~75%, ~22 agents)
         is significantly lower than existing agents (80~85%, ~26
agents). We have
         started daily standup meeting last week with new-hires to accelerate
         improvement, and have asked BSV (our CS vendor) to replace
those new hires

      location markets, covering top 5 lanes

improve CSAT [ETA 10 Apr]

§  All export lanes from ID (including IDSG, IDMY, IDPH): Reduce high
abandonment rate [ETA 11 Apr]



Slide 5

   - Not quite sure what we are going to do and when?

Especially for the

Hi Ian


See answers below:


Slide 10

   -

   How does the LLM + NLU co-exist?
   -

      LLM is mostly used in pre-sales scenarios, while for post-sales
      enquires, it will more rely on NLU (directly get fixed answer from biz
      maintained intent knowledge base)
      -

   If we look at deflection rate and CSAT, how does the LLM compare with
   NLU at the moment?
   -

      Neither product team nor us did a fair AB testing before. If we want
      to make an objective comparison, it would be best to wait for
the A/B test
      for choice/lovito/SIP LLM rollout at the end of March.



   -

   What is the plan moving forward for all 3 business lines? Will we be
   using LLM or NLU or both and when?
   -

      SCS/Lovito/SIP shopbot will all use LLM+NLU combination
      -

      Rollout plan:
      -

         Now - March 19: A/B  test preparation, solution review &
         customization, UAT
         -

         Late March - Early April: A/B testing, LLM performance evaluation
         -

         Late April:  fully rollout for SCS/Lovito/SIP



Slide 11

   -

   I am not sure it is one or the other but a balance
   -

      It's supposed to be a balance
      -

   If I have a question, I am perfectly ok if a bot can answer it. But if I
   find that you are avoiding me on purpose, naturally I will not be very
   happy about it. Off-hand, it does seem like our deflection rate is too high
   at the moment
   -

      Yes, agree that choice BR/PH/VN deflection rate is a bit too high.
      Actually when we start to replace leliao(3rd party bot) with LLM shopbot
      for SCS on 2024.10, SCS CS Biz have done a manual sampling & tagging of
      Leliao and shopee bot deflected sessions  to verify the 'real deflection
      rate'( bot truly understand buyer intent and provide the right answer
      before buyer leave) of these two bots and it turns out the true
      deflection rat

whether direct shipping is a highly desired service;

   -

   ETA: 26 Mar


Taobao

Slide 5

   -

   For the SKUs/ADO/ADGMV here, are they all direct selling? Or contains
   SIP as well?
   -

      They contain both direct selling and SIP. Please see the breakdown
      below.

                        [image: image.png]

   -

   For now SIP is small, with both SKU count and SKU effi lower than direct
   selling. We are in progress of revamping CNSIP to make it a more effective
   strategic tool (simpler, faster) to help close CN/KR/JP CB assortment gaps.

Slide 11

   -

   Are these all direct selling or SIP? Are they all SLS? Or includes LFF
   too?
   -

   I thought the sellers were only supporting SG/MY? How come there are
   sales in other markets?
   -

      These are all direct selling, which includes both SLS and 3PF (~75%
      of SKUs are 3PF)
      -

      Yes, we are currently only running the program in SG/MY (with plans
      to start in VN/TH by end-Mar/early-Apr). However as these sellers already
      have existing shops + SKUs in the other regions, we are tracking their
      performance as well

Slide 13

   -

   # of SKUs for MY seem to have dropped?
   -

      Yes, the drop was due to the top GGPs’ SKUs being unlisted due to a
      seller / ops issue (~10k SKUs). We are fixing this with Yiru’s
and Derek’s
      teams, and expect to recover the SKUs by Wed (19 Mar)


Best Regards
Zhang Yu

How does the mark up of cost via Shopee platform fee work?

tina.tai@shopee.com>; Tzong Meng Ong <tzongmeng.ong@shopee.com>
*Subject:* Re: SIP Weekly Update - Week ending 14 Mar



Hi Ian,



I remember that Christin had some concerns with ID direct selling, has that
been resolved?

content.answer:
Hi Ian,

How does the 3P Financier work? Who will pay for the cost of this?


3P financier is a local third-party insurance company that provides
early/fast escrow services to ID direct sellers. It will pay ID seller once
a parcel leaves ID TWS; and receive payment from MY after the transaction
is completed in MY. It will charge a service fee in exchange for the
financial risks between 2 payment cadences.


ID sellers will pay for the cost (3P service fee); either in the form of 1)
higher platform fee charged at MY side (MY pays 3P order transaction +
service fee), or 2) lower payment to ID seller (3P pays seller order
transaction - service fee). Details need to work out with the 3P financier
once ID biz team identifies one



How does the mark up of cost via Shopee platform fee work?

For example, MY platform currently charges CNCB sellers/IDMY SIP 15% base
comm. IDMY SIP data shows that RR cost on this lane is 5% GMV. To hide the
RR cost, MY platform will charge IDMY direct sellers 15%+5%=20% base comm.

If the actual RR cost is lower than 5%, MY platform will keep the margin;
if the actual RR cost is higher than 5%, MY platform will bear the loss.

Hi Ian,

For IDMY SIP, it will be the buyers paying for it right?

Cause sellers do
not actually pay any different

*Yes, the RR cost will be marked up into final listing price for both SIP
and Direct, which is buyer paying*

How does it affect price competitiveness?
5% is not small?

For direct, we intend to charge a different comm rate from what ID sellers
are paying locally?

*Yes, ID direct sellers by default will follow the CNCB/SIP comm rate in
destination market, which is different from ID local rate*

*Christin is ok with this, and advises her team to educate ID sellers
accordingly*

Then where is the markup coming from?

content.answer:
Hi Ian,

For IDMY SIP, it will be the buyers paying for it right? Cause sellers do
not actually pay any different

Yes, the RR cost will be marked up into final listing price for both SIP
and Direct, which is buyer paying

How does it affect price competitiveness? 5% is not small?

5% is the actual IDMY RR cost incurred to SIP

Price competitiveness against SIP: same (fair game), as the same 5% will be
marked up into the final listing price

Price competitiveness against seller self mark-up model: listings with
lower actual RR than 5% will be less price competitive from over mark-up;
listings with higher actual RR than 5% will be more price competitiveness
from under mark-up; assuming seller is capable of marking up based on
actual RR cost per listing

We aligned with Christin to start with 5% first, and subsequently

   -

   Short-term: break down 5% and assess ways to lower RR cost (e.g., reduce
   delivery failure, COD failure)
   -

   Mid-term: consider lower RR mark-up for good sellers, higher RR mark-up
   for bad sellers
   -

   Long-term: return RR responsibility back to seller

For direct, we intend to charge a different comm rate from what ID sellers
are paying locally?

Yes, ID direct sellers by default will follow the CNCB/SIP comm rate in
destination market, which is different from ID local rate

Christin is ok with this, and advises her team to educate ID sellers
accordingly

Then where is the markup coming from?


The RR markup will come from charging 5pp higher base comm vs SIP

   -

   ID base comm (local seller): 7.2%
   -

   MY base comm (CNCB/SIP): 15%
   -

   MY base comm (IDMY Direct sellers): 15% + 5% = 20%



Not sure we take all these into consideration while weighing the pros/cons
of being buyer friendly..


Buyer experiences should be similar between SIP and Direct as they won't
feel the difference on how RR is marked. However price competitiveness of
individual listi

topics:

   - By-month target setting and detailed initiative plan
   - Discussion topic: FRT (First response time) comparison vs. local
   SCS/SIP and CB Top Sellers
   - Discussion topic: Chatbot LLM & NLU application in pre-sales & after
   sales scenarios
   - Appendix: By-site performance of CS team

Please let us know if you have any questions. Thanks!

Best,

Russell

195d5b8fdfbf7c5d.subject: Re: [For Approval] [PHCB] Commission Waiver for 2025 Focus Brands Program
195d5b8fdfbf7c5d.deck_filename: Shopee Mobile Mail - Fwd_ [For Approval] 2025 Focus Brand Program.pdf

content.answer:
Dear Ian,

In line with PH’s direction to accelerate mall growth this year, platform
has designed the *P0 Focus Brands Program* to drive high topline growth
through offering attractive incentives to select key mall brands.

With this, *CB has 43 brands
<https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/16-F_KSnoZgbkRZTY6_XK-oIXJO_2rKgOSTabq4FX6EY/edit?gid=0#gid=0>*
(covers ~35% of CB Mall GMV) included in this program where the mechanics
are as follows:

   - Brand commitment - achieve *aspirational GMV targets*, collectively
   growing *~51% YoY*
   - Incentive - brands meeting their monthly targets will receive a *discount
   on their base commission ranging from 0.5 to 1.5%* (depending on the
   brand), as well as paid ads credits, with higher incentives rewarded to
   sellers that exceed their targets higher

This program has already been approved by Vincent, his email approval
attached to this email. Please also see CB BI assessment below:

[image: image.png]
*ABS drop and ADO growing faster than ADG because of mix of shops in the
pool, with lower ABS brands taking higher share of total GMV
*PC2% worse off due to lower take rate as a result of the foregone revenue
from this program

Seeking your approval as part of CB's SOP for commission related
adjustments.

Thank you,
Kevin

content.answer:
Cell in grey - reseller, cell in green - LFF penetration% %> KR ‘25 Dec
   penetration target, cell in red - LFF penetration% % <KR ‘25 Dec
   penetration target



Slide 25 Hyungi Lee <hyungi.lee@shopee.com>

Platform Video Supply Number View

Regarding the march platform video supply 59% of the numbers are coming
from KOL UGCs but with markets with low KOL supplies SG TW majority is
coming from ‘sellers’ or ‘Others(User, Bot, Untracked)’ > Most of ‘others’
are TT sync of KOLs that are posting contents overall


markets are more to KOLs in terms of video supply


*For KR seller origin video supply view, currently we can only extract data
based on video supply # number with GMV 0< performance for KRCB sellers, so
cannot be aligned with the current regional table above which can deliver
wrong information in terms of video supply for KR view, currently working
with CNCB on the seller origin tables based on item matching.

[image: image.png]
[image: image.png]


Video GMV Weightage

Regional platform numbers 78% of the gmv is coming from KOL videos however
the weightage of the seller videos is becoming higher in platform, since MY
TH PH VN is aiming for the video pc2% neutral in Q2, which has risks in
terms of KOL video supply #  & efficiency due to limiting shp supported
affiliate commission costs and PRM etc.

Since KR has its video growth driver for video by KOLs, to risk hedge the
situation we also need input in terms of seller video supplies also, as
there are cut offs PRM and SHP supported commissions, that were happening
already from March.

As pdp sync, is a low hanging fruit which is a success for SG &  TW  in
terms of GMV, but this is assuming a low baseline. A low hanging fruit but
not impactful in terms of end Video GMV, as other markets PDP gmv total is
1~2% of the total video ADG.

[image: image.png]



[Appendix] KRCB Video GMV source Type

On the other hand, KR is skewed to KOL videos at 90% weightage

Hi Ian,


Please find SIP weekly update for your review. Counter Taobao (including
ERP initiative) update in attachment. Thank you.

Performance Dashboard (as of 26 Mar 2025)

A/ Topline/P&L


Highlights

   1.

   [Topline] Mar proj ADO 214k, 112% RR; ADO as %CB +0.7pp MoM; proj ADG
   $2.5M, 115%RR; ADG as %CB +0.7pp MoM
   1.

      Local SIP proj ADO 117% RR, %CB +0.5pp MoM; overachievement driven by
      1) Raya push in MY+SG; 2) new SKU ads optimization; 3) top shop push in
      TH+PH
      2.

      CNSIP proj ADO 94% RR, % CB -0.1pp MoM; missing target mainly due to
      underperformance in VN driven by 1) VN VAT tax impact; 2) VNCB
      underperformance (CB RR 80%, likely due to PRM shift from FSV0
to discount
      of high-ASP SKUs)
      2.

   [P&L] Overall proj combined PC2 healthy at Shopee level at $2.7M, $391k
   higher than target driven by topline outperformance
   1.

      Local SIP proj P+SA PC2 $12k, slightly positive driven by 1) GMV
      out-performance leading to budget surplus; 2) FX and shipping P&L
      improvement
      2.

      CNSIP proj SA PC2 $3k


B/ ADO breakdown (multiple BU/lanes)


Highlights

   1.

   All top 5 SIP lanes and WH cross-selling lanes (except local SCS)
   outperform CB and platform
   2.

   IDMY proj 114% RR; driven by successful Raya push, a detrend in related
   categories was observed from 21 Mar with -46% WoW ADO as BWT surpassed Hari
   Raya and sellers holiday mode, in line with projection
   3.

   IDPH proj 125% RR; driven by additional new SKUs set up on ads
   automation contributing to 1k ADO
   4.

   VNMY proj 132% RR; ADO %CB +0.5pp MoM despite Raya month, growth due to
   increased SKUs through Ops revival, newly migrated SKUs ads ramp up and
   non-performing SKUs mass price reduction
   5.

   IDSG proj 130%RR; ADO %CB +0.8pp driven by Raya push with related
   categories +40% MoM


C/ BWT


Highlights

   1.

Hi Ian,

Taobao

Slide 8

   -

   PC2 for a few markets are probably too healthy? Like TW/TH/VN?
   -

      Yes, we have brought this up for their attention. Current
      plan/progress as below
      -

         VN: set up additional PRM vouchers to decrease PC2; implemented in
         end-Mar; we will monitor the PC2 trend in Apr
         -

         TW: designed a new incentive package to pitch to all sellers with
         higher voucher PRM [ETA start: 7-14 Apr]
         -

         TH: Platform willing to invest for “unique” and “price
         competitive” SKUs. Next step to work out with THCB on unique/price
         competitive SKU list from Taobao pool and investment plan [ETA 11 Apr]
         -

   For SG/MY, are we seeing a reversal in the # of SKUs yet? There are just
   too few SKUs overall
   -

      Yes, referencing the WTD SKU # in Slide 8, we see an increase of 7%
      and 10% WoW for SG/MY
      -

      Since the program started, we have brought ~20k/30k new SKUs for SG/MY
      -

      For now, we have concluded SKU migration from existing 164 program
      sellers. Next step to grow SKU is to acquire new non-SHP sellers/SKUs



Slide 10

   -

   How does this compare with our expectation?
   -

      From past CN SMT acquisition experiences, acceptance rate is ~3-5%,
      low as most sellers on Taobao are not contactable
      -

      Hence we expect ~100+ sellers to be acquired from this pool
      -

      We are now pending SG/MY’s package to be finalised (comms waiver
      approval - SG approved, MY pending)



Slide 14

   -

   Are we able to split ADO from SLS and LFF? And can give an update on sea
   shipping + bulky situations in each market? SLS will not work without this
   solution right?
   -

      LFF (3PF) is ~87-89% for SG/MY, due to the top sellers for both
      markets doing a 3PF model


   -

   Sea shipping + bulky situation
   -

   PC2 for a few markets are probably too healthy? Like TW/TH/VN?
   -

      Yes, we have brought this up for their attention. Current
      plan/progress as below
      -

         VN: set up additional PRM vouchers to decrease PC2; implemented in
         end-Mar; we will monitor the PC2 trend in Apr
         -

         TW: designed a new incentive package to pitch to all sellers with
         higher voucher PRM [ETA start: 7-14 Apr]

Taobao

content.question:
Slide 10

   - How does this compare with our expectation?

- From past CN SMT acquisition experiences, acceptance rate is ~3-5%,
      low as most sellers on Taobao are not contactable
      - Hence we expect ~100+ sellers to be acquired from this pool
      - We are now pending SG/MY’s package to be finalised (comms waiver
      approval - SG approved, MY pending)

This is probably too low to really move a needle right?

Slide 14

   - Are we able to split ADO from SLS and LFF?

And can give an update on
   sea shipping + bulky situations in each market?

SLS will not work without
   this solution right?

- LFF (3PF) is ~87-89% for SG/MY, due to the top sellers for both
      markets doing a 3PF model



So where do we see the growth coming from?
SLS or LFF?

o   Sea shipping + bulky situation

§  SG/MY: current sea shipping weight and dimension limits are
comparable/better than TB’s direct shipping, no plan to further improve for
now

§  TH/PH/TW: current seashipping limit is small. SLS is in progress of
assessing bulky solution for each market

§  VN: current seashipping limit is small. However, aligned with local and
SLS not to proceed to explore bulky solution, as VN customs has a limit of
32kg and 35% import tax to be added for bulky items, which will make bulky
products price uncompetitive in VN

When will we know for TH/PH/TW?
- For markets without SLS + Bulky, do we really think they can grow much?

- >70% of Taobao program SKUs are under 15kg, still significant enough
      to run the program first while we explore a bulky solution



It is not about Taobao program SKUs right, but what is Taobao selling into
these markets?

- PC2 is probably too high as well for most markets if we want to grow
   this?

- Yes, after we start running the program in VN/TH (ETA in 1 weeks’
      time), we’ll ask markets if they’re willing to drive down PC2 for these 2
      markets



Slide 15

   - For #2 – Do we have a breakdown of the funnel?

Which markets can they
   start in first?

- So far, we’re only pitching for SG/MY since these are the only 2 mkts
      that we have started in (VN/TH to start in 1 weeks’ time)
      - The following is the breakdown of SG/MY (the # of acquired sellers
      are the same, hence we didn’t split by mkt)


   - What is the issue for #5?

- There are certain furniture products that require to be shipped in
      multiple packages, such as furniture that needs to be assembled e.g.
      outdoor tables, or furniture that is fragile and hence needs to be split
      e.g. wardrobes
      - However, our system currently doesn’t allow sellers to ship one SKU
      via multiple parcels, and hence we found a system workaround for
sellers to
      be able to sell these items



Slide 17

   - Why such low targets for MY when compared to SG?

- Sourcing wishlist and targets were set by SG/MY Local curated from top
      TB furniture sellers’ top SKUs. MY set their SKU# target and sourcing
      wishlist based on this logic:


   - whether SHP MY has the assortment - if not on SHP MY, to be sourced;
         - whether SHP MY price point is cheaper vs TB - if SHP MY more
         expensive, to be sourced


   - Hence, both local mkts reached a different target # of SKUs after
      going through the above criteria
      - We are working with MYBD to do an interim review on the sourcing
      list and criteria, to open up to more SKUs [ETA 7 Apr]



Does not feel like a very sound logic…we should just go for as many SKUs as
we can?

You are telling me Local curated every single top SKU for TB
furtniure seller and found a cheaper alternative locally?

Others

   - How are we intending to approach F&B sellers?

- We have already mapped out the universe, and start with SKU migration
      (quick win), while preparing for top seller acquisition and sourcing to
      close the gaps between TB and us - gaps are still being identified
      - F&B: crawled and matched 10 TB GGPs with 250 unique SKUs, with
      opportunity to add 802 SKUs across 6 markets
      - Beauty: crawled and matched 73 TB GGPs with 8321 unique SKUs, with
      opportunity to add 34k SKUs across 6 markets

F&B so little?
Why did we look at Beuaty?

*SIP*



When are we looking to optimize CN SIP?

Similar to how we optimize Local
SIP?
What are the steps/deadline on that?

- We are revamping CNSIP in 3 steps



Step

Initiative

Progress

Next-step and ETA

1

Improve existing CNSIP performance by replicating local SIP initiatives

   - Reduced price mark-up of marketing budget from 17% to 12% to remove
   low ROI marketing spend [ETA completion: EOW]


   - Planned SKU revival of top 5 CNSIP lanes after price reduction
   (BR/VN/SG/PH/TH) [ETA: mid-Apr (SG); end-Apr (others)
   - Optimize new/revived SKU ads investment [ETA: end-Apr]

2

Simplify CNSIP biz model to make it a more effective SKU migration tool for
existing SHP sellers

   - Conducted 2 CN seller interviews to understand the rejection reasons
   to CNSIP
   - Key takeaway: 1) pricing formula complicated and not communicated
   clearly; 2) seller education on the program can be better


   - Proposal on a simpler migration tool (easy-to-understand pricing
   formula, comms) [ETA; 15 Apr]

3

Explore ERP collaborations to migrate new SKUs to SHP

   - In progress of exploring commercial arrangement with potential
   partner, business/ops flow and product requirement


   - 1st discussion with potential partner on 2 Apr
   - Product + business plan proposal ETA 15 Apr



Do we have a new projection and P&L?
(CRR/CSAT) We do not have past data?

We should be looking at more than just
CSAT?
How about productivity/abandon rate etc?

I remember that was quite
bad?

- Data is available after CS migration to IHCS from early Mar; we will
   have MoM data from Apr
   - Additional metrics we plan to add from 5 Apr


   - Agent: productivity, abandon rate, conversion rate, average response
      time
      - Chatbot:  CSAT, conversion rate, deflection rate



Can look at what Shuning/Esten side are looking at?

Maybe we should
standardize amongst the 3 teams..



How about the performance of our Chatbot?
Will we be tracking it?

- Yes as above plan



Others

   - Can we relook TH as an export market?

I think there is more potential
   there than we think (Fashion, Snacks etc)


   - Yes we are on it, assessment outcome and growth proposal will be ready
      for your review in the upcoming Apr SIP monthly meeting on 15 Apr
      - We are assessing the unique/price competitive SKUs from THSIP pool,
      and then replicate optimization initiatives to drive topline uplift
      - Initiatives we plan to replicate/consider to THSIP


   - (more SKUs) SKU migration improvement/revival [ETA mid-Apr]
         - (cheaper price) BPSF reduction: alignment [ETA end-Apr]
         - (cheaper price) SLS ASF reduction via vendor renegotiation [ETA
         end-Apr]
         - (better marketing) New/revived/trendy push


   - When are we going to look at KR and JP SIP?

Targeting resellers


   - KR managed seller: Aligned with KR to use mass migration tool. ETA
      April
      - KR unmanage seller/reseller: Projected ADO impact for SIP model is
      ~644 ADO in total in 8 markets. Requires 6-8 weeks development
efforts and
      3 additional HC for seller management, shop management and
listing control.
      Raised P2 ticket due to limited business impact. Similar dev efforts for
      JPSIP with a smaller business impact.

We need dev efforts for this?
Not just something we can turn on?

For step 1, we will track the impact from the 3 initiatives and update by
end-Apr. Our estimation is ~5-10% (+1K to 2K) ADO uplift.

For step 2 and 3, the impact will depend on the use case (whether we want
to focus on certain counter taobao categories and markets, or we want to
expand aggressively across CB categories in general). We will bring
preliminary thinking around both scenarios and projections to 15 Apr SIP
monthly meeting.
