Slide 14

·  Are we able to split ADO from SLS and LFF?

And can give an update on sea
shipping + bulky situations in each market?

SLS will not work without this
solution right?

o   LFF (3PF) is ~87-89% for SG/MY, due to the top sellers for both markets
doing a 3PF model

So where do we see the growth coming from?
SLS or LFF?

The growth is coming from LFF as our top sellers are driving the growth and
their shops are 3PF model

If our growth is determined by LFF, the potential will be very small so I
am not sure what we really mean here..

I am concerned we are focused on wrong things

For markets without SLS + Bulky, do we really think they can grow much?

o   >70% of Taobao program SKUs are under 15kg, still significant enough to
run the program first while we explore a bulky solution



It is not about Taobao program SKUs right, but what is Taobao selling into
these markets?

Yes agree. We believe SLS+bulky channel is important to have, because 1)
Taobao has a meaningful segment of bulky products (pending BI/MI support to
verify %, current estimation is bulky contributes to ~20% ADG market share
by CN furniture team); 2) our markets need bulky SKUs - so we are pushing
the bulky solution together with CN SLS commercial team as the plan above.



Meanwhile while waiting for a bulky solution in place, we think it is also
important to push the non-bulky ones as no-regrets to close assortment gap
with Taobao.



We need bulky, we need to push SLS..i am concerned we are focusing on wrong
things





Slide 17

·  Why such low targets for MY when compared to SG?

o   Sourcing wishlist and targets were set by SG/MY Local curated from top
TB furniture sellers’ top SKUs. MY set their SKU# target and sourcing
wishlist based on this logic:

§  whether SHP MY has the assortment - if not on SHP MY, to be sourced;

§  whether SHP MY price point is cheaper vs TB - if SHP MY more expensive,
to be sourced

o   Hence, both local mkts reached a different target # of SKUs after going
through the above criteria

o   We are working with MYBD to do an interim review on the sourcing list
and criteria, to open up to more SKUs [ETA 7 Apr]

Does not feel like a very sound logic…we should just go for as many SKUs as
we can?

You are telling me Local curated every single top SKU for TB
furtniure seller and found a cheaper alternative locally?

Understood. MY set initial target based on a much smaller target pool of
top TB SKUs. Aligned with MYBD to expand the target SKU pool and the
sourcing wishlist / target by next week, will update again.

On the program level, the current approach (ask top sellers to source) will
have a SKU # limit. This is because the local market needs to provide
incentives to these sellers to do the sourcing for us, so the budget
availability will limit how many SKUs we can support. It makes sense for
the local market to focus budget on the most-wanted SKUs.

Once we have the additional approach with ERP 聚水潭, then SIP team is able to
migrate SKUs without local market support - then we can go for as many SKUs
as possible.

Again I am concerned we are focusing on wrong things..i do not quite
understand how we can allow such a small SKU target





Others

·  How are we intending to approach F&B sellers?

o   We have already mapped out the universe, and start with SKU migration
(quick win), while preparing for top seller acquisition and sourcing to
close the gaps between TB and us - gaps are still being identified

o   F&B: crawled and matched 10 TB GGPs with 250 unique SKUs, with
opportunity to add 802 SKUs across 6 markets

o   Beauty: crawled and matched 73 TB GGPs with 8321 unique SKUs, with
opportunity to add 34k SKUs across 6 markets

F&B so little?

These are sellers identified by CN F&B team that are true TB sellers. We
have a limited CNCB seller pool on F&B to start with, due to our CB
logistics policy limits on F&B (officially no F&B products are allowed; in
practice some can pass the customs as the vol is not high). Taobao global
is able to support more F&B sellers because it uses 3P vendors that
leverage grey channel (Taobao official shipping service also does not allow
F&B products)

Concurrently, we’re finding alternative sources to get more TB
sellers/SKUs: 1) acquire top sellers that are not on SHP; 2) crawl 60k top
TB SKUs, and check how many can be matched to SHP; 3) source SKUs not on
Shopee.

Why are we not allowed to sell F&B via CB logistics?
Why did we look at Beauty?

Beauty was sizable on TB Global, e.g., in MY, SHP’s multiple is 0.7x of TB;
thus we wanted to do a sanity check after F&B to check if we did miss
anything.

So what did we realise here?

*SIP*



When are we looking to optimize CN SIP?

Similar to how we optimize Local
SIP?
What are the steps/deadline on that?

·  We are revamping CNSIP in 3 steps



Step

Initiative

Progress

Next-step and ETA

1

Improve existing CNSIP performance by replicating local SIP initiatives

Reduced price mark-up of marketing budget from 17% to 12% to remove low ROI
marketing spend [ETA completion: EOW]

Planned SKU revival of top 5 CNSIP lanes after price reduction
(BR/VN/SG/PH/TH) [ETA: mid-Apr (SG); end-Apr (others)

Optimize new/revived SKU ads investment [ETA: end-Apr]

2

Simplify CNSIP biz model to make it a more effective SKU migration tool for
existing SHP sellers

Conducted 2 CN seller interviews to understand the rejection reasons to
CNSIP

Key takeaway: 1) pricing formula complicated and not communicated clearly;
2) seller education on the program can be better

Proposal on a simpler migration tool (easy-to-understand pricing formula,
comms) [ETA; 15 Apr]

3

Explore ERP collaborations to migrate new SKUs to SHP

In progress of exploring commercial arrangement with potential partner,
business/ops flow and product requirement

1st discussion with potential partner on 2 Apr

Product + business plan proposal ETA 15 Apr



Do we have a new projection and P&L?

For step 1, we will track the impact from the 3 initiatives and update by
end-Apr. Our estimation is ~5-10% (+1K to 2K) ADO uplift.

For step 2 and 3, the impact will depend on the use case (whether we want
to focus on certain counter taobao categories and markets, or we want to
expand aggressively across CB categories in general). We will bring
preliminary thinking around both scenarios and projections to 15 Apr SIP
monthly meeting.



(CRR/CSAT) We do not have past data?

We should be looking at more than just
CSAT?
How about productivity/abandon rate etc?

I remember that was quite
bad?

·  Data is available after CS migration to IHCS from early Mar; we will
have MoM data from Apr

·  Additional metrics we plan to add from 5 Apr

o   Agent: productivity, abandon rate, conversion rate, average response
time

o   Chatbot:  CSAT, conversion rate, deflection rate



Can look at what Shuning/Esten side are looking at?

Maybe we should
standardize amongst the 3 teams..

Ok will do. We will compare with SCS/SBS metrics. On a high level, we can
use the same set of metrics, but each team has different weightage / target
to tailor to biz needs.



·  When are we going to look at KR and JP SIP?

Targeting resellers

o   KR managed seller: Aligned with KR to use mass migration tool. ETA April

o   KR unmanage seller/reseller: Projected ADO impact for SIP model is ~644
ADO in total in 8 markets. Requires 6-8 weeks development efforts and 3
additional HC for seller management, shop management and listing control.
Raised P2 ticket due to limited business impact. Similar dev efforts for
JPSIP with a smaller business impact.

We need dev efforts for this?
Not just something we can turn on?

content.question: Why are we not allowed to sell F&B via CB logistics?

CN logistics vendor set skus such as meat, floss, egg yolk, and dairy
products prohibited. Other F&B skus are in sensitive category, which needs
to control the order volume, as such to control the quantity of items per
linehual shipment to avoid inspection.

Deeper looking at the sub categories under f&b, snack and gift & hampers
are safe to scale up, fresh & frozen, beverages, cooking essentials and
dairy & eggs are under tight control

We need to work with SLS ops team to explore better logistics
vendors/services to open more skus/categories, will update back again next
week.

For the 0.2 multiple vs TB, can we look at what sub-cats are missing?

I
find it hard to believe that it is meat, floss, egg yolk type?

It should
fall under mostly snacks type?
Which should be able to be shipped?

So do
not quite understand why you say we cannot ship this. Or that our sellers
do not carry it currently?
Why did we look at Beauty?

Beauty was sizable on TB Global, e.g., in MY, SHP’s multiple is 0.7x of TB;
thus we wanted to do a sanity check after F&B to check if we did miss
anything.

So what did we realise here?

SN: We have some initial understanding.

a ) there are 73 confirmed TB sellers on SHP, with ~8.3k unique SKUs giving
us a migration opportunity of ~33.6 K.

So what are we doing here?
Will they be part of TB program or?

*From:* Shuning Wang <shuning.wang@shopee.com>
*Sent:* Friday, 4 April 2025 12:59 pm
*To:* Ian Ho <hoi@sea.com>; James Li [SZ] <james.li@shopee.com>; Saovanee
Somchit <saovanee.somchit@shopee.com>
*Cc:* Cuiwei Sun <cuiwei.sun@shopee.com>; Jianghong Liu <
jianghong.liu@shopee.com>; Keljin Chua <keljin.chua@shopee.com>; Jason Yu <
jason.yu@shopee.com>; Yu Zhang <yu.zhang@shopee.com>; Ailin Xie <
ailin.xie@shopee.com>; Wei Qiang <wei.qiang@shopee.com>; Momo Chen <
momo.chen@shopee.com>; Huang Wan-Jung <wanjung.huangwj@shopee.com>; Chiong
Zhi Ann <zhiann.chiong@shopee.com>; Steven Liu <steven.liusk@shopee.com>;
Wei Loong Yong <weiloong.yong@shopee.com>; Nicholas Lim <
nicholas.lim@shopee.com>; Keith Huang <keith.huang@shopee.com>; Joe Li <
joe.liyc@shopee.com>; Ren Jiahui <jiahui.ren@shopee.com>; Eveline Seah <
eveline.seahyl@shopee.com>; Carol Yan <carol.yan@shopee.com>; Hongjun Li <
hongjun.li@shopee.com>
*Subject:* Re: SIP Weekly Update - Week ending 28 Mar



Hi Ian



Let me address the questions here

So where do we see the growth coming from?
SLS or LFF?

The growth is coming from LFF as our top sellers are driving the growth and
their shops are 3PF model

If our growth is determined by LFF, the potential will be very small so I
am not sure what we really mean here..

I am concerned we are focused on wrong things

SN: To push SLS as P0 instead of LFF is aligned. Talked with SLS ops lead James
Li [SZ] <james.li@shopee.com>, we speed up TH setup in ~1 month, followed
by PH and TW in May to June. cc Jianghong Liu <jianghong.liu@shopee.com>

To clarify for Cuiwei/Nicholas: what updated about focus on LFF is bcz we
haven’t got the sls setup yet, the growth mainly comes from LFF now. We
actually push sls as direction.



It is not about Taobao program SKUs right, but what is Taobao selling into
these markets?

Yes agree. We believe SLS+bulky channel is important to have, because 1)
Taobao has a meaningful segment of bulky products (pending BI/MI support to
verify %, current estimation is bulky contributes to ~20% ADG market share
by CN furniture team); 2) our markets need bulky SKUs - so we are pushing
the bulky solution together with CN SLS commercial team as the plan above.



Meanwhile while waiting for a bulky solution in place, we think it is also
important to push the non-bulky ones as no-regrets to close assortment gap
with Taobao.



We need bulky, we need to push SLS..i am concerned we are focusing on wrong
things

SN: Yep, noted. SLS is the key, working with James Li [SZ]
<james.li@shopee.com> on ops setup, Hongjun Li <hongjun.li@shopee.com> for
system setup.



Does not feel like a very sound logic…we should just go for as many SKUs as
we can?

You are telling me Local curated every single top SKU for TB
furtniure seller and found a cheaper alternative locally?

Understood. MY set initial target based on a much smaller target pool of
top TB SKUs. Aligned with MYBD to expand the target SKU pool and the
sourcing wishlist / target by next week, will update again.

On the program level, the current approach (ask top sellers to source) will
have a SKU # limit. This is because the local market needs to provide
incentives to these sellers to do the sourcing for us, so the budget
availability will limit how many SKUs we can support. It makes sense for
the local market to focus budget on the most-wanted SKUs.

Once we have the additional approach with ERP 聚水潭, then SIP team is able to
migrate SKUs without local market support - then we can go for as many SKUs
as possible.

Again I am concerned we are focusing on wrong things..i do not quite
understand how we can allow such a small SKU target

SN: Add Saovanee Somchit <saovanee.somchit@shopee.com> for alignments

   - We push the sku # to an equivalent level as SG which target at the
   entire sku pool we have. For the marketing packages, it is step 2, we fit
   the assortment gap first. Can update the new plan in coming weekly update.
   - We have migrated 55K skus to MY in March, we work on the sku
   efficiency on them with MY teams.

F&B so little?

These are sellers identified by CN F&B team that are true TB sellers. We
have a limited CNCB seller pool on F&B to start with, due to our CB
logistics policy limits on F&B (officially no F&B products are allowed; in
practice some can pass the customs as the vol is not high). Taobao global
is able to support more F&B sellers because it uses 3P vendors that
leverage grey channel (Taobao official shipping service also does not allow
F&B products)

Concurrently, we’re finding alternative sources to get more TB
sellers/SKUs: 1) acquire top sellers that are not on SHP; 2) crawl 60k top
TB SKUs, and check how many can be matched to SHP; 3) source SKUs not on
Shopee.

Why are we not allowed to sell F&B via CB logistics?

CN logistics vendor set skus such as meat, floss, egg yolk, and dairy
products prohibited. Other F&B skus are in sensitive category, which needs
to control the order volume, as such to control the quantity of items per
linehual shipment to avoid inspection.

Deeper looking at the sub categories under f&b, snack and gift & hampers
are safe to scale up, fresh & frozen, beverages, cooking essentials and
dairy & eggs are under tight control

We need to work with SLS ops team to explore better logistics
vendors/services to open more skus/categories, will update back again next
week.

Why did we look at Beauty?

Beauty was sizable on TB Global, e.g., in MY, SHP’s multiple is 0.7x of TB;
thus we wanted to do a sanity check after F&B to check if we did miss
anything.

So what did we realise here?

Hi Ian,

Here are the next steps we discussed in today's meeting. Feel free to let
us know if you have anything to add.

Next Steps for Biz Owner

   - Business owners (Shunning and Li Ning) are responsible for the
   business and its CS, including agent performance. For local SCS/SBS, the
   responsibility lies with the local SCS/SBS business owner. The business
   owner should take ownership of the metrics and adjust reporting lines as
   soon as possible
   - Business owners to focus on driving business, not just cost. In terms
   of cost, to follow this order of priority:


   1.

      Improve productivity, provide plan in the next update (within 1-2
      weeks)
      2.

      Reduce contact rate via Chatbot; to work with Reg CS to ensure
      rollout doesn’t impact performance significant; proceed only when ready
      3.

      Move CB SCS and SIP agents to PH, to update on timeline in next
      update


Next Steps for Reg CS


Reg CS to analyze conversion rate/metrics

   1.

      Contact rate differences between us and the benchmark; determine the
      target equilibrium rate
      2.

      Shopchat bot seller adoption rates by ADO and GMV (in addition to
      shop numbers)
      3.

      Reg CS to assess GMV uplift from conversations, i.e. by comparing ABS
      values of conversations vs. non-conversations
      4.

      To provide update by next update in (early May)


   -

   Reg CS to learn from Ian’s team and apply best practices across markets,
   handling cross-cutting topics (e.g., driving conversions, pre-sales split,
   org. model)
   -

   Reg CS to support business owners with systems, tools, and performance
   tracking



Best Regards
Steven Shi

content.answer:
Capacity planning challenges – The rollout of the new chatbot (expected
   to significantly improve deflection rates) was delayed. This rollout is now
   scheduled for April 21 and is expected to reduce AR and wait time
   significantly.

Agent concurrency adjustment – In an effort to improve CSAT, maximum
   agent concurrency was reduced from 5 to 3 last week. This has since been
   reverted back to 5, as it impacted capacity.

Staffing shortages – A recent loss of 6 agents due to resignations
   created a headcount gap, further straining the team. MY will increase HC
   numbers in the upcoming weeks.

Despite these challenges, performance has improved since last week


   -

   Abandonment rates dropped from 5.2% to 1.5%


   -

   Average wait times improved from 65 minutes 45 minutes, expected to
   reach under 3 minutes by week of 5th May



Slide 9 <Individual Agent KPIs>

   -

   These agents are expected to perform outbound tasks?
   -

   Yes, agents are expected to conduct outbound follow-ups to address cases
   that come in during off-hours. For MP CS, these are typically handled by
   dedicated “email” agents or mitigated through 24/7 chat support. However,
   since Shop CS doesn't have a similar agent type and a large portion of
   cases require outbound handling, it’s important for us to track this metric
   for Shop CS agents.



Others

   -

   I wonder if it make sense to reshuffle HC between SG and MY businesses.
   Some teams feel like no need  people, some need more..

There are several key hurdles to overcome before combining operations for
SG and MY (as below). For now, we relook into WFM to cover the immediate
gaps first. Hurdles include:


   -

   ROI: Cross-market agent sharing may not be efficient, as frequent
   switching would likely require substantial retraining, increased costs and
   offsetting gains from improved utilisation.

   -

   User Experience: There's a risk of agents applying the wrong market's
   SOPs, which could lead to inconsistent or incorrect customer support.

   -

   Ownership/Structural Issues: The team overseeing the shared agents may
   prioritize their own market’s performance, potentially to the detriment of
   the other.

Nevertheless, as we explore the possibility of having a dedicated group of
pre-sales agents, we will consider combining operations and optimizing b

content.answer:
Case follow-ups
      -

         An inbound contact is received from the user during the day which
         is responded to by the agent within the same day
         -

         However, the user issue is still not resolved and he/she follows
         up on the same case a few days later
         -

         Agent responds to this query with an outbound contact to address
         user’s follow-up concern/clarifications

*Enquiry during non-working hours*
      -

         An inbound contact is received from the user during late-night
         hours when no agents are available/working. The inquiry is
then followed
         up by an agent the next day as an outbound task.

content.answer:
Metrics and Associated Considerations

Shopchat agents’ metrics are similar to that used for MP agents, with a few
key differences:


   -

   For MP agents, we measure handled productivity and solved productivity.
   However, for Shopchat agents, there is no "solved productivity" concept.
   Since outbound activity is considered part of their BAU (business-as-usual)
   tasks, handled productivity is further split into inbound and outbound
   productivity.
   -

   MP agents do not track a Pre-sale Conversion Rate, whereas Shopchat
   agents do. The weightage for this metric can be adjusted based on the
   agent’s role.

Implementation

Applicable for CB SCS, SIP, Local SCS/SBS:

Milestone

Phase 1:  Manual Bucketization and Scoring

Done via Google Sheets, calculated on monthly basis

Phase 2: System-Based Bucketization and Scoring

In-house system will automate process, allowing agents to view daily
performance and grading through online dashboard (akin to MP CS)

Communication to Local Teams

Introduction & briefing on shopchat bucketization on 13 Mar

Feedback gathering from biz. owners

Share manual bucketization working file and dashboard to business owners by
30 April

Finalise Product Requirement Documents (PRD) by 2 May

Training for Roll out

Conduct manual bucketization training by 9 May

Tentatively scheduled for Q3, with final timelines to be confirmed by May
after assessing development efforts.

Target setting

Target setting by biz owners by 16 May

Incentive Plan

Propose incentive plan by mid-May

BPO Communication

Communicate to BPO agents May - Jun 2025

Actual Roll out

June/July 2025


Best Regards
Steven Shi

Best Regards

On Mon, Apr 7, 2025 at 1:52 PM Jianghong Liu <jianghong.liu@shopee.com>
wrote:

Thanks Ian and Jianghong, pls see the response below in blue. Will
elaborate more in the meeting.

Slide 7 - 8

   -

   Has this approach been aligned with all parties?

Yes, have aligned with all parties that a) we first focus on resolving key
internal disagreement of the 2 sellers (舰长/超辉煌) with negative feedback and
acknowledgement of benefits from both sides; b) for the rest of accounts,
proceed with accounts with aligned next steps


Regarding 舰长/超辉煌, working closely with CN SMT on addressing seller
feedback, and will re-confirm seller willingness during seller visit W3 of
April.



Slide 15

   -

   Semi-consignment here equals to our FBS right?

Temu semi-consignment 半托管 does not equal FBS. Under Temu semi, Temu is
responsible for product selection, pricing, marketing & ads; sellers are
responsible for warehousing and fulfillment. Shopee FBS on the other hand
is a warehousing and fulfillment service.

   -

   Does it make sense/would we be able to get these sellers into our Local
   FBS as well?

Many of these sellers have their own warehouse in Manila already, so kind
of difficult to onboard FBS. However, for those using 3PFs we are pitching
FBS, and the rest we are pitching VisMin (Regional) FBS.



Slide 19: Think ABS $11.7 is from local team assumption. May I know how we
come to this number?


This is following CNCB new expert intel. We believe their ABS will be
higher than SHP (~$7.0) given the checkout mini spend (P280). Also they are
pushing in-app discount vouchers with P1000 and above mini spend to drive
the ABS


Slide 23

   -

   Should we be concerned that ABS is going down?


The main reason for ABS going down is that CB SCS enjoyed more FS0 during
March. If we compare Apr mtd with March, we can see an obvious increase in
ABS since the platform reduced low FS0 for Apr.

Besides, if compared to Feb, the hot selling product also changed to summer
items which are generally cheaper.

      ramping up evergreen assortment to recover CB%

complete price

   IDMY 87% RR +0.8 MoM; mainly driven by (1) KUL LH LT worse than expected

Hi Ian,

CS

Can we not use A market P market anymore? Just use Import/Export? I always
have a hard time remembering which is which

   -

   Noted, will use Import/Export from next weekly report


What does full month here mean? Which month are we referring to? Is there a
way to also look at last month numbers?

   -

   March full month data is used in this table. Will start Apr MTD data
   report from this week


Our chatbot CSAT is really really bad, what is our plan here?

   -

   Lower Chatbot CSAT is low as SIP is using the old NLU model
   -

   Plan to
   -

      Rollout NLU+LLM model; PH, VN, and BR as import markets are in A/B
      testing. Other import markets to start A/B testing from 23 Apr. Full
      rollout by the end of May if results are good
      -

      Set a chatbot CSAT target [ETA 15 Apr]


Agent CSAT for SG is so bad? How come?

   -

   SG Apr MTD agent CSAT has been improved to 73% and expect to hit 80%
   from 14 Apr
   -

   Low CSAT in March was due to poor-performing agents; 11 agents were
   removed from 10 Mar
   -

   Action taken to improve CSAT
   -

      Implemented weekly agent performance monitoring (3 warning letters =
      removal)
      -

      SOPs were updated in past 2 weeks with a CSAT improvement (79% CSAT
      on Apr 8)


Agent productivity in PH is so bad? How come? And yet their average
response rate is so high? How come?

   -

   PH productivity is low at 4.9 due to underutilization. We reviewed the
   HC planning with the PH team and adjusted it on the 1st week of Apr. Apr
   MTD productivity improved to 14.
   -

   Longer average response time is under investigation [ETA 15 Apr]


There is abandon rate and response time issue? In ID export? But their
productivity seems ok

   -

   IDSG/ID/MY/IDPH  abandon rate & average response time is relatively
   higher than other SIP lanes due to higher productivity and high chat

content.question: Our chatbot CSAT is really really bad, what is our plan here?

- Lower Chatbot CSAT is low as SIP is using the old NLU model
   - Plan to


   - Rollout NLU+LLM model; PH, VN, and BR as import markets are in A/B
      testing. Other import markets to start A/B testing from 23 Apr. Full
      rollout by the end of May if results are good
      - Set a chatbot CSAT target [ETA 15 Apr]



I thought we will be using LLM model for all import markets?

Slide 14

   - For the 16 new sellers, are they in all markets or?

- The 16 sellers are in most markets as some of them are newly onboarded
      in Shopee, pushing them to onboard to all markets.


   -  How do we convince them to set up shop in as many countries as
   possible?

- We will pitch them to set up shops to all markets with the incentive
      package aligned with each market i.e. if they join 3 mkts, they’ll get
      packages for all 3 mkts


   - Acquisition speed seems very slow if we only acquired 16 over 1 quarter


   - The previous acquisition was without a package. Our acquisition
      package was finalised last week, and we’ll ramp up from this month onwards



Any targets that we are setting here?

content.question:
Thanks.



Slide 4

   - I remember that we already budgeted for removal of PID boosting in Apr
   budget?

Not sure why we keep putting it back on S&R issue
   - We must hit Apr targets



Slide 5

   - That was not what you told me last time. We cannot keep changing the
   narrative to suit what we want to say, I do remember what was told to me



Slide 6

   - Why would there be such a high cost to BS if we can return to seller?

Slide 8

   - Not sure how we arrived at these numbers
      - We always knew PID boosting was going to go away
      - Without the emphasis on cheapest, we should be able to price
      higher, earn more and put back into ads
   - I thought our aim was to increase our penetration % of entire CB. But
   the way we are planning for the business, our penetration is going down
   instead



Slide 9

   - Again, we keep putting it on the S&R issues. What are our initiatives
   to increase ADO?
- Paid ads optimization?
- CS optimization?
- LS/Video opitimization?

- I keep seeing that we want to keep reducing targets, we need to grow..



Slide 11

   - Do the numbers here refer to models in SPU or?

Slide 13

   - This seems different from the number that was reported in the last
   meeting?
- Is Mohit aware of this?

Slide 17

   - What does ADO penetration vs CB mean here?

That our penetration went
   down vs CB sellers?
- Video is really bad?
Why do we think that it is on track?

Others

   - How is our not in CSPU doing?

I was asking if we were going to return
   the stock under “others” did not hear back
   - I thought there was some category study that we were doing?

When is a
   good time to discuss that?

Slide 25

   - Why did SG&A go down by so much MOM?
- Can we meet topline budget for Apr as well?

Slide 28

   - Can I understand the ADO breakdown of Shein better based on the L1
   categories?
- How big is kids fashion for them?
- If we look at our platform?

What is kids fashion contribution to
   fashion?

Slide 29

   - We should go for trendy style, but price points should be more similar
   to TT shop?
- How does the price point compare with our Women Clothes right now?

Slide 30

   - We do not want to do for Babies?

Slide 31

   - So long to get started?

Slide 33

   - How is our push into bags and shoes coming along?

Slide 34

   - What is the ADO expectation for this?
- Is Shopee BR going to help push this too?

Or it is mostly from Free
   Fire?

- I remember I had some comments on a Freefire email but did not hear
   back



Slide 41

   - We will miss June CPI targets?

Slide 42

   - Have we changed to 1 shift for Langhua yet?

- What is our breakdown between full timers and part timers now for WH
   labour?

Slide 50

   - Are we able to shift to the new chatbot sooner?

If not, is there a way
   to reduce deflection rate for existing chat bot?

it is quite bad
   - Productivity is quite bad?
- Average waiting time in queue is still wait too long at 20 mins?
- We have 6 agents in PH already?
- Do we have abandon rate?
- How about Lovito?
How is it being managed?

And do we have a view on
   metrics?

Slide 53

   - What is our operating hours right now for agents?
- How does it compare to what we want?

   - Does 1 agent only handle one market?

Or they do multiple markets as
   well?

Slide 56

   - Why is there a link to claim the voucher?

How will a buyer claim the
   via  a link in the app?
- How about dealing with post sales?

Especially for buyers who gave us
   poor rating?

Slide 58

   - We will only use LLM moving forward right?

Hi Ian,

Performance Dashboard (as of 15 Apr 2025)

A/ Topline/P&L

Note: CNSIP MTD P&L is preliminary due to ongoing BI data fix

Highlights

   1.

   [Topline] Apr proj ADO 187k, 104% RR; ADO as %CB -0.5pp MoM; proj ADG
   $2.1M, 110%RR; ADG as %CB -0.5pp MoM
   -

      Local SIP proj ADO 102% RR, %CB -0.6pp MoM; %CB MoM decline expected
      due to 1) Raya holiday impact, 2) Songkran holiday 3) Raya assortment
      detrend, ADO performance on track
      -

      CNSIP proj ADO 104% RR, %CB +0.1ppp MoM; MoM increase from (1) Price
      reduction (2) Increased Ads investment
      2.

   [P&L] Apr proj combined PC2 at Shopee level at $1.4M, $709k
   under-performance due to local SIP fraud case leading to historical high
   level of COD fail costs (update in separate email)


B/ ADO breakdown (multiple BU/lanes)

Highlights

   1.

   All top 5 SIP lanes and WH cross-selling lanes on par with projection
   (except for IDMY)
   2.

   IDMY proj 94% RR; Low RR driven by ADO loss from frozen high COD failure
   shops; ID seller Raya holiday recovery on track, in process of ramping up
   new trendy assortment to support growth
   3.

   MYSG proj 106% RR; with SG PRM support on BAU product promo with 10% off
   for unique SKUs push
   4.

   IDSG proj 111% RR; driven by strong top ID sellers recovery from holiday
   mode and growth in S&O



D/ CS

Highlights

   1.

   Low CSAT RR in SG: SG improves L7D CSAT to 82%; MTD performance will
   meet target by end Apr.
   2.

   MY productivity low at 5.1: primarily due to underutilization, in
   progress of reviewing HC planning. [ETA: next week]
   3.

   SIP low Chatbot CSAT is due to non-LLM chatbot; Next step is to pending
   detailed analysis results for further tuning the NLU+LLM model to achieve
   targeted DR and CSAT


E/ System/product


Highlights

   1.

   #1 Rolled out to TH on 17/Apr. Pending roll-out to TW ETA 23/Apr
   2

content.answer:
Hi Ian,

COD is still closed for ID export shops right?

   -

   Yes since Sat.
   -

   Meanwhile we are taking 3 actions as below target EOW, then we will
   propose when/how we would like to resume COD
   -

      Continue to identify fraud sellers, fraud orders, recognize loss, and
      conduct recovery (freeze wallet and claw back)
      -

      Build end-to-end SOP in fraud detection, escrow, seller comms
      -

      Assessing when/how to resume COD - which sellers, when, what needs to
      be in place to safeguard



Do we need to close for other export markets as well?

   -

   Based on RTS/RR data check, no obvious fraud behavior, but will further
   check at order  level data to make sure
   -

   Reg SIP is monitoring ops/p&L metrics daily, and reg biz security team
   is running fraud scans daily for all SIP lanes too
   -

   Next-step to review SIP business model with regional ops team and
   business security team, deep dive potential fraud risks [PIC: Reg SIP; ETA
   25 Apr]



We have CS agents located in SG? What does that mean?

   -

   This is a typo - CS agents located in MY serving SG buyers. Will correct
   from next report
   -

   They are separated from MY CS agents as currently managed by different
   teams



Average response time for all markets seem to have gotten worse MOM?

   -

   Low ART because there was a misalignment on what the target was
   previously. We have now aligned the target of 40 sec with all CS teams
   -

   To support local CS teams to meet this target, we work with them to
   improve the pre-set message template and introduce the new SOP requiring
   agents to send an official reply as soon as they receive a buyer message
   -

   The SOP is expected to launch on by end-Apr, expect to meet target from
   early May


For Brunei, do we know if they are PC2 positive?

   -

   Yes they are positive, and higher than MY platform, like

*Subject:* Re: SIP Weekly Update - Week ending 18 Apr



Hi Ian,



COD is still closed for ID export shops right?

- Yes since Sat.
   - Meanwhile we are taking 3 actions as below target EOW, then we will
   propose when/how we would like to resume COD


   - Continue to identify fraud sellers, fraud orders, recognize loss, and
      conduct recovery (freeze wallet and claw back)
      - Build end-to-end SOP in fraud detection, escrow, seller comms
      - Assessing when/how to resume COD - which sellers, when, what needs
      to be in place to safeguard



Do we need to close for other export markets as well?

- Based on RTS/RR data check, no obvious fraud behavior, but will
   further check at order  level data to make sure
   - Reg SIP is monitoring ops/p&L metrics daily, and reg biz security team
   is running fraud scans daily for all SIP lanes too
   - Next-step to review SIP business model with regional ops team and
   business security team, deep dive potential fraud risks [PIC: Reg SIP; ETA
   25 Apr]



We have CS agents located in SG?
What does that mean?

- This is a typo - CS agents located in MY serving SG buyers. Will
   correct from next report
   - They are separated from MY CS agents as currently managed by different
   teams



Average response time for all markets seem to have gotten worse MOM?

- Low ART because there was a misalignment on what the target was
   previously. We have now aligned the target of 40 sec with all CS teams
   - To support local CS teams to meet this target, we work with them to
   improve the pre-set message template and introduce the new SOP requiring
   agents to send an official reply as soon as they receive a buyer message
   - The SOP is expected to launch on by end-Apr, expect to meet target
   from early May



For Brunei, do we know if they are PC2 positive?

Slide 4

   - For Buyer experience, shall we expand to have more OKRs around Shop
   CS?

Just looking at CSAT is not sufficient anymore

   now to increase assortment?

Slide 11

And how can we help to grow TB fashion in

Would it be via SCS or Lovito?

Slide 15

   - What does Other good listings mean here?

   - Did we check if TB snacks was something that we could do?

Slide 20 -27

Slide 29

- When will we be starting B4?

Slide 31

- How are we ensuring that all our listings come with a video?

Slide 35 – 38

   - Who is coordinating the definitions right now?

Is it Mohit or Christin
   side?

Slide 40 – 43

   - How will this impact us?

No reason for PH < MY?

And obvious
   cost savings for LFF?

   - How is the performance of PH agents compared to VN agents?

      right now?

- And I think the wording is very bad right now. It feels like
      bribery. It should be more along the lines of how we can do better. And

What else do we need to do here?

   - We seem to be missing targets this month by quite a bit?

What is the

   - I think we cannot keep putting it on traffic issue. SCS has been able

   - Can grow faster?

   - Performance is very poor?
What markets are we selling to right now?
- Are we pricing ourselves too high?
- What are we doing about this?

It is hard to justify future

*Cc:* 'Ning Li' <ning.li@shopee.com>; 'Larry Lin' <larry.lin@shopee.com>;
'Chengchen Huo' <chengchen.huo@shopee.com>; 'Florence Zhai' <
florence.zhai@shopee.com>; 'Roger Yao' <roger.yao@shopee.com>; 'Jessie
Zhang' <mengqi.zhang@shopee.com>; 'Jiang Hans' <hans.jiang@shopee.com>;
'Mengxiao Yu' <william.yu@shopee.com>; 'Ran Yang' <russell.yang@shopee.com>;
'Ang Li' <ang.lee@shopee.com>; 'alfred su' <alfred.su@shopee.com>; 'Amina
Yin' <amina.yin@shopee.com>; 'Fan Fan' <jacob.fan01@shopee.com>; 'Roy Lv' <
roy.lv@shopee.com>; 'Lu Lin' <lu.lin@shopee.com>; 'Charissa Li' <
charissa.li@shopee.com>; 'Siqin Liu' <siqin.liu@lovito.com>; 'Weijia Song' <
vega.song@shopee.com>; 'Shuning Wang' <shuning.wang@shopee.com>; 'Rainy
Kong' <rainy.kong@shopee.com>
*Subject:* RE: [Pre-read] SCS & Lovito bi-weekly update_2025.04.22



Thanks



Slide 4

   - For Buyer experience, shall we expand to have more OKRs around Shop
   CS?

Just looking at CSAT is not sufficient anymore



Slide 6

   - Think we will adopt this version for our internal tracking



Slide 9

   - Feels like a lot off things to do, but not very clear what are the key
   things that each cluster should focus on and do right now
   - On #5 – Which is the competitor platform that we are looking at right
   now to increase assortment?

Slide 11

   - What does PDP optimization mean exactly?
- Did we check on TB Fashion?

And how can we help to grow TB fashion in
   local markets?
Would it be via SCS or Lovito?

- Feels like our growth will come from more optimization as compared to
   new SKUs?
Does not feel right for Fashion?

Slide 12

   - I do not disagree with this, but struggle to link with the impact on
   Slide 11, which line does it relate to?

Slide 15

   - What does Other good listings mean here?

Slide 19

   - Did we check if TB snacks was something that we could do?

Slide 20 -27

   - Hard for me to understand in general
   - I understood Slide 11 and 15 better



Slide 29

   - How fast can we get these new listings to be covered in SPU?
- When will we be starting B4?

Slide 31

   - How are we managing translation right now?
- How are we adhering to attributes requirements across various markets?
- For eg. Do we have a size chart for Fashion?
- How are we ensuring that all our listings come with a video?

Slide 35 – 38

   - Who is coordinating the definitions right now?

Is it Mohit or Christin
   side?

Slide 40 – 43

   - How will this impact us?

Slide 45

   - Shall we increase PH penetration%?
No reason for PH < MY?

And obvious
   cost savings for LFF?
- How about SG?
Can we do more LFF for SG as well?

Slide 47

   - For chatbot, is this the LLM chatbot?

Slide 48

   - How is the performance of PH agents compared to VN agents?

Slide 50

   - Are we keeping Shuning team involved in the process?

I told them to
   follow what SCS is doing just be behind on timeline
   - For eg. we should use same agency if it make sense



Slide 51

   - Option 1 is something that is already available for us right?

Slide 52

   - So how does this impact us exactly?

Slide 53

   - For rating invitation
      - Message is so long and hard to read. Should simplify it probably
      - Should we just give every reviewer?

Instead of having to do lucky
      draw?

- For bad reviewers
      - Did we manage to find out how sellers operate this on our platform
      right now?

- And I think the wording is very bad right now. It feels like
      bribery. It should be more along the lines of how we can do better. And
      would you like to give us another try via a voucher?

Slide 54

   - BR and PH still seems very bad when compared to the other markets?
What else do we need to do here?

content.answer:
Hi Ian,

Performance Dashboard (as of 23 Apr 2025)

A/ Topline/P&L


Highlights

   1.

   [Topline] Apr proj ADO 176k, 98% RR; ADO as %CB 108% RR; proj ADG $2.0M,
   103%RR; ADG as %CB 110% RR
   -

      Local SIP proj ADO 92% RR, %CB -1pp MoM; low RR due to IDSIP COD
      closure with -22% daily order impact from 19 Apr onwards; expect
to recover 11%
      ADO with low-risk shops COD re-opening
      -

      CNSIP proj ADO 104% RR, %CB +0.1pp MoM; driven by optimization
      initiatives 1) price reduction; 2) new SKU ads incubation
      2.

   [P&L] Apr proj combined PC2 at Shopee level at $88k, $2.1M
   under-performance mainly due to local SIP fraud case leading to
   historical high level of COD fail and cancellation costs



B/ IDSIP fraud situation


   1.

   Total 255 fraud sellers, total loss of $1.28M from 34k identified fraud
   orders, $200k (16%) recovered from seller wallet claw back
   2.

   Progress
   1.

      Concluded the investigation of current episode of fraud situation,
      seller identification, claw back etc.
      2.

      Started BAU daily escrow of COD success cases
      3.

      Started phased COD reopening to “safer” sellers
      4.

      Submitted long-term product solution request
      3.

   Next steps [ETA: end this week]
   1.

      Align long-term re-opening criteria and proceed to reopen
      2.

      Align escrow timeline for “delivery failed + non-fraud identified”
      orders
      3.

      ID legal to get back on the offline actions possible to recover loss
      4.

      Confirm long-term solution ETA and start to plan ops resources for
      fraud check


C/ BWT



Highlights

   1.

   MY SPX experienced manpower issues in SOCs which caused MY-export and
   MY-import lead time 0.5 days longer. Discussed with MY SPX - target to
   improve 0.1~0.2 days in May and fully recover in Jun
   2.

   IDSG 97% RR, 0.1 day long

Hi Ian,

Performance Dashboard (Apr full-month)

A/ Topline/P&L

content.answer: Apr MY SPX FM LT 1.5 days, 0.4 days longer than 1.1 days for Feb

According to MY SPX, LT started to recover since 21 Apr; expect to be
   1.3 days in May, and back to 1.1 days in Jun

Key reasons for slow recovery back to Feb level, due to HC/hiring
   gap. HC is not filled as planned and unable to hire sufficient part-timers



Do not understand. @'Lee Sun Cheah' <leesun.cheah@spxexpress.com> you know
what is going on here?


[image: image.png]


March and April generally due to the high volume and post raya backlog /
congestion issues


While in May, what we observe is that

(1) Relocation of JB RC end of Feb (no longer have the synergy of being in
the same WH as JB RC, required LH to transports goods to SIP WH

(2) Start of May with just post 5.5 volume (slightly elevated ADT), we
think should normalise to ~1.2


Next steps:

For immediate, to consider adjusting  LH, Ops shift timings and manpower
to improve handover %

To study SIP sorting plan with BRS come online to shorten overall
processing from SOC time in Q3



Best,

Lee Sun

Hi Ian,
Sorry coming back on this late.

When we look at ops clock, main gap is due to the delays in receiving of
volume to SIP warehouse that lead to
Cut off time 1 <3pm has reduced ~8% for May to 36.2% vs 44.42% in Feb,
though same day handover % remain high at >98.5%

Ops clock:
[image: image.png]
Same day handover:
[image: image.png]

<wanjung.huangwj@shopee.com> on how critical is to bring up the CO1 1
handover % (and at what % would really improve SG SPX downstream ADT), and
we layout the plans to achieve that

Best,
Lee Sun

relationship with Taobao, resulting in limited commitment to SHP

content.answer:
Hi Ian,

Please see responses below and version 5 attached.

Slide 4

   -

   Can highlight that changes started in Q4’24? Maybe box it up or
   something?
   -

      Ok. modified title and highlights on slide 4 accordingly

 Slide 22

   -

   Do not quite understand this, we are PC2 negative when advertising on
   ADO >5, but we will become PC2 positive when advertising on ADO >1? That
   does not sound right?
   -

      PC2 negative for March due to other IDSIP lanes cross-subsidizing
      IDMY lane for Raya push: for the total marketing budget for IDSIP, more
      budget is allocated to IDMY vs other IDSIP lanes; thus IDMY PC2 is
      negative, while other lanes positive, overall IDSIP PC2 breakeven
      -

      Updated the slide to show the incremental PC2 vs status quo;
      incremental PC2 comes from higher ads revenue collected by MP
from SIP. SIP
      ads budget is from export market profit passback (BPSF reduction
initiative)

Slide 26

   -

   What is the absolute PC2 difference here? % is very misleading? We could
   have made more overall even though the PC2% is lower? Especially if there
   is growth?
   -

      Added absolute PC2 difference in table
      -

      for case 1 (ads reduction), absolute PC2$ drops because topline drops
      -

      for case 2 (price reduction), absolute PC2$ drops because the growth
      in topline is not able to compensate PC2 drop per order

content.question: We need to ensure that acquired TB sellers are doing well?

If not it will
get harder and harder to acquire them?
Have we finalized the packages for F&B yet?
Why is it taking so long?

Are
we creating new packages or just leveraging existing packages?

*From:* Cuiwei Sun <cuiwei.sun@shopee.com>
*Sent:* Thursday, 22 May 2025 2:19 pm
*To:* Soo Siang, Ian Ho <hoi@sea.com>
*Cc:* Jianghong Liu <jianghong.liu@shopee.com>; Shuning Wang <
shuning.wang@shopee.com>; Keljin Chua <keljin.chua@shopee.com>; Jason Yu <
jason.yu@shopee.com>; Yu Zhang <yu.zhang@shopee.com>; Ailin Xie <
ailin.xie@shopee.com>; Wei Qiang <wei.qiang@shopee.com>; Momo Chen <
momo.chen@shopee.com>; Huang Wan-Jung <wanjung.huangwj@shopee.com>; Chiong
Zhi Ann <zhiann.chiong@shopee.com>; Steven Liu <steven.liusk@shopee.com>;
Wei Loong Yong <weiloong.yong@shopee.com>; Nicholas Lim <
nicholas.lim@shopee.com>; Keith Huang <keith.huang@shopee.com>; Joe Li <
joe.liyc@shopee.com>; Ren Jiahui <jiahui.ren@shopee.com>; Eveline Seah <
eveline.seahyl@shopee.com>
*Subject:* Re: SIP Weekly Update - Week ending 16 May



Hi Ian,



Please find our responses below. For CNSIP topic, will provide an update in
a separate email by EOD today.



Slide 8

   - The growth in # of SKUs is just too slow


   - +341k SKUs added across market since start of program (+112% SKU count
      May-25 vs Dec-24) mainly from cross-migration and new SKU upload

** Sourcing targets to be aligned

o   The slow  part is seller pitching and sku sourcing. Next steps are

§  Coordinate with BPM on the ERP project which is the key driver for #sku
growth

§  Continue to grow the pool via acquisition and sourcing in the upcoming
months

o   Expect to double current supply vs now by Dec’25 (+307% Dec-25 vs
Dec-24)



   - Do we know why some markets have much more SKUs than others?

For eg.
   PH has ~169K SKUs while other markets have way less?

- For PH, which is not lack of assortments, at the beginning, they
      decided to NOT design packages for counter TB, instead, we
agreed to track
      all TB skus in our program view
      - For other markets, we only track the TB skus which are put in TB
      program packages in program view
      - Note: we have migrated all the TB skus cross market, and had a
      process to migrate new skus every month



Slide 10

   - For these newly acquired sellers, how are we doing?

We probably need
   to create more success cases to gather more interest?

- Currently, they’re contributing around 1% of the total ADO (~360) and
      the key issue they’re facing is lack of traffic (~½ the current
traffic of
      existing sellers, with higher CPO / lower ROAS). To address this, we will
      push them to invest more on ads, and align with markets to provide more
      traffic exposure to these new SKUs
      - We’re currently working with markets to build success cases out of
      iFashion sellers (existing sellers that are on Taobao’s fashion microsite
      for trendy SKUs) - CN and us are aligned to use these success cases to
      pitch for acquisition instead



Slide 14

   - Performance is quite bad?

Growth is quite minimal


   - SKU effi is lower than expected, because


   - For both SG/MY, top GGPs are investing less in May vs Apr, and we’re
         working with CN team to ramp up investment for both markets


   - MY/SG’s decline due to 3PF rate increases and sellers shifting more of
            their resources from 3PF to CNLS (key reason), coupled
with other ops
            reasons such as infringement/penalty points that prevented
sellers from
            investing more


   - Platform’s PC2 reduction (to reinvest) is smaller than expected,
         because 1) (MY specific) the PC2 reinvestment is conditional on seller
         investment; 2) 3PF/SLS commission increased that offset the
PC2 reduction


   - SG’s plan is to ramp up on voucher rebates (ongoing)


   - MY’s plan is to do co-sponsor SSV to ramp down PC2 (start date 5.25)


   - How is it that # of SKUs can go down MOM?

- MY: Ops de-listed ~10k SKUs due to PO, that caused MY’s SKU# to fall
      significantly (-14% MoM) - and this caused overall SKU supply to fall.
      We’re working with sellers to re-list the SKUs by this week, and
in process
      to align with ops team not delist skus as the skus are approved to PO
      policy already.
      - VN: 2 top sellers move to CNLS

 Slide 15

   - #2 – How are the 8 sellers doing?

- The 8 sellers currently generate ~0.2% (~2 ADO) of the overall
      program’s ADO. The performance is due to poor conversion (0.1% CR vs
      existing sellers’ CR of 0.4%), likely due to the fact that none of their
      SKUs and shops have any ratings or credibility yet
      - We’ll work with SG/MY CB on what additional pushes can be given
      from platform to increase the credibility of these SKUs


   - #6 – What is happening here?

- This is to set-up installation for SG buyers (only for CB), and we’ll
      trial on a small scale with our TB furniture sellers with SLS enabled
      - We have also worked with SG cluster PICs to build a marketing plan
      around this, essentially waiving installation fees for buyers at launch
      - If UAT issues are fixed without a hitch, we target to be able to
      roll out by early next week


   - #8 – PH does not want to have bulky shipping channel?

- We have aligned with head of PHCB to de-prioritise this request due to
      the 2 following key reasons:


   - (1) Taobao PH will shut down their sea shipping channel effective 15
         May 2025, leaving only their air channel with a weight limit of 30kg
         - (2) Our current sea shipping channel already has a weight limit
         of 144kg


   - Hence, given that our shipping limits will be better than theirs, and
      no strong push from PH for bulky, we will not push this as priority



Slide 17

   - How about other markets?

- We’ll only push F&B for SG/MY/TH. For VN/TW, aligned with Reg PMO that
      we’ll not push this due to logistics restrictions. For PH, even
though they
      have not designed a specific incentive program for F&B, we’ll still look
      into other ways on how we can work with them in future



Others

   - What is the latest on ERP providers?

We are almost at the end of May


   - We’re preparing to start on the integration with both ERPs following
      the timeline below:


   - 聚水潭：Integration will start on 24 May (ETA completion early June)
         - 旺店通: Integration will start end-May (ETA completion mid June)


   - After integration is complete, TB/PDD SKUs can be synced to SHP and
      we’ll be start to ramp up by end-June


   - Have we started optimizing CN SIP yet?

I remember asking why the
   removal of management fee would take so long and only happen in June


   - Will share plan in a separate email


   - Do we have a mapping of GGP sellers that are not in all markets?

Shall
   we onboard them on all marketing using CN SIP?

- Yes, we do. And for all sellers that are not chosen by markets to join
      the TB self-run program, we have already used (and will continue to use)
      CNSIP to pitch these sellers


   - What exactly is happening on F&B?

content.answer:
Hi Ian,

We need to ensure that acquired TB sellers are doing well? If not it will
get harder and harder to acquire them?

   -

   Yes, however in fact, we’re ensuring that both key existing sellers
   (selected by CN team) that have influence over / being watched by unboarded
   sellers, as well as acquired sellers perform well
   -

   Hence, we’re selecting sellers for both fashion and furniture, and
   working with markets to start push for such sellers to be used as success
   cases for acquisition team (ETA start of push early June)



Have we finalized the packages for F&B yet? Why is it taking so long? Are
we creating new packages or just leveraging existing packages?

   -

   We’re leveraging existing packages (referencing fashion), however we
   were waiting on local team for the past couple weeks to confirm the budget
   for F&B incentives (now already in last stages of approval to be finalised
   this week), and will start F&B fully next week
   -

   In the meantime this week, we’ve also aligned with CN to start on
   acquisition/sourcing first without waiting for the incentives


Thank you.

We need to ensure that acquired TB sellers are doing well? If not it will
get harder and harder to acquire them?

   -

   (selected by CN team) that have influence over / being watched by unboarded
   sellers, as well as acquired sellers perform well
   -

   Hence, we’re selecting sellers for both fashion and furniture, and

proposal from us by 30 May on following initiatives.

content.answer:
Hi Ian,

For existing SIP sellers, are they in all markets that they are not in with
direct model?

   -

   Yes, we open SIP shops for all markets that they currently do not have
   Direct shops in



For new SIP sellers, the low hanging fruit for me would be GGPs who are not
in all our markets. It is unclear to me whether it should be

   -

   What the size is
   -

   We pitch them direct first
   -

   We get them on SIP first, then return shop to them later



Then we will move down the value chain in terms of type of sellers to
prioritize..

   -

   Size: we are building the funnel view for each market as part of our
   proposal next week. We are assessing, for all the CNCB GPs and their SKUs,
   how many are already in a local market (via direct or via CNSIP); for the
   remaining gap, why they are not live in CNSIP (not eligible? not pitched?
   Rejected? Migration issue?)
   -

   Direct first or SIP first:
   -

      We had initial discussion with Derek/Elaine, current preference is
      Direct first
      -

      The approach is
      -

         For the target seller pool that CN SMT team wants to pitch for
         Direct (mainly short-tail and Mall sellers), CN SMT will
pitch for Direct
         first, and then pitch for CNSIP as a backup option to seller
if the sellers
         confirms not to join Direct
         -

         For the sellers outside CN SMT target pool (mainly long-tail and
         non-Mall), we pitch to CNSIP directly
         -

         Eventual goal is we have one shop in each market, Direct or SIP,
         so the SKUs are made available to the market
         -

      We will jointly finalize the collaboration plan and impact early next
      week, to share our joint proposal by 30 May

Few things I think qutie important, but missing.

1) Team effort estimation.

For listing migration, @Yiru Zhao <yiru.zhao@shopee.com> team did some
estimation, which indicates quite substaintial HC requirement for listing
QC, unperforming listing removal and GR coordinations. Need to factor in
those. Also for RMs, please share the additional processes. Their focus for
2025 are LFF and pricing based on current plan. As SKU migration is the
major uplifting of this initiative, we have to consider the additional team
effort from local Ops, Reg Ops, CNCB RMs and CNCB SIP team.

2) For more SKU initiatives, can show me the SKU efficiency assumption on
slide 13? We have to bear in mind that large portion of these non migrated
SIP SKUs could be sellers purposely choose not to populate from MTSKUs to
MPSKUs.

3) Have we done remove duplications effect from SMT market expansion
initiative?

Hi JH,


Thanks for the comments. Please kindly see below responses inputted/aligned
among CB ops / CNCB SMT / CNSIP team. Thanks a lot!



1) Team effort estimation.


For listing migration, @Yiru Zhao team did some estimation, which indicates
quite substantial HC requirement for listing QC, unperforming listing
removal and GR coordinations. Need to factor in those. Also for RMs, please
share the additional processes. Their focus for 2025 are LFF and pricing
based on current plan. As SKU migration is the major uplifting of this
initiative, we have to consider the additional team effort from local Ops,
Reg Ops, CNCB RMs and CNCB SIP team.


Listing QC [Input from Reg Listing team]

As of April 2025, SIP has significantly higher QC agent cost per order
compared to non-SIP SKUs. Cost per order is expected to increase when SIP
migration intensifies.

As such, we proposed the following SOP to SIP to ensure they can curate
SKUs flexibly without being constrained by QC cost or volume:

   1.

   SIP SKU quality shall not rely on agent QC, most of the qc rules are
   already being put in SIP migration system.
   2.

   By default, SIP SKUs that trigger QC (e.g. flagged by Listing QC DS
   models) shall not be reviewed nor go live. You can imagine this to be an
   additional layer of filter to the existing listing validation checks such
   as logistic channel/prohibited category restrictions. Then these manual
   checks will be put in the system.
   3.

   If there are certain SKUs triggered for QC that SIP believe have high
   potential (e.g. above a certain ADO/ADGMV threshold), they can be
   whitelisted for agent review and a c

Hi Ian,


*Slide 3*

   -

   Do we have a week by week target at least for the next couple of months
   for both ERPs? # of sellers, SKUs, ADO etc?

We’re aligning ERP target with Jason team and CN team,  target to share
monthly target in next monthly slide

*Slide 10*

   -

   Are we at a stage right now that we should be rejecting sellers? We
   should just take all sellers?
   -

   What is the reason that sellers will reject us right now? And what
   exactly are they rejecting? I thought they agreed to onboard to begin with?

For acquired sellers that accept to join Shopee, we will not reject onboard
these sellers. Sellers can open their shops without incentives. However,
since resources are offered to the sellers, market prefer to focus
resources on sellers that matches market demand, hence we allow for market
level selection to join program

Main rejection reasons are (1) sellers not having sufficient manpower (2)
their focus is in selective/other markets. We will re-pitch these sellers
using ERP solution again

*Slide 14*

   -

   So bad for SG/MY? We should not be too affected by actions of 3PF if the
   growth lever should be via sellers on SLS? Feels very odd that both markets
   are just not growing at all

To clarify on how 3PF fee impacts SLS sellers

   -

   Current MY ADO contribution: 57% from 3PF, 38% from CNLS, 4% from SLS
   -

   Most 3PF sellers use SLS channel, either as backup fulfillment or they
   are “fake 3PF” - this group is impacted by 3PF fee increase despite
   fulfilling via SLS

We deep-dived to SG/MY ADO drop

   -

   [-77 ADO] 3PF fee increase leading to seller lower ads / higher pricing;
   Jun focus on pushing seller to recover investment, and work with SG/MY to
   reinvest PC2; ETA 13 Jun
   -

   [-60 ADO] Top sellers incur penalty points blocking them from campaign
   participation; in progress of identifying reasons for penalty
   (system/program/seller issu

*From:* Peiya Xia <peiya.xia@shopee.com>
*Sent:* Saturday, 7 June 2025 11:47 am
*To:* Ian Ho <hoi@sea.com>
*Cc:* Cuiwei Sun <cuiwei.sun@shopee.com>; Jianghong Liu <
jianghong.liu@shopee.com>; Shuning Wang <shuning.wang@shopee.com>; Keljin
Chua <keljin.chua@shopee.com>; Jason Yu <jason.yu@shopee.com>; Yu Zhang <
yu.zhang@shopee.com>; Ailin Xie <ailin.xie@shopee.com>; Wei Qiang <
wei.qiang@shopee.com>; Momo Chen <momo.chen@shopee.com>; Huang Wan-Jung <
wanjung.huangwj@shopee.com>; Chiong Zhi Ann <zhiann.chiong@shopee.com>;
Steven Liu <steven.liusk@shopee.com>; Wei Loong Yong <
weiloong.yong@shopee.com>; Nicholas Lim <nicholas.lim@shopee.com>; Keith
Huang <keith.huang@shopee.com>; Joe Li <joe.liyc@shopee.com>; Ren Jiahui <
jiahui.ren@shopee.com>; Lee Jia Yi <jiayi.jylee@shopee.com>; Eveline Seah <
eveline.seahyl@shopee.com>
*Subject:* Re: SIP Weekly Update - Week ending 30 May



Hi Ian,



*Slide 3*

· Do we have a week by week target at least for the next couple of months
for both ERPs?
# of sellers, SKUs, ADO etc?

We’re aligning ERP target with Jason team and CN team,  target to share
monthly target in next monthly slide

*Slide 10*

· Are we at a stage right now that we should be rejecting sellers?

We
should just take all sellers?
· What is the reason that sellers will reject us right now?

And what
exactly are they rejecting?
I thought they agreed to onboard to begin with?

For acquired sellers that accept to join Shopee, we will not reject onboard
these sellers. Sellers can open their shops without incentives. However,
since resources are offered to the sellers, market prefer to focus
resources on sellers that matches market demand, hence we allow for market
level selection to join program

Main rejection reasons are (1) sellers not having sufficient manpower (2)
their focus is in selective/other markets. We will re-pitch these sellers
using ERP solution again

*Slide 14*

· So bad for SG/MY?

We should not be too affected by actions of 3PF if the
growth lever should be via sellers on SLS?

Feels very odd that both markets
are just not growing at all

To clarify on how 3PF fee impacts SLS sellers

· Current MY ADO contribution: 57% from 3PF, 38% from CNLS, 4% from SLS

· Most 3PF sellers use SLS channel, either as backup fulfillment or they
are “fake 3PF” - this group is impacted by 3PF fee increase despite
fulfilling via SLS

We deep-dived to SG/MY ADO drop

· [-77 ADO] 3PF fee increase leading to seller lower ads / higher pricing;
Jun focus on pushing seller to recover investment, and work with SG/MY to
reinvest PC2; ETA 13 Jun

· [-60 ADO] Top sellers incur penalty points blocking them from campaign
participation; in progress of identifying reasons for penalty
(system/program/seller issue); then act accordingly

· [-57 ADO] Top sellers moving to CNLS; next step to include CNLS ADO into
tracking; ETA 13 Jun



*Slide 15*

· For #2, have they started selling in markets?
· For #4, is this sourcing translating to orders?

_attachment.filename: Counter Taobao Weekly Update - Week ending 30 May.pdf

content.answer:
Hi Ian and Jianghong,

Pls find tmr's FP&A monthly meeting, we will be covering following topics


*CB Performance update*

   - May’25 Actuals Performance (as of Jun 6th) - with mgmt reporting view
   and SLS true cost savings view
   - Jun’25 Projection - with KRJP breakdown

*Key Initiatives Updates*

   - All BU: CB P&L Projection Accuracy Improvement Initiatives
   - All BU: CB Seller Universe current and Year End performance
   - Marketplace/Choice: June Finalized Commission Change Update
      - Anbo warehouse commission and business follow-up
      - CN new merchant commission waiving proposal
   - Marketplace: May’25 Commission Waive Program Update
   - SLS: MY Self Collection Channel Push study

*Besides, next Thursday will be Reg/Local FP&A workshop, where CB is
invited for a sharing, and kindly seek your review on if any adjustments
are needed! Key purpose as follows,*

   - *Local Ownership Initiative* Review and* re-emphasize importance *of
   timely, careful, and accurate submission requirements
   - Collect constructive feedbacks on how to collaborate better future

Best,

*Faye Yan*

Shopee | Cross Border

*Mobile:* +65 87767030

*Address:* 5 Science Park Drive, Shopee Building, Singapore 118265

Slide 9: For SLS logic errors, I remember we saw that in  MY, TH and BR in
May?

Slide 23: So the key issue here is ESF is too low. True cost difference is
$0.10, but ESF difference is $0.67.

Hi Ian and Jianghong,

Pls find reply to your questions below.

Slide 4 Kuan Wai Mei <waimei.kuan@shopee.com>

   -

   [Ian] For 1P + Consignment, what is the latest alignment? The losses
   here are quite huge?


   -

   1) commission to be -2% vs CNCB SLS post commission increase rate
   -

   2) SLS not to adjust in system, for CB internal view to use last
   alignment ~5% discount vs current ~29% discount
   -

   3) SCS target reported to external will be moderate version (Jun’25
   target - moderate (and external view) is 255k RR 108%, while optimistic
   (and internal view) is 278k RR 98%.

Big losses mainly in MP PC2, seller P&L breakeven and MoM UE stabilized

   -

   For MP PC2, why is it very negative?
   -

      Mainly due to high ADGMV% penetration in BR where CB is also negative
      -

   For MP PC2 MoM changes, +89k MoM vs May’25, improvement is driven by:
   -

      Lower MP Revenue from reduced platform fees (net off base commission
      reduction from Anbo rate and increased fees in SG/MY/VN/PH),
this reduction
      have been reflected as improvement in seller P&L (+315k USD MoM
vs May’25 )
      -

      Offset against lower PRM spending, CIR% -22%, +0.5% MoM vs May’25

Table - Jun’25 MP PC2 and MP PC2% by markets


Slide 5 Kuan Wai Mei <waimei.kuan@shopee.com> Faye Yan <faye.yan@shopee.com>

   -

   [Ian] Same question for 1P + consignment >> addressed above
   -

   [Ian] Why is KR missing ADO targets? Haenle Suh <haenle.suh@shopee.com> Yuna
   Kwon <yuna.kwon@shopee.com>

performance, KR ADO runrate expect to land at 103% runrate, will monitor
closely

come? Steven
   Liu <steven.liusk@shopee.com>

push(MoM +8%), incremental ROI is -22% which is below regional guideline of
50%

content.answer:
Expand Item Coverage: 1) Include high ASP items from our existing
      product lines; 2) Develop more new high ASP items.

Enhance Traffic: 1) Utilize content traffic channels, such as
      livestreams and AMS; 2) Leverage Shopchat for increased
engagement; 3) Add
      a collection entry on the PDP - the product feature request has been
      submitted

Improve Conversion: Focus on increasing conversions alongside a push
      for high MBS vouchers.
      -

   Note: Definition of high ASP collection: ASP > 4~11 USD (varies by site,
   higher vs. shop average ASP$2.3), which is above shop ABS & local high MBS
   FSV threshold



Slide 14

   -

   Average waiting time in queue is probably too long? This is MY numbers.
   -

   Not sure where you get your benchmark from but looks odd in general


Our MY site waiting time in queue comparison with Local SCS/SBS MY is as
table below:

   -

   In May, our waiting time in queue was actually shorter than Local
   SCS/SBS MY
   -

   However, our waiting time in queue is longer than local SCS/SBS MY,
   mainly because we launched outbound promotion chats in Jun to promote high
   ASP collection, ect, resulting in higher volume of inquiries, stretching
   our service SLA. We will optimize the SOP to increase the productivity,
   meanwhile to hire more agents if really need.




Slide 15

   -

   Is this too much to cover in welcome message? Feels very messy and not
   focused



   -

   We believe that our current approach aligns with common practices
   adopted by most top sellers on Taobao and JD (can refer to the snapshots
   below). These sellers frequently highlight various shop promotions in their
   welcome messages.
   -

   However, ours look messy because our chat doesn’t support well designed
   menus/hyperlinks to host the promotion messages, which we have already
   requested BPM to support. Once the function is ready, our message will look
   as clean as others.


Snapshots: 1) JD: 京东京造, Thinkpad; 2) Taobao: Olay, P&G




Slide 16

   -

   How does the cost element look?
   -

   I am not sure about this recommendation at all. Never heard of running
   CS out of VN before. I think it is our management problem which is the issue
   -

   This is a odd recommendation in general..

Thanks.



Slide 4

   - Can we always benchmark our YOY growth against platform?
- Can remind me what our full year 2025 growth is expected to be?

And
   how does it compare with platform?
- Our YOY topline grew by 17% but our P&L only grew by 2%?

What is the
   reason for this?

Slide 6

   - Where is growth of JP new sellers coming from?

- I remember we had some plan to help more sellers apply for license to
   go into VN, where did we land on that?

LFF seems to be missing targets by
   quite a bit?

Slide 10

   - How are we faring vs targets for each market right now?

Slide 12 -25

   - I am not sure about the context here. I think for any topic that is
   brought to me, I need to understand what we are trying to do,
   recommendation and impact. Right now, I cannot tell what is being presented
   here



Slide 27 – 29

   - Is there a difference if it is LFF?

Slide 33 – 35

   - Again, not very sure of the context.
   - What is the impact?

Who does it impact, what exactly are we doing, and
   what will the eventual impact be?

Others

   - I am concerned about the growth on several markets which are lagging
   behind platform growth
   - I remember we been trying to launch several programs on JPN front, how
   is that doing and what is the impact
   - I saw Ali doing something with KR sellers, is that worth reviewing?
- I saw TT doing something with KR sellers, what exactly are they doing?
And is it something that we are lacking as well?

And I remember we were pushing for LS and Video growth previously, how is
all of that coming along?
