And is it something that we are lacking as well?

content.answer:
In general, the CN items demonstrate a strong positive relationship
   between PRM and order volume—indicating that increase in PRM is closely
   associated with higher order counts. However, KR items exhibit a more
   inelastic response. That is, increase in PRM does not translate into order
   volume with the same magnitude observed for CN items

Even for markets where KR items do show a positive correlation between
   PRM and order volume, the effect size is minimal, typically less than 1% of
   the corresponding impact seen for CN items in the same market

-> This suggests significant differences in buyer responsiveness between
the two items which aligns with our general understanding of KR items.

CN items are generally price sensitive which makes more responsive to
   PRM campaigns(vs. KR items being premium priced/less price elastic so the
   conversion will be lower unless consumers were already looking for the
   product)

CN items are more value for money which also leads to impulsive buys yet
   KR items are premium thus the buying journey is longer.



Slide 6 Haenle Suh <haenle.suh@shopee.com>

   -

   Where is growth of JP new sellers coming from? Xiaolin Huang
   <xiaolin.huang@shopee.com>
   -

   ~30% of JP ADO growth comes from new sellers. New seller growth is
   largely organic, facilitated by ICB mass education and initiatives:
   -

seller community high quality sellers with background of domestic
      and CB ecommerce; and resellers are the key drivers (~90% of ADO
      contribution to new seller ADO)
      -

ICB revamped multiple communication channels (emails, webinars
      etc.) for pushing sellers open multiple market/shop when onboarding
      -

revamp of onboarding form to highlight PH market -  drive new
      seller ADO in PH + 28% MoM (35% of new seller ADO)
      -

   So in H2, the key focus -  push more for new sellers, and new sellers
   shop/SKU migration. And will share in more detail once ready.
   -

   Joanne Lee <joanne.lee@shopee.com> I remember we had some plan to help
   more sellers apply for license to go into VN, where did we land on that?
   LFF seems to be missing targets by quite a bit?




   -

   Yes, the program is up and running — we currently have 5 sellers
   enrolled in VN (ADO impact: 0.8k), along with sellers from other markets.
   Most are expected to inbound by mid to late July.
   -

   As of Jun 19, LFF ADO target is on track at RR% 99% MTD, but our LFF
   penetration to KRMP RR is relatively low at 81%. Key contributing factors
   include:
   -

      VN:
      -

         Delay in new seller/SKU inbounds due to customs policy tightening
         (June 1–15), which has since been resolved
         -

         LFF order decline from one seller due to lower 3PF SKU exposure
         vs. May (a one-off issue)
         -

         VN topline actuals reached 140% of target, which affected the LFF%
         target
         -

      PH:
      -

         One key seller (Mediheal, LFF ADO impact: -0.2k) has not
         replenished stock since Mar due to seller side manpower
shortage, though
         the target was still included in June
         -

         PH topline actuals were at 121% of target, mostly driven by K-pop
         orders—a category not suited for FBS—affecting LFF% target



Slide 10  Joanne Lee <joanne.lee@shopee.com>

   -

   How are we faring vs targets for each market right now?

As of Jun 19,

   -

   LFF ADO at 15.4k (RR 99% to ADO target), MoM +14%
   -

   26% penetration (RR 81% to penetration target)
   -

   LFF ADO EOM projection lands at 90%, having a projected MoM growth% o

the projected UE may not be healthy given the high LFF penetration
target in H2

and whether the very high LFF% targets set earlier in the year were
realistic


Discussion/Decision to be made: After reviewing the UE for the initial
targets, we found that overall UE was quite healthy (UE=0.95 USD), having
the best UE across all 3 scenarios.

At the market level, SLS showed higher UE than LFF in SG/MY, while LFF
   UE was higher over SLS in TH/PH/VN.

However, the initial targets appear too aggressive given the H1 track
   record.

Therefore, we propose 2 revised target scenarios (scenario 2 and 3),
   guided by two principles: 1) Minimizing impact to UE 2) Ensuring
   projections are realistic yet ambitious. Slides 12–25 show the resulting
   PnL and UE across Scenarios 1, 2, and 3.


Recommendation & Impact:  We recommend proceeding with Scenario 2 (slide

as it is a more realistic projection than Scenario 1—excluding
non-managed sellers and SKUs while still being ambitious—and results in a
smaller negative impact in PnL and UE compared to Scenario 3 (PnL down
1.46%, UE down 2% vs. Scenario 1).

Scenario 2 has a lower UE than Scenario 1 mainly because it assumes a
   lower VN 3PF% (44% → 33%) and a higher VN SLS order share—since VN 3PF has
   a higher UE than VN SLS.

Scenario 2 also has better UE than scenario 3 because it assumes higher
   VN 3PF% vs scenario 3.


Slide 27 – 29

   -

   Is there a difference if it is LFF? Jaeeun Park <jaeeun.park@shopee.com>



   -

   Can lower the selling price when using LFF (e.g. from 7.1usd to 6.3usd)
   but still cannot fully close the gap with local selling price (vs. 5.5usd).
   Pls see below table.



   -

   When using LFF (White channel, ocean), sellers can expect -4%p decrease
   in commission, -33%p decrease in operation cost logistics cost in SG, and
   for MY, -6%p decrease in commission, -30%p decrease in logistics cost
   -

      Cost savings from using LFF can help to close the price gap between
      KR sellers and local sellers, KR selling prices become -1 USD, -0.5
      USD cheaper ([A]-[B]) in SG, MY respectively when using LFF
      -

      Using the consol (ocean) channel results in $0.4 less cost saving
      (avg. of SG/MY) compared to the white channel
      -

      -

         SG don’t have gray channel
         -

   However, need to check sellers’ feedback on using LFF because:
   -

Due to F&B’s relatively short shelf life (ocean shipping can take
      up to 4 weeks), sellers might think it’s risky to inbound, or the WH may
      reject the inbound.
      -

Not many F&B SKUs can meet the ADIS requirements set by the WH,
      and if they are not top SKUs, they may remain as black stock.
      -

White channel is the most cost-effective option but F&B resellers
      are hard to get proper license to use white channel


Notes:

*Price comparison from the SKUs of KR F&B brand’s same skus of KR & Local,
item & model name matching 80%, single-item order (using SLS)

*LFF costs are calculated based on level 2 category = ‘snacks’, under the
assumption that ‘white channel & ocean shipping’ is used



Slide 33 – 35 Songie Song <songie.song@shopee.com>

   -

   Again, not very sure of the context.:


[Background] As shared by TH team during THCB monthly meeting last time,
TISI &FDA requirements will be rolled out to all SKUs throughout the year,
Thus, We looked at KR assortment to get a sense of how many SKUS/ ADO to be
potentially impacted, by when, and potentially how much could be saved, and
what’s our plan on saving more.


   -

   What is the impact? Who does it impact, what exactly are we doing, and
   what will the eventual impact be?


[Impact] It will impact new listing SKUs from July onwards, and existing
SKUs in case that VN gov’t reports. FDA related categories (mostly beauty)
to be impacted, which accounts for 70% out of total KR-TH ADO.

   -

   70% of KRTH ADO’s breakdown as follows:
   -

      30%, already complied. Thus no issue at all
      -

      16%: Structurally possible to comply as they’re operated by brand
      owners; however, this depends on cost efficiency on SKU level  to get the
      FDA license
      -

      24%: highly likely not be able to get the license as operated by
      resellers; resellers can use registered FDA license numbers but having
      legal risks if they don’t get agreement from brand owners, thus
we will not
      proactively push resellers to use it.


[what we’re doing now] we think that quick win is to push ‘not yet
complied’ SKUs operated by brand owners. Thus, we’ll target high ADO
efficient SKUs to push brands to get the license. Key blocker i

LS

  As of YTD, KRCB LS GMV contribution stands at 11.5%, which is higher than
the platform average of 7.0%.

  We’ve also exceeded our target run rate so far and will continue to push
through the second half.

VDO

As of YTD, KRCB VDO GMV contribution stands at 4.0%, which is slightly
below the platform avg of 5.3%.

However, we are quite confident that we can ramp up in 2H. This week, we
aligned with the VN local team on LS/VDO push initiatives and got key
programs confirmed specifically for KR sellers for 2H. Since VN is the most
important market for KR, we plan to use VN as a reference and expand these
programs to the remaining three markets — TH, PH, and MY. (those 4 markets
account for 85% of LS/VDO GMV).

There are mainly 2 reasons that we were a bit slow with Video in 1H.

[1] Low KOL adoption of top order volume items compared to local

: for top 20% SKUs by order volume, KR VDO avg order contribution - 1.1% vs
significantly lower than the local avg of 6.7%.


[2] Low supply of seller generated videos compared to local

: for the same top 20% SKUs, there are approx.49 videos per each local top
SKU, compared to only 10 videos per KR top SKUs - a gap of nearly 5x.


  To address these challenges, we are working on two main tracks for 2H:

[1] Boosting KOL Adoption based on local push program participation

VN: We have aligned with local to ensure top performing KR sellers
participation in the

monthly KOL video push programs starting from July, which is expected to
positively

impact video traffic and order performance.

      (* Program in detail : 1) boosting good qualified KOL videos of KR
sellers with platform

support voucher & exclusive slots. 2) encouraging good qualified KOLs to
create

videos with KR SKUs)

TH, PH, MY:  Based on the local video push program in VN, we plan to roll
out similar

programs in those markets.

[2] Increasing Seller Self-Video Supply

All four key markets (VN, TH, MY, PH) are clearly facing a shortage of KR
seller self-video

 supply. We will continue pitching KR sellers to upload more videos,
supported by the

recent PC video upload rollout and mass communication of conten

content.question:
If we look at actuals, other than TW, all markets are growing slower than
platform

   - MY from Jan to May
   - PH from May to June
   - SG from Jan to June
   - TH from Apr to June
   - VN from Jan to June
   - BR from Jan to June



Do we know the reason why?
And how are we dealing with this?

In general, I think we had 3 main initiatives at the start of the year

   - New seller acquisition (not quite sure how it is doing and impact)
   - Growth via Content (seems ok I think?)
   - Growth via FBS (just missing targets all around)



And in order to grow faster than platform, I am not sure if the above is
enough or if we need to do more. But I am not sure we will hit our year end
projections, if we our actuals has been slower than platform for H1’25.

Hi Ian,



*Performance Dashboard** (as of 11 Jun)*



*A/ Topline/P&L*

** Taobao program target ongoing adjustments*





How come direct selling is growing so slowly?
What lanes are coming up next?
For CN SIP, why is the P&L negative?

Growth seems really slow as well given
the small size?

*D/ CS (as of 8 Jun 2025)*



SG Numbers are very bad in general across the board?

content.answer:
The TB wishlist SKU sourcing target is for all markets, and differs by
   site due to different market potential and incentive package across sites
   (see chart below)

From existing sellers for wishlist SKU sourcing


Chart: Fashion wishlist SKU# target and Jun progress (as of Jun 17th)

TB SKU Wishlist, SKU # target

Dec Target

Jun Target

Jun MTD progress

Total new to SHP

19,500

1,500

1,769

SG

9,750

750

150

MY

9,750

750

222

TH

15,600

1,200

1,022

VN

15,600

1,200

685

Note: Sourced SKUs are currently under local review. Total new-to-SHP SKU #
expected to decrease if local identifies some sourced SKUs as non-wishlist
items; SKU # per site expected to increase, as SKUs will migrate to more
sites once confirmed as wishlist SKU.

Why the number of Wishlist SKU sourcing is small:



   -

   Total TB wishlist pool for fashion is 42k, through regional BI crawling
   of top 100 pages of TB SKU
   -

   Wishlist SKU sourcing EOY target is 19.5k, 46% coverage of total pool
   42k.
   -

      19.5k target is based on bottom-up method: ~150 managed sellers to
      source SKU, where 30 top sellers each source 80 SKUs per month, and other
      120 sellers source 5 SKUs per month
      -

   Despite strong push by CN cluster, wishlist SKU sourcing is limited by

complex SOP and 2) low seller willingness due to insufficient incentive
   -

Complex SOP for sellers: Need project team and CN cluster co-work
      to further simplify SOP
      -

low seller willingness mainly due to incentive is not attractive
      enough
      -

         SG/MY: lower willingness of SKU sourcing, due to 1) relatively low
         market size and not priority for sellers to invest and upload
SKU, 2) low
         incentive (SG/MY gives only 2% item rebate)
         -

         TH/VN as benchmark: relatively higher willingness, given 1) larger
         market size, 2) higher lever incentive (e.g., VN provides 8%
item rebate
         for Top 200 in newly sourced TB SKU)
         -

      wishlist SKU sourcing

Additional SKU sourcing besides wishlist



   -

   Besides Wishlist SKU sourcing, Fashion also drive SKU# by SKU site
   migration and other SKU sourcing (not in wishlist), will contribute
   40k-90k new SKU# per site by Dec’25


Slide 8

#3 >> Where are we on solving of the pricing problem?

   -

   Feedback from ERPs is that they don’t have the desired TB price in their
   system
   -

   Thus, we’ll build a seller tool to lower prices for us and work with CN
   team to push sellers to use the tool


Slide 16 and 21

Do we have actual ADO numbers here? Progress seems very slow

   -

   Program has already started, and pitching will conclude this week
   (started around 1 week ago after package was finalised)


   -

   Once we’ve the final numbers from pitching, and BI completes all key
   metrics of the table, we will be ready to show it in next week’s update


Overall, the progress seems very slow for this entire project. It is not
moving at the pace and speed that it should.

Noted, we will push harder with CN team and local team on both sku# and sku
efficiency.

related responses will follow separately.

How come direct selling is growing so slowly? What lanes are coming up next?



MYSG

   -

   Overall ADO growth on track considering new system de-bugging and new
   shop ramp up
   -

   New seller acquisition slower than expected mainly due to slow
   onboarding: Jun MTD 735 sellers acquired, out of which 458 (62%) still in
   onboarding process to open shop, 101 (14%) shop opened but on HM to
   finalize pricing; 176 (24%) active selling, 41% RR of Jun active selling
   target of 430
   -

   Slow onboarding because sellers need to take multiple steps to set up
   sub account to run oversea shops. We have explored ops and system
   workarounds, but decided to park as they could disrupt sellers’ local shop
   (MY shop) operations. Plans in place to accelerate 1) short-term: RM to
   support sellers closely on onboarding and set up the shop for sellers with
   their permission; 2) long-term: system solution to open shop automatically
   for sellers once they accepted invitation [FRF submitted, ETA pending RSX
   review]



Do we know when this will be ready?



Why did we not consider this in the development phase?


FRF submitted for RSX review, target launch ETA end-Jul/early-Aug. We
considered it in the dev phase but originally put it in phase 2 due to high
dev efforts needed. Have expedited it to be the P0 feature to add once we
noticed the onboarding issue.



   -

   Concurrently we will accelerate pitching by expanding mass pitching pool
   to cover additional 50k qualified sellers (total mass pitching 133k) and
   repitching non-responsive sellers via system PN/SCA [ETA 20 Jun]

How is the user experience right now, is it that bad? Does it make sense to
pitch to so many sellers if the user experience is bad?

content.question:
What exactly are the multiple steps that sellers need to take in the
onboarding process, and how long do these steps actually take?

*From:* Cuiwei Sun <cuiwei.sun@shopee.com>
*Sent:* Sunday, 22 June 2025 7:07 pm
*To:* Ian Ho <hoi@sea.com>
*Cc:* Shuning Wang <shuning.wang@shopee.com>; Jason Yu <jason.yu@shopee.com>;
Keljin Chua <keljin.chua@shopee.com>; Jianghong Liu <
jianghong.liu@shopee.com>; Yu Zhang <yu.zhang@shopee.com>; Ailin Xie <
ailin.xie@shopee.com>; Wei Qiang <wei.qiang@shopee.com>; Momo Chen <
momo.chen@shopee.com>; Huang Wan-Jung <wanjung.huangwj@shopee.com>; Chiong
Zhi Ann <zhiann.chiong@shopee.com>; Steven Liu <steven.liusk@shopee.com>;
Wei Loong Yong <weiloong.yong@shopee.com>; Nicholas Lim <
nicholas.lim@shopee.com>; Keith Huang <keith.huang@shopee.com>; Joe Li <
joe.liyc@shopee.com>; Ren Jiahui <jiahui.ren@shopee.com>; Lee Jia Yi <
jiayi.jylee@shopee.com>; Eveline Seah <eveline.seahyl@shopee.com>
*Subject:* Re: SIP Weekly Update - Week ending 13 Jun



Hi Ian,



Please find responses related to Direct Selling below in green. Taobao
related responses will follow separately.



How come direct selling is growing so slowly?
What lanes are coming up next?

MYSG

·  Overall ADO growth on track considering new system de-bugging and new
shop ramp up

·  New seller acquisition slower than expected mainly due to slow
onboarding: Jun MTD 735 sellers acquired, out of which 458 (62%) still in
onboarding process to open shop, 101 (14%) shop opened but on HM to
finalize pricing; 176 (24%) active selling, 41% RR of Jun active selling
target of 430

·  Slow onboarding because sellers need to take multiple steps to set up
sub account to run oversea shops. We have explored ops and system
workarounds, but decided to park as they could disrupt sellers’ local shop
(MY shop) operations. Plans in place to accelerate 1) short-term: RM to
support sellers closely on onboarding and set up the shop for sellers with
their permission; 2) long-term: system solution to open shop automatically
for sellers once they accepted invitation [FRF submitted, ETA pending RSX
review]



Do we know when this will be ready?
Why did we not consider this in the development phase?

FRF submitted for RSX review, target launch ETA end-Jul/early-Aug. We
considered it in the dev phase but originally put it in phase 2 due to high
dev efforts needed. Have expedited it to be the P0 feature to add once we
noticed the onboarding issue.



·  Concurrently we will accelerate pitching by expanding mass pitching pool
to cover additional 50k qualified sellers (total mass pitching 133k) and
repitching non-responsive sellers via system PN/SCA [ETA 20 Jun]

How is the user experience right now, is it that bad?

Does it make sense to
pitch to so many sellers if the user experience is bad?

Based on seller feedback, the onboarding process and instruction are clear;
just the process is tedious and troublesome. Experience after shop opening
is good with automatic listing/review/rating migration, price
recommendations, and chat/logs operations in the same interface.



Agree that we need the onboarding process to be simplified before we can
meaningfully scale up. Therefore we will pause mass re-pitching of
not-yet-accepted sellers (originally planned once per month) till the
system improvement is in place in early-Aug. Meanwhile for savvy sellers
who are able to navigate through the current system we will support
onboarding closely via RM/CS.



Upcoming lanes (overview attached below)

·  *VNMY (biz launch 23 Jun)*: system ready by 16 Jun, pending final legal
clearance ETA 19 Jun; pitching started 2 Jun, target to onboard 1st batch
of ~300 sellers on 23 Jun

·  *IDMY (biz launch 23 Jun)*: system ready by 23 Jun, pitching started 28
May, target to onboard 1st batch of 200 sellers on 23 Jun

·  *MYPH (biz launch early Aug)*: system ready by end-Jul; aligning with
the MYCB team to secure RM resources for pitching and onboarding support

·  *THPH (biz launch early Aug)*: system ready by end-Jul; pitching to
start from 23 Jun, target to onboard 1st batch of ~900 sellers by early-Aug

Will the issue above plague other new lanes as well?

content.answer:
Hi Ian,

Performance Dashboard (as of 18 Jun)

A/ Topline/P&L

* Taobao program target ongoing adjustments

Highlights

   1.

   [Topline] Jun proj ADO 228k, 109% RR, %CB +0.3pp MoM; ADG $2.6M, 113%RR,
   %CB +0.9pp MoM
   1.

      Local SIP ADO 104% RR, %CB +0.2pp MoM; increase in %CB driven by 1)
      higher ads coverage with BPSF budget and ID fraud clawback amount, 2)
      upsized rebates for trendy assortment push
      2.

      CNSIP ADO 103% RR, %CB stable MoM; RR on target driven by ongoing
      optimization initiatives (e.g., ads markup removal for
non-performing SKUs)
      2.

   [P&L] Jun proj combined PC2 $3.55M, $0.16M over-performance
   1.

      Local SIP 0.35M out-performance, mainly due to higher ads take-rate
      earned by MP as SIP increases ads spending with BPSF budget
      2.

      CN SIP 0.2M under-performance, mainly due to reinvesting 5% GMV to
      counter revenue loss from mgt fee removal, to protect topline; will
      maintain reinvestment in Jun-Aug


B/ CNSIP revamp progress update

   1.

   New seller pitching/rejected seller repitching post management fee
   removal
   1.

      May pitching on 28 May: 45M SKUs pitched together with seller center

Hi Ian,

Please find SIP weekly update below for your review, including follow-up
questions from last weekly on 1) CS; 2) Taobao (in pdf).

Performance Dashboard (as of 25 Jun)

A/ Topline/P&L

* Taobao program Jul target to be aligned

Highlights

   1.

   [Topline] Jun proj ADO 230k, 104% RR, %CB +0.3pp MoM; ADG $2.4M, 104%RR,
   %CB +0.2pp MoM
   1.

      Local SIP ADO 105% RR, %CB +0.3pp MoM; higher RR driven by higher new
      ads ramp up with BPSF budget support and IDSIP fraud clawback.
ADG and ADG%
      slightly missed targets due to ABS lower than expected driven by 1)
      platform MP PRM increases significantly. 2) Platform provides SIP
      additional item rebates in SG/MY to drive ADO
      2.

      CNSIP ADO 103% RR, %CB stable MoM; RR on target driven by ongoing
      optimization initiatives (e.g., ads markup removal for
non-performing SKUs)
      2.

   [P&L] Jun proj combined PC2 $3.3M, $69k under-performance
   1.

      Local SIP $159k out-performance, mainly due to higher SLS profit as
      vol increase led to lower cost
      2.

      CN SIP $228k under-performance, mainly due to reinvesting 5% GMV to
      counter revenue loss from mgt fee removal, to protect topline; will
      maintain reinvestment in Jun-Aug


B/ CNSIP revamp progress update

   1.

   New seller pitching/rejected seller repitching post management fee
   removal (May+Jun batch): total 27k GPs pitched; overall 65% pitching
   success rate on GP level and 39% pitching success rate on SKU level (lower
   than 70% target)


   2.

   Top rejection reasons are: 1) worry about potential loss-making (40% sku
   coverage); 2) want to focus on direct shop (30% sku coverage); 3) not
   familiar with SIP model (11% sku coverage)
   3.

   Next steps to improve pitching success
   1.

      Before next repitch, add a CNSC banner + improve mass pitching comms
      to educate seller further on how settlement price

D/ CS (as of 22 Jun 2025)





Waiting time in queue still feels long in general for ID lanes? And MYSG?


   - ID lanes: because ID experienced a spike in chat volume last Monday,

   improved to 6.1 minutes.
   - MYSG: because SG lanes faced a manpower shortage due to the

   handle live chats from 30th June after one week of training. Improvement in
   waiting time should be seen from this week onwards.



Performance (IDMY, VNMY) - As of 28 June

IDMY: MTD 103 sellers acquired, 97 in onboarding process since 24
VNMY: MTD 3.5k sellers acquired, onboarding by batches since 24 Jun;

content.answer:
MYSG: topline on track: MTD ADO 7.8k, 110% RR, +7% MoM; SKU count
493k, 109% RR; # sellers behind target mainly due to slow onboarding
process of newly acquired sellers, May-MTD 917 new sellers acquired, 492
(54%) in onboarding process; 151 (16%) shop opened but in HM to finalize
pricing; 274 (30%) active selling. Product solution to support fast
onboarding expected in early/mid-Aug

IDMY: MTD 103 sellers acquired, 97 in onboarding process since 24
Jun; 6 starts to sell

VNMY: MTD 3.5k sellers acquired, onboarding by batches since 24 Jun;
105 start to sell, MTD ADO 2



For 2, seems very few sellers?

   -

   Pitching pool batch 1 of 1.3K target managed sellers kicked off pitching
   from 28 May; out of which, 103 accepted, 209 rejected, and 1K pending
   responses



Any reason why so many reject?




   -

   83% rejected sellers “want to focus on local businesses” or have
   “manpower limitations”. We suggest to park them for now and repitch next
   year when they are more ready to expand oversea business
   -

   2% “already has MY store” - not to repitch as the SKU supply is already
   available to MY
   -

   14% has concerns on Direct selling ops such as “high admin fee (i.e.
   platform fee)”, “export limitations”, “worried about export success” - RMs
   will repitch this group in Jul after validating limitations, to educate
   them how to hide admin fee in listing price, etc.



For 2 and 3, how are we ensuring that they succeed? Do they partake in
local programs right now?

   -

   Growth package designed to support direct seller in the first 3 months
   with 1) commission fee waivers up to 500 orders per month 2) free ads
   credit with 200RM per shop 3) ads voucher with 50% off, cap at $200 RM /
   month 4) 5 CFS slots per seller per month
   -

   Checking with product team on what marketing tools sellers can use and
   participation process, to prioritise MY spike day LTD and voucher enrolment



Are we making it easy enough for sellers now? For eg. we should just give
more at the start to grow them. As compared to asking for co-fund etc. and
hard to move forward



Probably easier to ask at later stage when proven orders

   -

   Yes, the current growth package does not ask any seller co-investment
   commitment in the first 3 months; it serves to encourage sellers to test
   out various marketing tools in each import market (e.g., ads, CFS)

197c95431dfd9db2.subject: [For Approval] Request to approve CNLS partial fulfillment as Key Project

content.answer:
Hi Jianghong and Ian,

As discussed with Terence during the CB Monthly Meeting on 25 June and with
Bingyu at the CB Workshop on 2 July, we would like to seek your approval to
designate the following initiative as a Key Project for the PM/Dev teams to
prioritize.

Initiative: Enable partial fulfillment for CNLS local shop

   -

   Allow partial fulfillment using CNLS sellers’ available inventory in
   China or nearby local markets.


Rationale:

   -

   CNLS is a key growth pillar for the Shopee platform, with June 2025 ADO
   reaching 3.9 million (+19% YTD).
   -

   Relying on a single source of fulfillment limits potential—when OOS
   (out-of-stock) occurs, CNLS sellers are unable to fulfill orders using
   alternative inventory.
   -

   Enabling partial fulfillment can help recover an estimated 75K ADO by
   reducing OOS impact.


For details, kindly refer to the attachment below.


Thank you.

HI, Ian.
Following up on the item #5.

. Context : Last marketing growth analysis, we shared that overall MS spend
(paid-ad + CPAS + AMS) decreased in PH and VN and ROAS decreased in SG.
Today we're sharing only the deep dive into paid-ads and how we
transitioned to the different paid-ad type, which is GMV Max.

. Conclusion : Take rate is growing with stable YoY, and KR sellers moved
to GMV Max on par with platform level by this June. However, despite having
transitioned to GMV Max, SG MY PH have lower ROAS YoY and here are the
possible reasons.

(Hypothesis 1) KR sellers are investing more in auto bidding than in custom
ROAS within GMV Max, which is the opposite of the trend seen on the
platform and CNCB—where more investment should have gone into custom ROAS
mode.
(Hypothesis 2) KR sellers may have set their custom ROAS targets too
conservatively (i.e., too high).

Both hypotheses require further review of platform-wide and key
sellers-level data. so, our next step is to compare with platform's ROAS of
auto bidding vs custom mode to see if we should push for higher portion of
auto bidding spend only or also push for increasing the ROAS of custom
mode.
Further details can be found in the following three slides (also attached).
thanks!

Hi Ian,

Based on last KR/JP bi-weekly meeting, VN team is looking into the
following the three areas:

1. YoY trend for KR vs. platform at category level

2. KR brand total business view

3. growth angles

Please review to our quick summary answer below (detail analysis kindly
refer to our material attached):

1. VN Platform ADO shows growing trend YoY in both ADO and ADG (+15% and
+26% respectively) while VN KRCB ADO is lagging behind in both metrics.

2. The significant decline is primarily attributed to (1) a decrease in the
makeup category, and (2) underperformance among several key brands.

   - Make-up category decline: Make-up -30% YoY, mainly impacted by 3CE
   drop, while other CATs remain stable.
   - Top brand's underperform: Stronger growth in brands' local retailers
   compared to CB shops among top 10 KR brands while lacking of new brands in
   pipeline

3. From VN market perspective, accelerating growth in the KR business will
be driven by strategic brand expansion, leveraging trend-driven
assortments, and enhancing localization efforts via 3PF and KR LS. These
key levers will enable us to capture market opportunities and strengthen
our competitive position.

Please let me know if you have further questions.

Regards,
Lisa

Think the implication to us is that though SCS isn’t as important as AE
consignment to AE, but SCS is still serving with our strategic values by
building e2e supply chain capabilities; We are prepared and on standby to
scale whenever Shopee needs us, such as entering new markets, especially
those with strong AE/Temu presence. This would allow us to compete
effectively with more controlled products and pricing, and potentially
seller profile and 2. ops blockers:

   sellers take ~50-70% of CN CNLS portfolio ADO), which are reluctant to
   convert as they use WH joint ventures to connect with local

Key blockers are: a) duplicate listing need for 铺货; b) service level; c)
   bulky items.

We will also work with local FBS teams to solve inbound issues and VAS
selections by EoY and target to convert multiplatform fulfilling and seller
own wh sellers as much as possible. The framework will be aligned with CB
FBS and each local market.

Cost advantage:

to convert more items with cost efficiency. We are still assessing whether
the cost efficiency is sustainable in the long run by excluding free

Hi Ian,

Regarding yesterday’s Chris meeting P&L question, pls find below answer (i
sent here for CB review first, and only keep Choice members)

#1 Standalone P&L in MP business session confusion


#2 For choice, why seller P&L is positive, but EBITDA is so negative

reflected in the seller P&L (+0.5mil) in Jun’25, the gap is due to

1) -4.2mil from SLS discount (31% discount on ASF vs other sellers)

2) -0.8mil from MP commission discount (total 4% discount, 2% from Anbo
rate lower than SLS sellers applied to Choice-SLS, another 2% from Dec’24
Chris alignment on mgmt reporting - applied to all Choice)

3) *-**1*.3mil from MP ads discount (50% off, also per Dec’24 Chris
alignment on mgmt reporting)

To further explain the run-down of how Choice mgmt. EBITDA is calculated

Definition clarity:

   -

   EBITDA (per meeting deck) reflects Choice as a business contribution to
   SHP EBITDA (i.e. in Jun, our CB biz has a overall EBITDA of 10mil, with
   choice contributing -6mil, rest of BU contributes +16mil), this includes A)
   Choice own standalone P&L, Choice as a biz contribution to the platform B)
   MP P&L, C) SLS P&L, and D) shared cost allocation, this definition
has been aligned
   with Reg FP&A reporting to Chris
   -

   However, this definition is different from Choice own entity EBITDA (so
   call seller EBITDA), which is used solely to evaluate Choice as a
   seller’s earning/loss when operating its business, the equation shall be
   seller P&L - interest - tax - depreciation - amortization, which is the
   version that Lining showed to G-team Oct’24

content.question:
Please ensure we are speaking the same language.



I would like to match SKUs after vouchers and buyer paid shipping. And we
need to compare it vs KR sellers and Local TW sellers so we know how to
tackle the problem.



@Yuna Kwon <yuna.kwon@shopee.com> on KR side, how are we managing sellers
right now in terms of price negotiation and campaign participation?

content.answer:
@Ian Ho <hoi@sea.com> for price nego with sellers,

   - For exposure items : Normally local teams give our RMs the list of
   specific SKU x quantity x target price (usually lowest price L90D), and RMs
   check with sellers if sellers can give this price and quantity to feedback
   to local teams Yes or No, and usually no room for counter offer. More rare
   case is that local teams give our RMs only the eligible sellers and RMs
   choose the SKU and price negotiated with the seller to suggest back to
   local teams

   - For no exposure items (BAU) : No pre-emptive price negotiation,
   meaning RMs notice drop in sales and price increase then negotiates with
   the seller to drop the price. However, this approach doesn't work well
   because sellers increase the price for their pricing policy and P&L reasons
   so won't "move" price without exposure slots

   - For special occasions : When we adjust down the logistics rate table
   or negotiate with local teams to discount or delay commission increase, we
   use this to negotiate with sellers to reflect the cost saved into their
   price. We monitor the price before and after. The controllability is low,
   since the rate or commission adjustment is not offered to sellers with the
   condition of price discount.

content.question: For exposure items, what is the ADO coverage typically?

And do we track the
success rate?
Do we have a breakdown by market?
And we have to do this separately for all markets?

@Keljin Chua <keljin.chua@shopee.com>, how does this compare with how CN
does this?

content.answer:
Sharing a quick check on CFS specifically first. Can use this to compare
apple to apple with CN when needed.

*For exposure items, what is the ADO coverage typically?*

Based on CFS exposure, for instance, KR-UST sellers's CFS ADO coverage vs.
their total ADO in '25 H1 is 14% (By market: SG 35%, MY 16%, VN 13%, PH,
TH, TW 4% each) If increasing the seller pool to LT, this might be lowered
even further

*And do we track the success rate? Do we have a breakdown by market?*

: Typically, local teams track when needed and we don't track the success
rate after the price is submitted. Only for specific programs where price
match is done as a top-down initiative, local teams track and share the
success rate, such as TTS vs SHP price match of top brands. For now, let us
share a quick review based on June-July cycle of the CFS nomination success
rate of KR SMT by each market (TW VN tbc) :

   -

   [June_success rate] MY 57%, PH 48%, SG 63%, TH 63%
   -

   [July_success rate] MY 51%, PH 36%, SG 34%, TH 79%

content.answer:
Hi Ian, please find my answer to your questions below in blue text.


   - Do we think being in campaigns help? And if so should we put it as
   part of KPIs?

--> as aligned at KR/JP bi weekly meeting yesterday, we'll look at campaign
nomination by benchmarking CN practices

   - It seems like we are having issues with ads take rate. We have it as
   part of input KPIs but is it really helping? For the brands which are not
   doing well, how are the RMs faring? If they are still faring well, it means
   there is an issue with the KPI settings?

--> It's aligned with RM KPI result, meaning because we have UST sellers'
take-rate issue, thus SMT1 RMS had the lowest score from the take-rate
metric. But the target setting logic itself, we may need to revisit as
current logic is a bit conservative. Will work with the MS team to set the
right & bit aggressive target for take-rate. (cc: @Hongkeun Park
<hongkeun.park@shopee.com> )

   - What KPIs do we have to remove for the above 2 to be inserted in? I
   think we can change things up (add and minus) to ensure our KPIs are
   aligned to business

--> We were planning to remove two metrics:  New KRJP origin SKU#, and ADO%
to KRJP origin C2C ADO as not having strong lever for resellers group ; And
additionally can consider removing fulfilment qty which has less
controllability by RM side. So they can focus on what they can really push.

Just to clarify, 'above 2' means by price bidding & price com. which CN RM
has, but not KR RM? I'd like to check with market teams first on how manyKR
SKUs are/can be in the target list per SMT1/2/3 /PSP, in order to make the
right proposal for RM KPI.  We'd like to assign key items that each team
should focus on for our business growth because respective team has
different size/ staged sellers (SMT1 >1k ADO, SMT2 >200 ADO, SMT3 >50, PSP
= NSP sellers)

content.answer:
Hi Ian,

Slide 5

   -

   Seller PC2 here includes fee that we pay to platform? Do we get any
   discount to the fee?
   -

      Yes, seller PC2 includes platform fee (commission, service fee,
      transaction fee, infrastructure fee) as SIP cost (markup in SIP listing
      price)
      -

      We do not get any discount to the fee, we pay the same as a normal MP
      seller
      -

   Why is CN SIP so negative on PC2? What was it previously?


CNSIP:


   -

   Seller PC2
   -

      Jun: Seller PC2 became negative due to reinvesting 5% GMV to counter
      revenue from mgt fee removal (~$160k) and OPEX deterioration (~$40k) and
      incubate new SKUs
      -

      May: slightly positive as our target is breakeven
      -

   MP PC2
   -

      Continue to be worsened MoM from May to Jul due to higher platform
      PRM cost (generic to all sellers, not dedicated PRM to CNSIP)



   -

   Can we also show the MP PC2 and SLS P&L as separate row items?


Local SIP:


   -

   Seller PC2
   -

      Jul is on track to target PC2 (-3% for MYSG while breakeven for other
      lanes)
      -

      Jun and May are higher than target due to under-spending of the
      budget because actual budget is higher than projected
      -

         Jun: one-time passback from ID on fraud case claw back (~$400k)
         -

         May: higher than expected passback from export markets from
         regional BPSF reduction initiative
         -

   MP PC2
   -

      Jul proj vs Jun: maintain at similar level as Jun
      -

      Jun vs May: MoM worsened due to higher platform PRM cost (generic to
      all sellers, not dedicated to local SIP)
      -

   SLS PC2
   -

      Jul proj vs Jun: MoM worsened due to transitioning to new vendors in
      Jul and will be adjusting ASF on 22 Jul for 14 lanes
      -

      Jun vs May: MoM improved due to ADO growth


Slide 6

   -

Hi Ian,

Noted. We will assess overall ROI, taking into account topline growth

Hi Ian,

Please find the updates on the follow-up actions in the attachment. In
summary,

1. SIP UE

   - Local SIP UE remains healthy at $0.69 for Q2-25 despite fraud loss in
   Apr
   - CNSIP UE $0.56 in Jun, half of Apr/May due to PC2 reinvestment to
   counter management fee removal, but still higher than CNCB ($0.46)
   - Key reasons for higher CNSIP UE vs CNCB MP are 1) higher MP PC2 due to
   higher ads takerate; 2) higher SLS PnL due to higher mix of parcels to
   Latam lanes with higher PPO (same SLS rate card)

2. CNSIP ramp up

   - Propose to migrate new SKUs acquired earliest possible to generate
   organic topline and marketing budget to fund more SKU incubation; no CB
   ops/listing BPM concerns
   - Incubate new SKUs the same approach as local SIP, use budget from 1)
   new seller commission waiver; 2) new SKU organic orders; 3) import market
   seller programs
   - Project 28.3k ADO from new SKUs by Dec


3. Chatbot rollout (DR/CSAT trade-off)

   - Higher Chatbot CSAT from new chatbot leads to higher shop CSAT and
   higher order conversion, however staff cost of incremental orders converted
   is high at $7-$27 per order
   - However overall impact to SIP EBITDA is manageable as total staff cost
   is small compared to the total SIP profit
   - Propose to target 52% chatbot CSAT + 56% chatbot Deflection, to reach
   66% shop CSAT (+11pp vs now), with minimal negative impact to overall SIP
   economics

content.answer:
Hi All,



Please find the weekly Counter Taobao project update as of 18th July below.


Key Highlights



   -

   Overall:
   -

      Program Targets Adjustment: All targets have been adjusted to the
      latest iteration to achieve 226K by Dec’25, WIP to breakdown targets to
      monthly pacing by 25 Jul.
      -

      ADO/ADG Target Overachieve: MTD ADO MoM +36% to 68K (102% RR), ADG
      MoM +15% to  $734K (99% RR) driven by Fashion
      -

      SKU Target Underachieve: SKU# at 1,034K (86% RR) due to F&B & Beauty
      below target due to Lever 3 delay in onboarding process.



   -

   Fashion:
   -

      ADO/ADG Target Overachieve:  ADO 46K (108% RR), ADG $482K (102% RR)
      driven by growth in Lever 1: Mosequeen July ADO +46% MoM to 1.2K
in TH, and
      +76% to 113 in VN. Ongoing support provided to position the seller as a
      success case for others. & Lever 3: SKU# increase 141% MoM to
26K; RR 166%
      as more sellers started listings. As a result, ADO +300% MoM to 76. Top
      GGP, ITCHICK (Women’s Bags), saw ADO +172% MoM to 49 across all sites
      -

      SKU Target Overachieve: SKU# at 743k (101% RR). Lever 3 RR 166%
      catching up after sellers started listing. Expected to increase to 2M by
      Aug after mass pitching commence.
      -

      Program PC2: -1.80% driven by re-investment in program sellers from
      MY(-1%) TH (-2%) and PH (-6%)
      -


      -

   Furniture:
   -

      ADO/ADG Target Overachieve: ADO 2.4k (106% RR), ADG $105K (108% RR)
      -

      SKU Target Underachieve: SKU# at 263k (96% RR) , impacted by
      operational issues from top seller 润泽优 in MY/TH—SKU delisting and upload
      quota limits. WIP to fix by next week
      -

      Program PC2: 7.82% due to increase in take-rate in SG/MY following
      commission fee changes. WIP to discuss reinvestment plans
      -

   F&B
   -

      ADO/ADG Target Underachieve : AD

content.answer:
Develop price comp definition (ETA: 30th July) - Initial findings is
   that the listing price gap is big and we will have to compare price comp
   using the final buyer paid price. (Include BPSF + Tax).  An example of
   comparison illustrated below

Confirm TB Order UE (ETA: 25th July) - To identify if TBG is making a
   loss per order when compared to SHP. Findings will be used as an input to
   determine what is the rebate % we can give to seller based on the item
   Selling Price/Weight/Category

We will increase the sample size of SKU to analyse the price structure so
that we can better gauge the additional seller margin we can squeeze SHP CB
seller to reduce prior providing item rebate to match TB final listing
price.


Others

   -

   Where did we land on PH joining the program for Fashion and Beauty

We are in discussion with PH to enable

Incentive packages for newly acquired TB sellers mainly to support Lever
   1 growth.

Incentives for SKU sourcing for Beauty products. (Fashion SKU Sourcing
   is already available) to support Lever 2 growth

Incentive package for ERP migrated sellers

Will conclude by early next week.

   -

   Where did we land on focusing on Fashion and Beauty if we need to? And
   how does the program look like per market right now? Is it streamline or
   very complicated?

On focusing, yes we have aligned not to expand F&B and Furniture further
into other countries and to stay focused on Fashion & Beauty

   -

   For incentives: For SG/MY/TH where we have been pushing F&B & Furniture,
   we propose to keep the current program as-is, in consideration of seller
   sentiment as sellers were recently pitched and not scale up the program. We
   also assess that the budget supporting F&B/Furniture push is relatively
   small, and reallocating it to Fashion & Beauty would bring limited impact.
   -

   In terms of new seller acquisition efforts, we have also reallocated SAI
   headcount to focus on Fashion & Beauty acquisition.

Currently the incentive program is mostly streamlined across markets for
both seller commitment and platform commitment with local CB team’s buy-in.

Improvement will be needed to increase the SKU commitment per seller in
view of the new targets. We will work with CN SMT and the local CB team to
confirm this by next week.

Best Regards,

Max Goh

content.question:
Thanks



Slide 6

   - What is the YTD growth rate for KR and JP so far?

And how does it
   compare vs platform?
Is it getting better/worse?
- Do we have the topline breakdown by different countries?

Slide 8

   - #2 – Why is this not solved yet?
When will it be solved?
- #4 -  Can I see the LFF numbers for each market?

And which market is
   meeting/missing targets?

Slide 10 – 15

   - It is quite clear that direct sales is better than spending on ads
   right?
How much are we spending/month on ads right now?

Shall we reduce
   that drastically and put that into hiring of BPOs to do acquisition?
- How much shall we reduce?
How many should we hire?

Slide 17

   - What is the ADO of JP broken down by various markets right now?

Slide 19

   - Is the same new seller program as KR?

How is it working compared to KR
   new sellers?
- Any feedback on the program in general?

Slide 22

   - Is it easy to get JP sellers to migrate these SKUs?

Slide 23

   - On the licenses issue, if we do not face it on KR sellers front, we
   should not face it from JP sellers right?

Slide 27 – 29

   - What is the ADO impact from paid ads vs ams right now?

Which one
   should we get sellers to adopt first?

Or are we confident in pushing both
   at the same time?

Slide 32

   - So sellers who complete in 1st month would get 4 months free comms
   waiver?
- Have we sense check this with the markets yet?

Slide 34

   - Are we sure these are the main levers to get orders?

- It should be
      - Full assortment
      - Competitive pricing
      - Participating in our campaigns
      - Ideally starting paid ads?

Slide 35

   - What is the situation of the sales team right now. Are they FTEs or
   BPOs?

Slide 36- 37

   - Is this worth while to do?

Most of ADO did not come from
   partners/non-partners?
We should just focus on our outbound sales efforts?

Slide 39

   - For VN, is there a possibility of going through FBS?

Or it is purely
   3PF?

- I remember we were doing a bunch of things to incentivize sellers to
   register their SKUs and go into 3PF, how is that coming along?

Others

   - If we see an issue in top sellers doing paid ads, what exactly are we
   doing to encourage them to top up again?

And do we have a sense on which
   sellers and which markets are the main issues?

19853cb5c042c7f8.subject: [Approval Needed] JPCB P1 Program Commission Discount Request (Aug–Dec 2025)

content.answer:
Hi Ian,

I am writing to seek your approval for the JPCB P1 Program commission
discount request for the August–December 2025 cycles, as recapped during
the biweekly yesterday. Due to time constraints, we’ve already secured
approval from Yuna in advance — please find a snapshot of her approval
email attached for your reference.

*Background & Business Objective:*
As shared previously, this program is designed to accelerate new seller
onboarding and ADO by offering a 3% commission discount (excluding PH and
TH) to sellers acquired through our key JP acquisition partners (seller
communities). These sellers have historically outperformed the general new
seller base, with 2–3x higher ADO per GP.

*Commission Waiver Details:*
a) Date: *1st Aug – 31st Dec 2025 (5 months)*
b) Commission discount %: 3% discount of the MP commission
c) Eligible shops: 952 shops (subject to updates)

*KPI Targets :*
- Expected ADO: 1,720 ADO by Dec 2025 (+198% vs June baseline)
- Expected ADGMV: 121K USD by Dec 2025 (+202% vs June baseline)
- Estimated Foregone Revenue: ~15K USD/month initially, rising to ~30K
USD/month by Dec, as more sellers begin generating orders

To support the review, please find the attached P&L assessment table
provided by CBBI. The table reflects the performance of the updated August
cycle shop list.
[image: image.png]
-- 

*Huang Xiao Lin*

Shopee | Cross Border | JPCB SA & ICB

*Mobile:* +82 010-9663-1316

*Address:* 16F, Centerfield West Tower, 231, Teheran-ro, Gangnam-gu, Seoul,
Republic of Korea

content.question: Can remind me what is this about again?
As new sellers, their commissions is already 0% right?

content.answer:
Hi Ian,

Yes, you're right — new sellers enjoy a 0% commission for their first 3
months as part of the standard onboarding benefit.

However, the JP P1 Program is different and does not target new sellers.

As Haenle mentioned during the meeting yesterday, the P1 Program targets
existing resellers from top-performing seller communities. Under this
program, Shopee offers a 3% commission discount, and in return, sellers are
expected to reinvest in paid ads and work toward specific ADO targets set
by us, with support from the communities (e.g., education materials,
listing tools, etc.).

The program is starting from 8/1, and the seller communities have expressed
strong confidence in the scheme and their ability to meet the assigned
seller count and ADO targets. We're now following up to seek your official
approval to engage with CBBI for the commission discount setup.

Please let me know if you have further questions.

below.

   -

      Topline: ADO 61K (91% RR), MoM +24%, ADG $653K (89% RR), MoM +13%,
      mostly caused by Beauty underperformance.
      -

      SKU#: SKU# at 1,051K (99% RR) due to Lever 3 (ERP) delay in
      onboarding process


   -

   Fashion:
   -

      Topline: ADO 41K (97% RR), ADG $423K (90% RR), ADO% to CB 4.4% (MoM
      +0.1pp). Low RR impacted by underperformance of Lever 2 in PH,
driven by a
      decline in top sellers' performance following a scale-back in June

      up as onboarded sellers begin listing. SKU# is expected to reach
2M in Aug
      through Lever 3 seller mass pitching

      Program PC2: -1.5% driven by re-investment in program sellers from
      MY(-0.7%) TH (-1.9%) and PH (-4.9%)



   -

      Program PC2: 7.4%

      Topline: ADO 1.0K (99% RR), ADG $7.9K (93% RR), ADO% to CB at 1.6%

Hi Ian,

Please find responses in blue below

Slide 9

   -

   Performance is not very good for such a key cat?

Performance in July was below expectations mainly driven by challenges in
TH and VN, both related to top seller:

   -

   TH: Underperformance was largely due to top seller Judydoll (ADO MoM
   -13%), with a 20% drop in ad spending following an operational staff
   change. Performance is expected to recover in August as operations resume.
   -

   VN: Driven by underperformance of XIXI (ADO MoM -18%). New distributors
   entered the market in July with a low-price strategy, disrupting pricing.
   The brand has since intervened to stabilize prices, and improvement is
   expected in August.

August Action Plan to Strengthen Beauty Program Performance:

   1.

   Expand the seller pool – We aim to onboard 74 additional shops (~11K ADO)
   starting in Aug, with a focus on high-potential beauty brands and some
   resellers. This diversification will help reduce reliance on individual
   brand sellers and mitigate performance risks when certain brands
   underperform.
   2.

   Closely monitor top sellers – Ensure timely identification of any
   operational issues especially during campaign periods.
   3.

   Upsize incentives for focus shops:
   -

      SG/MY: Incentive plan confirmed and rollout. (Double down on paid ads
      and rebates%)
      -

      TH/VN: Local teams to finalize incentive mechanisms by end of week.
      -

      PH: Progress slower due to non-participation in the previous phase.
      Regional PMO is in discussions with PH BD Head to improve
incentives which
      will help to attract more beauty sellers.

Despite July challenges, sellers in the Counter TB Beauty program
outperformed the overall Beauty category, with ADO growing +3% MoM vs -6%
for the category.


Slide 14

   -

   Interest amongst Beauty sellers is so low?

Lever 3 (ERP) has not ramped up yet, as Ju

content.question: *Expand the seller pool* – We aim to onboard *74 additional shops
*Closely monitor top sellers* – Ensure timely identification of any
*Upsize incentives for focus shops:*

content.answer:
JST's reluctance to mass collaborate directly, as they are concerned
   about reactions of other e-commerce partners, especially Taobao.

WDT’s limited mass-scale support, offering us only one livestream
   slot/mth. Last month's livestream gave us 11 converted sellers only.
   Nonetheless, this livestream will still be held.

Instead of mass pitching, we will focus on 1:1 outreach to WDT’s top 500
sellers, starting from today 11th Aug, together with internal Shopee
publicity channels and a PR article release. With this approach, we expect
to convert 392 additional sellers, which is significantly below our
original target of 7,763 sellers for August. However, each of these sellers
is expected to have a larger catalog than our original target (200 SKUs per
seller). We will also build a VIP onboarding channel for high-potential
sellers and evaluate the number of SKUs they can bring to Shopee. Detailed
timeline and plan below.

Slide 16

   -

   What exactly are we matching for here? Top TB vs Shopee? Or Top Shopee
   vs TB? Or something else?
   -

   Do not understand the color coding, why is negative green? Negative is
   supposed to be bad right?
   -

   There has not been much improvement wow?



We are currently matching the top TB items (1.8K SKUs) against Shopee CB
Shop to ensure that the most sold products on TBG are price competitive on
our platform.



The green color coding indicates categories that have shown WoW improvement
since we began co-sharing rebates with sellers on July 24. Going forward,
we will include an RR column in all future tables and apply the color
coding there instead.


For the 1.8K SKUs we are focusing on to improve price competitiveness in
July, we asked sellers to lower their listing prices by approximately 6% (a
tentative target, while the actual discount is being finalized using the BI
model). However, this reduction does not necessarily make all targeted SKUs
cheaper than TB in terms of total buyer-paid price. Some SKUs may still
remain more expensive overall if the total price gap exceeds 6%.

We aim to resolve thi

Hi Ian,

Lever 3 (ERP) has not ramped up yet, as July's seller outreach primarily
targeted traders through offline events where beauty sellers are not the
focus audience. Once mass pitching begins in August (Pending CN’s
confirmation), we expect Beauty to pick up, as it accounts for
approximately 40% of the ERP seller pool.

When exactly will we be able to start mass pitching?

Just received the latest update from ERP team. Mass pitching will not be
the focus of this month due to:

content.question: JST's reluctance to mass collaborate directly, as they are concerned

content.answer:
JST's reluctance to mass collaborate directly, as they are concerned
   about reactions of other e-commerce partners, especially Taobao.

What changed? I thought they were ok to send messages to all sellers for us?

JST rejected the proposal, citing concerns that supporting our platform
could be perceived as favouring us. They fear that this may compromise
their platform neutrality and hinder broader business development efforts.
Alternatively, they agreed to provide us with seller leads from their city
managers, but the amount is small with just 40 sellers shared this month.
MKT team will be visiting these sellers 1:1 to pitch over this and next
month.

WDT agreed to a one-time push notification to its entire base, which MKT
team is preparing for now and aligning on format and ETA.

WDT’s limited mass-scale support, offering us only one livestream
   slot/mth. Last month's livestream gave us 11 converted sellers only.
   Nonetheless, this livestream will still be held.

How come so bad? How many viewers? What was the issue for the poor
conversion?

Please find conversion funnel for last month’s livestream below:

Conversion Stages

Stage 1:

Unique Seller Who Joined Live Stream

Stage 2:

Sellers Who Expressed Interest

Stage 3:

Seller Who Proceed With Onboarding

Stage 4:

Seller Who Completed Listing

#Sellers

1,775

44

11

2

Conversion %

100.0%

2.5%

0.6%

0.1%

Stage 1 > Stage 2:  Working with CN Marketing team to confirm the reason
for low conversion and generate more unique view - ETA: 15th Aug

Stage 2 > Stage 3: CN Marketing team has observed that sellers typically
require some time to reflect and internally align within their
organizations before deciding whether to proceed with onboarding.
Additionally, we have onboarded a new BPO team member to assist with seller
inquiries and to consistently follow up with reminders to prompt action on
a daily basis

Stage 3 > Stage 4: The main drop‑off stems from sellers not taking action
to list their SKUs, despite us successfully completing their onboarding
authorizations. We are working with the BPO team to follow up with these
sellers daily to prompt them to take action.


Instead of mass pitching, we will focus on 1:1 outreach to WDT’s top 500
sellers, starting from today 11th Aug, together with internal Shopee
publicity channels and a PR article release. With this approach, we expect
to convert 392 additional sellers, which is significantly below our
original target of 7,763 sellers for August. However, each of these sellers
is expected to have a larger catalog than our original target (200 SKUs per
seller). We will also build a VIP onboarding channel for high-potential
sellers and evaluate the number of SKUs they can

One-time push notification to all of WDT sellers that will happen this
   month. MKT team preparing the message and aligning on format + ETA with WDT

SAI aligned to start 1:1 pitching of 500 WDT sellers, and pitching has
   begun as of 13 Aug

++ @Ivy Chen <ivy.chen01@shopee.com> @Gerold Wang <gerold.wang@shopee.com>

Across 10 markets, we are at MTD 1K ADO. By EOM with the latest plan, we
estimate potentially landing at 2.6K ADO vs 10.2K ADO target.
I cannot agree current evaluation of 2.6k ado, we have too many
uncertainties, we are also too conservative and not deep enough to own each
channel's leads experience. @Nicholas Lim <nicholas.lim@shopee.com> and @Ivy
Chen <ivy.chen01@shopee.com> ,we need look at each step closely and improve
mass pitching channel. @Nicholas Lim <nicholas.lim@shopee.com>, for 1:1
pitching, we also need to be involved in the wording and process design
with Derek team, make sure the efficiency.

*Ian and Jianghong*
*Call out a help:* even we stretch above, don't think we can achieve 10k
ado target due to slowness of 聚水潭, Ivy and I had a few rounds catchup. Our
conclusion is:
JST is 暧昧 on commercial side (tech side is ok), they agreed on most of the
things to us, such as city level information, help us push etc, but just
very slow and much smaller than our expectation
As JST is 5X bigger seller pool of WDT, it is key for our success, see if
you can help push them?

Meanwhile, I heard marketing team and RM team are stretched on manual
resources, @Ivy Chen <ivy.chen01@shopee.com> and @Cheng Derek
<derek.cheng@shopee.com> you want to call out help? In general, I still
feel we are slow..

Thanks

Hi Shuning,

Thanks to Shuning's insights.

1) JST: So far our topline ADO target is on track. And we know that
leveraging JST sales is currently the most effective way to reach and
acquire key JST sellers. JST sales helps Shopee build trust with sellers
and will recommend highly motivated sellers to Shopee. Compared to
large-scale placement of JST internal banners or gaining exposure, JST
sales deliver better results and higher conversion rates. JST will help
recommend and visit key decision-makers among sellers, while those who see
the banners are usually just the operators of Taobao stores.

Therefore, JST has already selected three key cities for us that match the
Count taobao project seller cluster: Hangzhou, Shenzhen, and Foshan. Next
week, MKT will work with Cluster Sai to visit the sales leaders in these
cities one by one, and encourage the sales leaders to hold events or refer
sellers to Shopee. Three more key cities will be added next week.

2) WDT: WDT will promote the Swarm project to all WDT sellers this month
via pop-up messages in its ERP system.

And in August it is estimated that about 1,400 WDT seller leads will be
obtained.

Thanks a lot.

B.R

Ivy

Slide 6

   - CPO of Anbo is so much higher than NNWH?
Are we sure about this?

- For TH, why is Anbo only 0.4 days faster than SLS while for VN, it is
   3.5 days?
- #2 – For TH, all CB orders can be fulfilled locally?

That is quite
   different from previous understanding?

Slide 9

   - For both VN and TH
      - What is before/after?
For commissions and SLS discount?
- How does it compare with SLS?
- How does it compare with Anbo WH?

Others

   - What is the utilization rate for NNWH and Anbo right now?

How will the
   changes affect these WH?
- When will we announce the changes?
And when will it take effect?

- For VN/TH, we should announce it concurrently with 3PF/Local WH
   benefits to soften the blow?

content.answer:
NNWH has a cheaper rental and costs 0.4x of Anbo for each square meter
because 1) Anbo is closer to big cities and with higher demand; 2)
Warehouse market in Nanning is over supplied last year, so the price is good

NNWH has a lower salary rate and costs 0.81x of Anbo, but with similar
productivity

NNWH has slightly lower consumable cost: NNWH re-use some consumable
from Lang Hua WH, eg: the carton box at 7 RMB each



   -

   For TH, why is Anbo only 0.4 days faster than SLS while for VN, it is
   3.5 days?

Sorry there was a BI data issue for SLS to VN BWT data. Please find below
the updated table

For VN, SLS BWT is 4.6 days. NNWH is around 1 day faster vs SLS while Anbo
is 0.7 day faster vs SLS, both are quite similar to each other.


   -

   #2 – For TH, all CB orders can be fulfilled locally? That is quite
   different from previous understanding?

There will still be CB orders that cannot be fulfilled locally with FBS,
e.g., prohibited/ license categories for WH, Made to order/ customizable
SKUs etc. We will push these SKUs using 3PFs.

The update from previous understanding is

   -

   Recently there’s spike in GR risk as the minister of industry is
   specifically targeting Chinese owned inventory after the collapse building
   case from May earthquake
   -

   Given this, TH’s decision based on Legal/ Risk/ Government Relation team
   advice is to adopt stricter requirements for CB FBS vs 3PF since stocks is
   stored in Shopee’s WH
   -

   In the long run, the plan is to have government API sync with TH Shopee
   and license constraints expected to be at listing level, impacting all
   fulfillment channels. However, the API sync timeline still uncertain and
   pending government confirmation

Slide 9

   -

   For both VN and TH
   -

      What is before/after? For commissions and SLS discount?
      -

      How does it compare with SLS? Sellers?
      -

      How does it compare with Anbo WH?

Please find below table for assumption details, in short,

   -

   NNWH Commission: same as Anbo sellers, lower than SLS sellers
   -

   NN SLS discount: remove existing discount by 24/46% margin for VN/TH
   respectively, post adjustment margin same as normal SLS sellers

@Jianghong Liu <jianghong.liu@shopee.com> @Ning Li <ning.li@shopee.com> when
does the lease for NNWH end? I wonder what is the long term view of this WH
given

   - Utilization should go down quite a bit with this change
   - We have space in Anbo WH
   - BWT difference between NN and Anbo is not huge (even though it is
   indeed cheaper)

NNWH leasing contract will end by 24th Nov, 2027, >2 years from now;
However, NNWH cost to terminate the contract earlier isn't very high and
much lower than AnboWH:

   - the penalty cost for NNWH early termination is 3 months of rental
   (~200K USD) vs.
   - that of Anbo WH is to fully pay 3 years' rental (~11mil USD)


*Best regards,*

*LI Ning*
SCS

content.answer:
we switched to automatic price-follow strategy to gain more traffic by
   being the cheapest after PID boosting removal and

we increased seller rebate on high ASP assortments to push sales of high
   ASP products.

We will closely monitor and balance UE and ADO, in order to maximize seller
profit

   -

   So boosting has been removed 100%? We are now treated similarly to every
   seller?

Yes



Slide 9

   -

   What is the guideline that we are setting for ourselves in PO listing %?


Overall PO listing % cannot exceed 10%, need to balance the pacing between
new listing and delisting.


PO, seen as resource, will be prioritized and allocated more to the
categories which see higher convert-to-VMI %



   -

   What is #5 about? I understand we recently tightened the DTS rules for
   CN CB sellers, how will this affect our JIT operations?


From 8/18, DTS for non PO orders will shorten from 2 days to 1 day, and
does not impact PO orders. We evaluate NFR/LSR impact on our business in
page 13 - basically no impact to NFR/LSR



Slide 11

   -

   A bit hard for me to understand right now
   -

      How many models do we have in total?
      -

      How many will be JIT?
      -

      How many will be PO? (Assuming not all JIT will be put in PO)
      -

      How many will be VMI?
      -

      And how do we see this evolving?
      -

      Maybe can split by existing and new listings to get a clear view



Model status is summarized as below:

   -

   We have 383k models in total
   -

   14% are JIT and 86% are VMI
   -

   Model PO% is 15% in total (JIT PO% is 14; besides JIT, we also have PO
   set for OOS models)
   -

   Listing PO% is 10% in total (JIT PO % is 7% )


   -

   Can remind me on what is the criteria for us to go from JIT >> VMI? 1
   ADO? What is the success rate of that happening right now?


Current criteria: L7D adi>=0.35 which aligns with the warehouse standard

Future criteria: to set up by L2 sales criteria to better balance
short-tail and long-tail categories
spu coverage leadtime as well as

our big volume of seller nominations which platform may not have same
product



Slide 19

   -

   Why do we look at Top 20 Grocery Store CB sellers here as comparison?

Grocery store here meaning stores selling assortments from all categories,
rather than conventional terms of FMCG.

They are like-a-like reference as our SCS shops, which sell assortments
from all categories..




Slide 20

   -

   For the 2688 not from pool, how will we be selecting these products?


The assortment planning team has prepared a CB by L2 high GMV efficiency
assortments details for models >=20RMB. Based on these information, buyers
will provide guidance regarding high-performing high-priced assortment to
sellers in seller nominations guide.


FMCG example




   -

   I do not understand the % very well in this table, not sure what they
   add up to



The denominator is the total new models target


Slide 21

   -

   Is this cumulative numbers? Or monthly?


We have both monthly and cumulative numbers in this chart



   -

   We listed way more products in July compared to past months? Because of
   JIT?



Yes, because we open up seller nominations via JIT


Slide 23

   -

   If UST sellers ASP is similar to us, why are they able to be more
   profitable than us?

Not necessary the same product; overall SCS assortment is more
   short-tail and UST is more long-tail with higher markup. Therefore SCS
   assortment has higher price competitiveness

Shopee operating model (铺货 multiple duplications) spend much less ads
   than us. (<1% vs ~7% revenue in fully loaded view)

Lower fix costs (manpower, warehouse), specially USTs are selling via
   many other channels, besides Shopee, to dilute their fixed cost.






Slide 27

   -

   For sellers like that, we will move them back to VMI?


We will continue to ask them to nominate more skus through JIT (if they do
not have models in sourcing pool)
For skus meet the sales threshold: convert to VMI
For skus not performing well: delist

In addition, for sellers who are only willing to do VMI, we will offer
them quick-in quick out model (快进快出VMI)

They could also choose to ship directly to SOC once the feature is ready
(expected in Nov)



Slide 28

   -

   Where would such sellers be selling normally? And what is their business
   model?


These sellers are usually factories / factory-trader, we usually have
multiple sourcing method:

   -

   竞品供应商挖掘
   -

   1688供应商挖掘
   -

   淘天大麦挖掘
   -

   产业带扫街


For this specific seller, itis an eyelashes factory with an annual value of
50 million RMB. About 80% of the products are exported to US and EU (B2B
model), and 20% to mainland China. The majority are OEM.

Starting from this year, the supplier started to run its own operations
(B2C model), and established a team of 7-8 ppl in Shenzhen. Currently,

   -

   self-owned Shopee shop (SEA): > 1000 ado
   -

   Tiktok SEA shop: 200 orders
   -

   SCS model: just started one month ago: > 20 orders



Slide 29

   -

   We believe that one source of JIT suppliers are our CB sellers, why do
   we think so?


Suppliers who are potentially doing good in JIT are those with capabilities:

   -

   盘货能力
   -

   上新能力
   -

   小单发货能力

Half of successful SCS JIT sellers are CB MP sellers.



Slide 35

   -

   So what are next steps for #2? Is it worth doing?


   -

   To add these models into buybox, S&R asks CB SCS to provide manpower
   capacity for precision annotation
   -

   We estimate that 120 mandays will be needed to complete the annotation
   for all 12K models
   -

   Considering manpower constraints, we will prioritize those models that
   can be buybox cheapest (~1K models)

Slide 42

   -

   Think we shall find other ways to sell high ASP products. For Miniso,
   what do we expect their ASP to be?
   -

   Current Miniso CB shop’s ASP is ~8-10USD, mainly 潮玩



Slide 46

   -

   We can set up local WH in VN? How will we do that? Or are we referring
   to NN WH?

Local WH指在VN Shopee官方仓，非NNWH。我们需
从大盘中选择市场验证过的老爆品，联名是通过Annita来重新包装这些爆品，以实现销量和品牌宣传的平衡

联合设计师按照Anitta的要求定制新品，强调环保概念，面料全部用环保材料，销量一般，以品宣为主



Slide 62

   -

   How do they use old products and push? Do they change the label to her
   name? But use same product? Or make tweaks to the product?t?
   -

   Did the celebrity had any input to the design? Or she just land her name
   to the campaign/label?

For a

   -

   Anitta will pick the products but will not be involved in the design
   process
   -

   They just keep the original listing but refresh the titles and images,
   e.g., adding “SheinX x Annita’s edit” on the PDP image.
   -

   For the product design, no significant change, but some minor upgrades
   on materials for better quality


For b

   -

   Anitta will input her ideas and preferred design elements into the
   designs, and based on these, designers will launch new assortments based on
   her input



Slide 64

   -

   Will there be commissions sharing with them?


   -

   Still in discussion. Revenue sharing will be an option for the deal
   structure.



Slide 65

   -

   I still struggle to understand how this is different from our usual KOL
   collaboration?

Key differences vs usual KOL collaboration: 1) Higher KOL tier, 2) More KOL
participation 3) More Lovito investment (Can refer to the table below)




Slide 75

   -

   Our ABS seem to be dropping even though we want to push higher ASP
   products?

Overall ABS dropped mainly in BR and MY, the ABS is increasing in all other
major markets

- MY：MY ABS MoM -1.5% due to category mix, that we published more FACC
listings this month to boost orders and PC3, but the ABS is lower than
apparels; if we exclude those new FACC listings, the ABS is increasing;

- BR：BR ABS MoM -18% due to 3 main reasons

   -

   category mix with more FACC listings, same as above;
   -

   seasonal change from winter to spring/summer;
   -

   BR local’s PRM overspending to further boost local orders;

Smaller addressable product base: that only products with ADI<0.1 and
   seller ADO>5 are prioritized to pitch for FBS conversion, the rest are
   targeted for LFF

Delayed target setting: Anbo FBS KPI is only managed to add to SMT KPI
   1wks ago due to MT team restructured

And DTS to 1 day will take effect only after 18th Aug; it will take some
   time for sellers to feel the pain and to consider FBS services.

Besides working with CB team, our own FBS adoption initiative is to
consider launching a free trial program, which probably will launch in Sep
or Oct, after our overall P&L is more stabilized.



New callout: Paid Ads Bug causing Lovito ads expense loss


   -

   On Aug 7, an ad bid fallback issue led to a sudden surge in ad spending,
   estimated loss of 5.9K according to lovito preliminary estimation
   -

   Pending CB market feedback confirmed loss expense according to CB loss
   calculation method, but CB market will not cover the Lovito loss as they
   will only cover the MP seller loss for goodwill

Slides are ok.



Slide 98 (Now removed)

   - Do not quite understand this, we are saying that our CB sellers still
   have margins that we can further reduce?

This view compares the breakdown of a like-for-like SKU uploaded by the SHP
TB sellers on SHP and TB.

Yes, within the 24K expensive SKU pool, 16K SKUs have an average 30% seller
margin higher than TB that can still be optimized. We're already in price
negotiations, and so far, 350 SKUs have agreed to price reductions purely
based on seller margin. SHP will also support with additional item-level
rebates to further offset the discount for sellers

Removed this slide to improve story flow



Do we know why CB sellers still have so much margins?

I thought we squeeze
them quite  a bit already

Hi Ian, Jianghong
