let me know if any questions.



Slide 41 (Now Slide 44)

   - Think we need to talk about management of blackstock before going into
   why we need PO listing

Added new page 43 regarding the blackstock risk if all using VMI model,
instead of JIT+PO.

To summarize, due to the higher uncertainty of seller self-nominated models
(50% are very new to Shopee), if all using VMI, the BS% of these models
will surge to 84%, the total SCS BS will increase by 9.7 p.p. Then we would
need to return the stocks to sellers, which incurs extra costs for
shipping, impacting suppliers’ willingness to nominate more.

Slide 43  (Now Slide 46)

   - Think we will just make it clear NNWH will not be part of LFF% and
   that is the new direction moving forward

Ok, revised in the slide with red font highlight



Slide 80/83  (Now Slide 75/78)

   - We still talking about NNWH here?

Updated the materials to exclude NNWH from LFF/ FBS ADO for all slides and
moving forward



Others  (Now Slide 81-87)

   - Where is the whole part on what we are doing regarding the treatment
   with NN WH?

Yes, added back NNWH treatment with aligned direction



Slide 97  (Now removed)

   - Cannot quite understand this, are we comparing TB SKUs with CB
   sellers/CB sellers on TB program/Shopee Sellers in general?
- Also do not understand what it means from 1.6m SKUs >> 400k >> 48K?

We are comparing TB SKUs against all CB sellers (both on/not on TB program)
on Shopee.

   - We targeted top 400K TB SKUs to match (from top TB shops in our focus
   TB categories)
   - These 400k are matched to 1.6M SHP CB SKUs, with a 4:1 ratio, as it
   includes SHP SKUs with multiple matches to a single TB SKU.
   - Out of the 1.6M SHP CB SKUs, we prioritize 48K for price comp action,
   after filtering out non-performing SKUs (defined as stock <0, rating <4,
   sold count <10), in line with Reg BD price comp prioritization logic.
   - This 48K prioritized SKUs contribute 2.6% of CB ADO, compared to 2.7%
   from the full 1.6M SKUs

Removed this slide to improve story flow



Slide 98 (Now removed)

   - Do not quite understand this, we are saying that our CB sellers still
   have margins that we can further reduce?

This view compares the breakdown of a like-for-like SKU uploaded by the SHP
TB sellers on SHP and TB.

Yes, within the 24K expensive SKU pool, 16K SKUs have an average 30% seller
margin higher than TB that can still be optimized. We're already in price
negotiations, and so far, 350 SKUs have agreed to price reductions purely
based on seller margin. SHP will also support with additional item-level
rebates to further offset the discount for sellers

Removed this slide to improve story flow



Slide 107 (Now Slide 101)

   - I think it is easier to do an apple to apple comparison here in term
   of timeline..

Noted. Will align to Dec-25 for before/after.

   - For Latam, this is wrong right?

content.question:
Thanks.



Slide 65

   - Our match rate seem to have increase by quite a bit over time (17%
   from Apr >> July), do we know what caused this?

Were we really able to
   source more SKUs?
Or were we just better over matchin?

Slide 69

   - In our NPC, do we compare TB vs entire Shopee?
Or against CB?

- Do we have a sense why price competitiveness is so bad for Furniture
   and F&B in particular?
- After we reduce SLS mark up to 5% profits, how would the NPC look?

content.answer:
Hi Ian,

Slide 65

Our match rate seem to have increase by quite a bit over time (17% from Apr
SKUs? Or were we just better over matchin?

The increase in match rate was driven by improved sourcing efforts. Between
Apr and Jul, we sourced approximately 37k SKUs directly through sellers,
contributing an estimated +9% uplift in match rate. The remaining of the
match rate growth was still driven organically by sellers' own listing. The
matching methodology remains consistent.


Slide 69

In our NPC, do we compare TB vs entire Shopee? Or against CB?

We are currently comparing TB against all CB sellers, since our push
efforts are targeted at CB RMs/sellers. We are also adding another view to
include local sellers to have a complete view of Shopee vs TB NPC.

Do we have a sense why price competitiveness is so bad for Furniture and
F&B in particular?

Not quite sure what is causing the NPC issue for F&B, especially in SG and
MY.

Overall, for Furniture & F&B, our price competitiveness is lower mainly
because:

   -

   Sellers’ COGS and seller margins on SHP are ~1.2x-1.5x higher compared
   to TB (50% higher than Fashion/Beauty)
   -

   Logistics fees: SHP’s hidden fees plus BPSF add up to be 17% higher than
   TB. (Bigger impact than Fashion/Beauty)
   -

   Commission rates: SHP charges a commission rate of 14-20%, while TB’s
   rate is just 2% for F&B and 5% for Furniture

After we reduce SLS mark up to 5% profits, how would the NPC look? (Am
assuming this is the main issue for furniture right now).

Assuming the SLS markup is reduced to 5% profit margin, NPC is expected to
improve to 1%. (+8% from existing NPC at -7%).

content.answer:
Hi Ian, Jianghong,

Please find the weekly Counter Taobao project update as of 22 August below.

Key Highlights


   -

   Overall:
   -

      Topline: ADO 87.6K (92% RR), MoM +32%, ADG $943K (93% RR), MoM +17%,
      behind target due to Lever 3 pause in mass pitching to onboard
sellers via
      ERP system.
      -

      SKU#: SKU# at 1.2M (19% RR) due to Lever 3.


   -

   Fashion:
   -

      Topline: ADO 47K (87% RR), ADG $461K (83% RR), ADO% to CB (excl CNLS)
      5.7% (MoM +0.6pp) mainly due to Lever 3 underperformance (RR 28%), we aim
      to catch up via 1-1 seller pitching, remaining levers are on track driven
      by MY/TH overperformance.
      -

      SKU#: SKU# at 858k (28% RR) due to Lever 3 challenges similar to
      topline underperformance.
      -

      Program PC2: 0.1%


   -

   Furniture:
   -

      Topline: ADO 7.5K (106% RR), ADG $273K (122% RR), ADO% (excl CNLS) to
      CB 1.3% (MoM +0.0pp) driven by program extension to CNLS particularly for
      MY given seller’s shift in focus from CB to CNLS following 3PF comms fee
      hike. MY top CNLS seller 中南 contribute 4.6K ADO (88% of CNLS Furniture
      Program sellers).
      -

      SKU#: SKU# at 279k (RR at 86%) due to Lever 3 underperformance,
      expected to catch up via 1-1 seller pitching.
      -

      Program PC2: 8.1%


   -

   F&B
   -

      Topline: ADO 1.7K (57% RR), ADG $12K (44% RR), ADO% to CB 2.5% (MoM
      +1pp). Low RR due to Lever 3 underperformance (RR at 4%) due to
slower than
      expected onboarding where only 3 of 14 onboarded sellers have completed
      listings, of which publish rate was at 36%. We have started a
trial test to
      unblock 3 subcategories in SG, successfully listing 6 SKUs on 15 Aug.
      -

         Next Step:To continue to monitor post-launch compliance risk
         before expanding to more SKUs.
         -

      SKU#: SKU#at 49K (2% RR), mainly due

content.question:
And how our orders will allow us to close the gap. And how soon will we
close the gap..



This program is designed based on the study that Tina sent last month,
specifically targeting *185 brands *that contribute to* ~20% of Coupang
CB’s business.*



After matching 1.5k top SKUs from these sellers, CB has a *match rate of
50%, *and *NPC of -37%* as of July.



We plan *to close the SKU gap from 50% to 75% by Dec’25, with an increase
from this month onwards.*



For the ADG gap using this same group of *items*, we are *36% of CP
currently, and we target to close the gap to 70% by the end of the year.*







This able is very hard to understand in general. Can we always show Coupang
numbers?

SKUs, ADO, ADGMV. And subsequently how our KR CB is doing vis a
vis Coupang?
And for Coupang, are we comparing with Coupang or Coupang CB?

proper way to compare.

come up with a proper table to compare, it is very painful for me right now
to understand where we are at exactly. And we have been ding donging around
this for weeks already. Can get back to me by today please.



Lastly, I do not understand why we need to take so long to improve our NPC
numbers..

*Interview Feedback:*
*Ren Jiahui, A*

+ He can clearly communicate his past experience, provide background on the
company he is in, the team he is in, the projects/product features he
delivered; His communication is also structured, little prompt is needed
from me.
+ When asked about his exact involvement, he gave honest feedback about the
work split between himself and his supervisor
+ When asked about what to consider to allow MY local seller to sell to PH,
he can provide a comprehensive list of check points, showcasing he has done
research or learned from previous interview with Eveline
+ Can start immediately
= When explained to him that CB BPM needs to handle live bugs, which is
different from traditional PM roles that he experienced through internship,
did not sense concern from him
= When asked about stressful time, he mentioned about about having to rush
through and deliver UAT in 2 days, which require some form of OT. But, i
did not sense any complaint on that.



*Eveline Seah, A*

(+) End-to-End Product Lifecycle Experience: Jesper has proven, hands-on
experience in managing the entire product lifecycle from his time at Ant
International. He led a second project more independently, which is a
significant strength. His involvement in requirement gathering, writing
PRDs, collaborating with dev team, and finding and fixing bugs demonstrates
a comprehensive understanding of the role.
(+) Strong Problem-Solving and Technical Aptitude: His background in
Business Analytics is a major asset. He showed a clear, logical approach to
problem-solving by identifying a bug, checking his own work (test cases,
PRD), and then confirming with the dev team. His ability to simplify
complex systems is a valuable skill for a BPM.
(+) Relevant Industry Experience and Communication: His internship at Ant
International, a fintech company, provides him with relevant experience in
a regulated environment that deals with a wide array of users and financial
products. His experience in translating PRDs to Mandarin Chinese and his
communication with the Shenzhen development team is a direct match for the
bilingual requirement of the CB BPM role.
(-) Lack of E-commerce Domain Expertise: His primary experience is in
fintech and enterprise software. While he mentioned being an avid Shopee
user, his answers did not demonstrate a deep understanding of the specific
complexities of cross-border e-commerce, such as logistics, operations, or
the intricacies of different markets. This is expected from his lack of
experience in e-commerce.
(-) Guidance-Oriented Mindset: While Jesper strives for independence, he is
used to seeking guidance from his supervisor. While this is expected of an
intern, it may signal that he is not a "self-starter" to the level required
in a dynamic, high-growth environment where a high degree of autonomy is
expected. However, he is able to identify areas of improvements under
guidance from his supervisor
(=) Jesper is a highly promising candidate with a solid technical
background and a strong track record of hands-on product management work.
He has a better grasp of the full product lifecycle than the other two
candidates and his experience with an international, cross-functional team
makes him an excellent fit for the role's collaborative nature.
(?) Autonomy in Decision Making: I would probe further into how he would
handle a task with a vague mandate where he has to define the problem and
the solution with minimal guidance.
(?) Interest in Cross-Border: While he expressed a general interest in
e-commerce, it would be important to validate his specific motivation for
the cross-border side of the business and why he believes he can contribute
meaningfully.

Thank you!

content.question:

Slide 7

   - Where do I find the standalone P&L?

- What is the total standalone P&L and how does it compare with targets?

Slide 10

   - Do not understand what we are proposing here, we already submitted
   option 2 on Slide 9 to regional right?

- And I cannot reconcile any of these options with Slide 9



Slide 16

   - What is the ADO% that has accepted?

And what is the % that has
   started?

Slide 18

   - What programs are there right now for Direct Sellers?

Can they be part
   of an existing CB program instead of creating another?

Slide 19

   - For direct sellers who perform well, who manages them?

Export or
   import market?
How do we see this working?

Slide 20

   - So sellers can start to invest in paid ads already?
- How about AMS?
- How does LS and Video work for direct sellers?

Slide 21

   - I remember there was some issues around what pricing the seller centre
   will recommend, has that been fixed yet?
For old as well as new sellers?

Slide 23

   - For the other lanes, would there be extra development effort required?
- Will there be direct model for these 4 markets if sellers choose to?

- Where did we land on getting USTs to be part of CNSIP as well for
   these new markets?

Slide 24

   - When will these features be ready?

Slide 44

   - When will SIP allow chat to flow to sellers?

How are we thinking of
   seller chats when that happens?

Slide 49

   - When are we migrating for MY?

Waiting time still looks quite bad



Others

   - How is CN SIP doing?
Adoption, scaling up etc?

content.answer: streamline touchpoints (1 RM for multiple markets);
better seller understanding and relationship in the same market;

easier to coordinate and prioritize platform initiatives from multiple
import markets for seller



   -

    How do we see this working?
   -

      Seller’s local RM  doubles up with seller’s direct RM, same as Taobao
      domestic + Taobao international RM support model
      -

      While we do see the management and acquisition is slow due to
      resource constraints during peak season,  we are proposing to a) add RM
      export KPI; b) simplify RM acquisition process with automation tool etc.



Slide 20

   -

   So sellers can start to invest in paid ads already?
   -

      Yes, sellers can purchase ads; meanwhile to better improve purchase
      UX, we are in progress of developing global wallet in their
direct shops to
      purchase directly; target ETA end Nov.



   -

   How about AMS?
   -

      System request to be raised in September cycle, pending revised
      business impact for priority review. Live ETA target end Q4.



   -

   How does LS and Video work for direct sellers?
   -

      Video
      -

         PDP video: already synced to direct shops
         -

         Seller video: 1) mass migration tool in place for IDMY; 2) sellers
         can also manually upload for direct shop
         -

      Livestream: sellers can set up livestream on web SC for direct shop
      already



Slide 21

   -

   I remember there was some issues around what pricing the seller centre
   will recommend, has that been fixed yet? For old as well as new sellers?
   -

      The system issue is fixed on 5 Sep; new sellers joined after 5 Sep
      will have the correct prices; historical sellers joined before 5 Sep have
      been notified to toggle on promotion resync from 16 Sep. To
monitor seller
      adoption % and to start weekly mass resync for sellers in Oct.



Slide 23

   -

   For the other lanes, would there be extra development effort required?
   -

content.answer:
Prioritization: new sellers with limited manpower to prepare stocks and
   shipping for different sites, tend to prioritize bigger sites, such as PH
   and MY.

Bad experience: Due to bad experiences in PH, such as PN pause and
   limited inbound quota since Aug., sellers who have not started doing SG
   tend to further postpone investing in SG.

Currently, SG has proposed a new service to do a one-time stock transfer
from 3PF to FBS, to drive up the progress. SG will also ensure of MY free
trial sellers who did not join SG free trial to join at least MY->SG Direct
or MYSG SIP

Slide 73 (now Slide 81)

How many have we gotten for H2 so far?

We have moved 51 sellers for 2025H2 so far.

For 2025H2 we aim to move 305 sellers in total, target still pending
feasibility check with CN and Local CNLS team:

   -

   CNLS mode: aim to onboard additional ~90 sellers, we will increase pitch
   success rate by facilitating vendor handshaking, FBS attack and ensuring
   regional role coverage
   -

   Direct model: we will kick off PH pitching in Oct, assume 30% success
   rate - same as other non-CNLS seller direct pitching
   -

   CB model: will kick off PH pitching in Oct, target pending CN side final
   confirmation


Slide 88 (now Slide 93)

Missing targets across the board other than TH?

For TH, the FBS MoM growth is actually lower than other markets at 13% (vs
PH MoM 23%, SG 17%). TH RR is high mainly due to revised down targets given
insufficient WH capacity and need to limit the new SKU coming in

For PH, missed targets mainly due to previous WH capacity issues (pausing
PN to stop new SKU and insufficient IB quota). While there’d be new WHs
opening in Sep and Oct, the space estimated to be still insufficient and
will need to limit new SKU coming in and revise down targets accordingly

For SG/ MY, it’s mainly due to low new seller/sku acquisition rate.
Catch-up plan: 1) to push for higher agree rate and push agreed sellers to
raise PN/IR; 2) to review top seller rejection reason 1 by 1, identify
tailored levers required for the conversion of these specific sellers, 3)
to pitch

content.answer:
High Seller COGS: RZY is a 铺货 reseller from Taobao or 1688, resulting in
   less competitive pricing. Although this seller's pricing is not ideal, they
   were able to bring in 450k SKUs which gave us the breadth of assortment
   that we wanted at the start.

Next step: We are identifying more direct TB sellers who can offer
      more competitive prices and onboard them onto TB program. (e.g 常州市希薇尔
      120 ADO selling dining tables and chairs, already onboarded to program)

Incorrect Price Setting: RZY uploads up to 30K SKUs per month to support
   assortment growth, using of blanket pricing strategies.

Next step: We are pushing RZY to improve SKU-level pricing and
      expect to correct this by 17 Oct in view of CN golden week. There has
      already been some progress here (12% of SKUs saw price reduction
after our
      NPC push)

Overall, RZY profit margin is around 10–12% of the selling price, which is
consistent with other top furniture GGPs on SHP.

content.answer:
Got it. Per HQ/CN legal guidance, we can pitch for all markets at one go if
the confirmed launch dates are same/close. We just need to state the market
names clearly in the pitching message, and give sellers options to choose
which ones they want to opt-out.

content.answer:
Hi Ian,

Please find the answers to your questions regarding overall KR growth on
Slide 6.

*#1 Why is there a lack of aggressive promotion except TH/VN? It is key
campaign season with 9.9?*

First of all, in overall, KR MoM% 1.07% is not lagging behind CB MoM%
0.68%.(KR/JP ADO ADG Target, Mom vs. CB & Platform
<https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1KD2-1mZeFn-glp6o7oHKwfvVmCjK-0_zrmImqlPrxt8/edit?gid=0#gid=0>
)

[image: image.png]


However, we are lagging behind the targets in 5 markets – SG/MY/PH/VN/MX.


MY/PH/VN/MX are mainly due to ▲some beauty sellers having a once-off
aggressive promotion and ▲k-pop sellers having new product launch in Aug
only none in Sep.

The beauty sellers that had once-off promotion in PH/MY were unmanaged
sellers so we didn’t have control over them. In VN, one managed beauty
brand had no once-off promotion in Sep but will resume the promotion in Oct.


   - Nacific PH(unmanaged sellers in Sep. Will be allocated to PSP team in
      Oct)’s no bundle deal promotion in Sep (but had the promotion in
Aug) (40%
      contrib. to PH target shortage)
      - Organacell MY(unmanaged seller which will convert to CN seller
      starting Oct)’s no KOL collaboration in Sep (but had the KOL
collab in Aug)
      (102% contrib to MY target shortage)
      - Clio VN’s having no bundle deal promotion in Sep unlike in Aug (33%
      contrib to target shortage)

The kpop sellers in MX had MoM regression because there was no new album
launch this month unlike Aug (stray kids album pre-order in Aug) (95%
contribution to MX target shortage)


SG is due to Sep Target being set too high due to high Aug EoM ADO
projection 14,406 (as of Aug 10 vs 13,355 Aug Full Month actual) based on
14 additional minispike days according to the regional campaign calendar.
In fact, no huge ado MoM regression in general from overall sellers,
including top UST sellers. Compared to PLT and CB, KR MoM% is equal at
-1.2% vs PLT -1.

*Subject:* Re: KR/JP Bi-weekly Update_250930



Hi Ian,



Please find the answers to your questions regarding overall KR growth on
Slide 6.



*#1 Why is there a lack of aggressive promotion except TH/VN?

It is key
campaign season with 9.9?*

First of all, in overall, KR MoM% 1.07% is not lagging behind CB MoM%
0.68%.(KR/JP ADO ADG Target, Mom vs. CB & Platform
<https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1KD2-1mZeFn-glp6o7oHKwfvVmCjK-0_zrmImqlPrxt8/edit?gid=0#gid=0>
)





However, we are lagging behind the targets in 5 markets – SG/MY/PH/VN/MX.



MY/PH/VN/MX are mainly due to ▲some beauty sellers having a once-off
aggressive promotion and ▲k-pop sellers having new product launch in Aug
only none in Sep.



The beauty sellers that had once-off promotion in PH/MY were unmanaged
sellers so we didn’t have control over them. In VN, one managed beauty
brand had no once-off promotion in Sep but will resume the promotion in Oct.


   - Nacific PH(unmanaged sellers in Sep. Will be allocated to PSP team in
      Oct)’s no bundle deal promotion in Sep (but had the promotion in
Aug) (40%
      contrib. to PH target shortage)
      - Organacell MY(unmanaged seller which will convert to CN seller
      starting Oct)’s no KOL collaboration in Sep (but had the KOL
collab in Aug)
      (102% contrib to MY target shortage)
      - Clio VN’s having no bundle deal promotion in Sep unlike in Aug (33%
      contrib to target shortage)

The kpop sellers in MX had MoM regression because there was no new album
launch this month unlike Aug (stray kids album pre-order in Aug) (95%
contribution to MX target shortage)



SG is due to Sep Target being set too high due to high Aug EoM ADO
projection 14,406 (as of Aug 10 vs 13,355 Aug Full Month actual) based on
14 additional minispike days according to the regional campaign calendar.
In fact, no huge ado MoM regression in general from overall sellers,
including top UST sellers. Compared to PLT and CB, KR MoM% is equal at
-1.2% vs PLT -1.2% and higher at -1.2% vs CB -6%.


*#2 Do we have a plan to avoid same thing from happening in October?*

For PH/VN, Nacific PH and Clio VN have confirmed that they will resume
the promotion in Oct.



Since MoM regression in MY/MX is caused by unmanaged beauty sellers and
some kpop sellers’ having no album launch plan in Sep, which we don’t have
control over, we can’t tackle these sellers directly.



However, to make up the gap, KR’s next steps are as below:


   - 1:1 KOL matching AMS program launch in SG/MY in Q4
   - Securing more PLT PRM support


   - PH: plt. GMV push rebate nomination push ($13k plt. budget) for key
      sellers
      - VN: 10k - 20k PRM support from local team to push further on BAU


   - Securing more KMD(Gov. project) budget for slower growth markets like
   SG, MY, PH, MX



Thank you,

Hyeyun Choi

content.question: What is the blocker to having more sellers and new SKUs right now?
Clio(the biggest contributor to the regression in Sep) has confirmed to
KR and VNCB team has secured 10k - 20k PRM support from to push further
Nacific(the biggest contributor to the regression in Sep, unmanaged
We have secured additional KMD budget($45k) 50% vouchers to make up the

content.answer:
Hi Ian,

 From KRCB's perspective, it seems that *sellers' lack of resources for TW
biz or having exclusive B2B distributor is the main blocker *to more brands
joining SHP KRCB for TW market.

 (In fact, headcount does not seem to be the issue currently as SA team has
contacted 100% of the sellers more than 2 times so far)

 In detail, please refer to the seller types who have refused joining SHP
KRCB for TW and solutions KRCB is planning to work on to accelerate
sourcing per each type:

*1) Sellers are not interested in expanding biz to TW market -->
Deprioritized*


   - These sellers are focusing on other markets/platforms and thus will
   not consider onboarding SHP TW KRCB
   - (The reason why these sellers are selling on Coupang is bc Coupang has
   taken the stock they bought from sellers for KR biz and started selling on
   TW biz without seller's confirmation, which is possible bc of their 1P biz
   model)

*2) Sellers are interested in TW market, but lack the resources to operate
--> Should tackle for cases b, c*

   - Blockers: Such sellers cannot invest much SHP/TW-dedicated headcount +
   resources especially in the below areas
      - a) Logistics: While other markets (who offer LFF/FBS) and Coupang
      (1P model) offer logistics channel with little resource input,
SLS requires
      a lot of preparation from seller side
      - b) TFDA license/certifications: Currently vendors supporting KR
      sellers for TFDA certifications do not support TW market. Bc there is no
      vendor introduced from SHP side, sellers drop off in the
onboarding process
      due to difficulty in preparation
         - Vendors do not support TW due to smaller market size while
         having many difficulties in applying for licenses
         - TFDA is not enforced by SHP unless sellers hope to become OS in
         TW, but many sellers hope to become OS since OS vs non-OS GMV
disparity is
         especially large in TW

exclusive B2B distributor (who controls greater China area) --> Should
tackle*

   China region with 1 vendor, they are not inclined to join SHP KRCB as shops
   - Solutions: In this case, KRCB plans to reach out to vendors to get
   them to onboard as SHP TW local shop



If there are local distributors already, it should be a matter of acquiring
them right?

content.answer:

 Yes that is true.
 Following next steps would be to identify local distributors and onboard
them as local shops.

content.answer:
Hi Ian, Jianghong,

Please find the weekly Counter Taobao project update as of 26 September
below.


[image: image.png]

Table: Counter TB Program Performance SEA + TW


[image: image.png]

Table: Counter TB Net Price Competitiveness Performance SEA + TW

Key Highlights


   -

   Aug SOB:
   -

      Across SEA+TW, SHP vs TBG ADO at 4.57x (MoM -0.09x), ADG at 1.17x
      (MoM +0.01x), ABS at 0.26x (MoM +0.01x). ABS SOB increased due to TB’s
      direct price discount (超级立减) and smart coupons in Aug. ADO and ADG SOB
      remain stable as both platforms operated with low investment
during the BAU
      month before Sep.
      -

   Overall:
   -

      Topline: Sep MTD Program ADO 123K (RR 101%) MoM +31%, ADG 1.1M (RR
      100%) MoM +19%, ADO% to CB at 6.8%, +1.6pp MoM driven by
overachievement of
      existing fashion sellers in MY (ADO 12.7K, RR 117%) and TH (ADO 13.9K, RR
      106%). Notable seller performance include Lever 3 top GGP 安阳市殷
MTD 602 ADO
      (MoM +204%) and Lever 2 top GGPs 深圳市哒 MTD 429 ADO (MoM +47%)
      -

      Target has been adjusted upwards to account for this overachievement
      of targets
      -

      SKU#: SKU# at 1.8M (69% RR) due to lever 3 underachievement.


   -

   Fashion:
   -

      Topline: ADO 63.4K (104% RR), ADG $572K (96% RR), ADO% to CB 7.4%
      (MoM +1.4 pp). Overachievement is contributed by strong
performance of new
      sellers from Lever 1 and Lever 3 in MY and TH
      -

      SKU#: 1.1M (RR 80%) driven by Lever 3 underperformance (RR 44%) and
      Lever 1 underperformance in TH (RR 74%) and VN (RR 79%).
      -

      Program PC2: -0.7%

   Furniture:
   -

      Topline: ADO 7.4K (RR 98%), ADG $241K (RR 99%), ADO% to CB 0.7 (MoM
      +0.0pp) mainly due to SG underachievement as top shops are under penalty
      due to order non-fulfillment rate.
      -

      SKU#: 570K (RR 175%)  MoM +73% driven by strong performance

Hi Ian,

Please refer to our replies in blue below.

Slide 7

   -

   How is the F&B unlock situation in SG/MY right now? Are we doing it for
   other markets?


We have unlocked all suitable categories in SG/MY/PH and started trialing
using ERP. For the remaining markets (TH/VN/TW), we will not explore as
these markets have strict licensing requirements for F&B. Through these
unlocking, we currently have 26 F&B ADO for restricted categories (5.6% of
ERP F&B ADO).


Next step: We will explore to list more SKUs in these unlocked categories
from CNCB MP sellers (Both Lever 1 and 2).

      -

      F&B > Dairy & Eggs > Milk > UHT Milk
      -

is the JST events in which their business sales are actively involved with

Slide 14

   - Where is the contribution of sellers coming from right now?

Is it JST
   or WDT?

More sellers are coming from JST (89%) vs WDT (11%).

Meanwhile, we developed a new vendor called ‘Ocean Friend’ (OF) who
onboarded in mid Sep. They can help us mass pitch, auto onboard TB sellers,
which contributed to 732 new sellers in the past 2 weeks.





What is in it for OF?
They are an ERP as well?

- Do we still face the situation where we can only take their listing
   price (and not discounted price) from ERP?

For JST and WDT, by default, we can only take their listing price without
discount. In Sep, we built the feature to let seller offer better prices.
With this, 12% of sellers did give us 10%-30% discounted price through ERP.



For OF, it was discussed that discounted prices can be offered, however, we
are currently still in the early stages of understanding their price
structure. We will pull the data and provide an update next week.



Adoption of the discount feature is quite low?

So in general, the pricing
may not be the best?

Hi Ian,



Please refer to our replies in blue below.



Slide 7

   - How is the F&B unlock situation in SG/MY right now?

Are we doing it
   for other markets?

We have unlocked all suitable categories in SG/MY/PH and started trialing
using ERP. For the remaining markets (TH/VN/TW), we will not explore as
these markets have strict licensing requirements for F&B. Through these
unlocking, we currently have 26 F&B ADO for restricted categories (5.6% of
ERP F&B ADO).



Next step: We will explore to list more SKUs in these unlocked categories
from CNCB MP sellers (Both Lever 1 and 2).



Detailed Unlocked F&B Categories By Market:

   - SG:


   - F&B > Dairy & Eggs > Milk > UHT Milk
      - F&B > Convenience > Ready-to-eat > Cooked Food


   - MY


   - F&B > Snacks > Dried Snacks > Meat Floss
      - F&B > Dairy & Eggs > Milk > UHT Milk
      - F&B > Convenience > Ready-to-eat > Cooked Food


   - PH:


   - F&B > Bakery > Breads
      - F&B > Bakery > Cakes & Pies
      - F&B > Snacks > Dried Snacks > Meat Floss
      - F&B > Bakery > Pastry
      - F&B > Snacks > Dried Snacks > Dried Seafood Snacks



Slide 14

   - Where is the contribution of sellers coming from right now?

Is it JST
   or WDT?

More sellers are coming from JST (89%) vs WDT (11%).

Meanwhile, we developed a new vendor called ‘Ocean Friend’ (OF) who
onboarded in mid Sep. They can help us mass pitch, auto onboard TB sellers,
which contributed to 732 new sellers in the past 2 weeks.



   - What is the most effective form of acquisition right now?

Via leads
   from ERP?
Or mass acquisition via events?

The most effective way is through the new vendor (OF), as they can mass
acquire skus and auto onboard to Shopee through system. Following which, it
is the JST events in which their business sales are actively involved with
us to introduce leads.



   - Given the momentum, are there more categories that we should start
   onboarding more?
Like HL or Furniture?

Yes, especially after we have the OF vendor in place, the sku pitching is
relatively lower effort, we have acquired 6.5k HL skus and 0.2k Furniture
skus.

We are currently still quite focused on Fashion and FB categories,
according to the wishlist from local teams, 84% of the sku needs are
fashion, but will start to explore other categories.



Slide 20

   - Furniture and F&B still quite bad?

Furniture: 82% of expensive SKUs are driven by seller margin markups, with
the majority concentrated under 润泽优, which accounts for 73% of the pool
(11K SKUs). So far, we have successfully negotiated 1.6K SKUs (15%) to meet
our target prices, while 3.8K SKUs (35%) were rejected. We are continuing
negotiations on the remaining SKUs.

   - However, 润泽优 faces structural limitations due to higher seller COGS,
   as they operate on a smaller scale on Shopee and have limited bargaining
   power with suppliers. This restricts the potential for further price
   reduction.


   - Next Step: We are identifying and engaging with direct Taobao sellers
      (e.g., 常州市希薇尔) who can offer more competitive pricing.

F&B: We are actively pitching sellers to adopt the heavy rate table in
order to address the structural logistics fee issue. ETA to conclude by 15
Oct, after CN Golden Week



   - Do we still face the situation where we can only take their listing
   price (and not discounted price) from ERP?

content.answer:
Hi Ian,

Please see replies in purple below.

Slide 14

   -

   Where is the contribution of sellers coming from right now? Is it JST or
   WDT?

More sellers are coming from JST (89%) vs WDT (11%).

Meanwhile, we developed a new vendor called ‘Ocean Friend’ (OF) who
onboarded in mid Sep. They can help us mass pitch, auto onboard TB sellers,
which contributed to 732 new sellers in the past 2 weeks.


What is in it for OF? They are an ERP as well?

OF is a dropshipper that has API integration to TB backend. We pay 1-2% fee
per order to OF.

The key value proposition of OF is that they are able to provide top TB
sellers/skus we want to source very quickly. As of today, OF sourced ~1.2
million SKUs in 2 weeks,



   -

   Do we still face the situation where we can only take their listing
   price (and not discounted price) from ERP?

For JST and WDT, by default, we can only take their listing price without
discount. In Sep, we built the feature to let seller offer better prices.
With this, 12% of sellers did give us 10%-30% discounted price through ERP.

For OF, it was discussed that discounted prices can be offered, however, we
are currently still in the early stages of understanding their price
structure. We will pull the data and provide an update next week.



Adoption of the discount feature is quite low? So in general, the pricing
may not be the best?


The adoption of the discount feature is quite low now as we are currently
pitching to only 13 sellers.

We will start pitching the discount feature to all sellers, prioritizing
those >10 ADO, and target to achieve 30% seller adoption rate. Once
adopted, we expect sellers to improve their settlement price with us by
5-10%.

content.question: What is in it for OF?
They are an ERP as well?

OF is a dropshipper that has API integration to TB backend. We pay 1-2% fee
per order to OF.

The key value proposition of OF is that they are able to provide top TB
sellers/skus we want to source very quickly. As of today, OF sourced ~1.2
million SKUs in 2 weeks,



How does the model work?
They pay us comms but we pay them fee?
How is their LSR/NFR like?
Given that we have tightened DTS in CB?
Adoption of the discount feature is quite low?

So in general, the pricing
may not be the best?

The adoption of the discount feature is quite low now as we are currently
pitching to only 13 sellers.

We will start pitching the discount feature to all sellers, prioritizing
those >10 ADO, and target to achieve 30% seller adoption rate. Once
adopted, we expect sellers to improve their settlement price with us by
5-10%.



This is probably a self-fulfilling prophecy?

They will not have high ADO if
their pricing is not good?

Hi Ian,



Please see replies in purple below.



Slide 14

·  Where is the contribution of sellers coming from right now?

Is it JST or
WDT?

More sellers are coming from JST (89%) vs WDT (11%).

Meanwhile, we developed a new vendor called ‘Ocean Friend’ (OF) who
onboarded in mid Sep. They can help us mass pitch, auto onboard TB sellers,
which contributed to 732 new sellers in the past 2 weeks.



What is in it for OF?
They are an ERP as well?

OF is a dropshipper that has API integration to TB backend. We pay 1-2% fee
per order to OF.

The key value proposition of OF is that they are able to provide top TB
sellers/skus we want to source very quickly. As of today, OF sourced ~1.2
million SKUs in 2 weeks,



·  Do we still face the situation where we can only take their listing
price (and not discounted price) from ERP?

For JST and WDT, by default, we can only take their listing price without
discount. In Sep, we built the feature to let seller offer better prices.
With this, 12% of sellers did give us 10%-30% discounted price through ERP.

For OF, it was discussed that discounted prices can be offered, however, we
are currently still in the early stages of understanding their price
structure. We will pull the data and provide an update next week.



Adoption of the discount feature is quite low?

So in general, the pricing
may not be the best?

*Subject:* Re: SIP Weekly Update - Week ending 3 Oct



Hi Ian,



Slide 3

   - Any sense why WH cross selling is dropping by so much?

- MoM -1534 ADO mainly driven by


   - CB Choice LFF MY>SG -1334 ADO MoM, because in Sep SCS did not
         replenish SKUs that are 1) P&L -ve; 2) not doing well in MY
despite doing
         well in SG, as from P&L perspective it makes more sense to fulfil from
         CN-SG instead of CN-MY-SG.
         - MY local SCS>SG -346 ADO MoM, due to a reduction in ad budget
         from 1) COGS increased to optimize MYWH P&L; 2) MYSG SIP P&L
revised up to
         breakeven.
         - We will revise ADO targets for above 2 BUs for Nov-Dec due to
         above systemic changes that will last.


   - Are we exploring WH cross selling for other lanes on top of just MYSG?

- Yes we are assessing potential and feasibility of local FBS
      cross-selling with MY/VN/PH local FBS teams; will bring findings
to Oct SIP
      monthly meeting for review.



Slide 5

   - What is the issue on TH side?

- Challenges faced in TH 1) local RM has high number of ongoing
      initiatives, difficult to push acquisition KPIs; 2) initial group of ~20
      pilot sellers demand a high level of hand-holding to set up and start
      selling while TH Export Team has only 1 HC to support.
      - Aligned with THCB team the next steps


   - For THPH lane, work with PHCB to narrow down 1:1 pitching pool from 6k
         to 1-2k, focusing on most-wanted brands/SKUs PH need and willing to
         support, then work with THBD to formalize into local RM KPI
[ETA 17 Oct].
         - Work with PHCB to build 2-3 success cases, showcase them in Nov
         TH seller summit [ETA end-Oct].
         - THCB in progress of hiring 3 BPOs into TH Export team to provide
         seller support; to ramp up mass pitching from mid-Oct alongside BPO
         onboarding.


   - Has MYPH and MYTH started yet?

- Yes MYPH and MYTH launched on 22 Sep, has whitelisted 1st group of 9K
      existing MYSG Direct sellers via system pop-up, to invite them to open
      TH/PH shops. MTD 247 active sellers for MYPH, 203 active sellers for MYTH.
      - In progress of standardizing export CS / seller support process,
      then ramp up mass pitching to all MY eligible sellers from mid-Oct.



Slide 14

   - On IDSIP Sea shipping, why is ADO uplift minimal?

Seems different from
   what ID team was proposing?

- ID assessment uses a very aggressive growth assumption - that $0.06
      price reduction leads to ~20% ADO uplift, vs our real experience
is ~2% ADO
      uplift only.
      - Has aligned the assessment with IDSPX team and concluded that the
      ADO uplift (<500-800 ADO on IDMY max) not able to justify a new channel.
      Therefore we will park the Seashipping channel discussion.
      - Instead, we will explore with IDSPX alternative CPO/lead time
      saving initiatives focusing on parcel vol allocation and air channel CPO
      optimization.



   - On Dino, when are we starting to sell?
How is ADO like right now?

   - For VN, was this caused by infrastructure fee or by the tax issues?
   - The penetration delta here is mostly caused by the infrastructure fee.

   stable, until the step decline in July following the launch of infra fee.

   15 days before and 15 days after the launch of the infra fee, which showed
   a similar decline in CB ADO penetration.

content.question: - Any other levers to grow faster?

- We have maximized SKU count via migration, and now focus on driving up
      SKU efficiency via


   - Price reduction for non-performing SKUs; target ADO uplift ~3k; ETA:
         end-Nov
         - AMS coverage ramp up in PH/VN (80% coverage in SG/MY/TH done);
         target ADO uplift ~1k; ETA: end-Oct
         - Optimize marketing budget ROI: target ADO uplift ~1k by Dec





Are there still sellers who are not on SIP if they are not doing direct
selling in the market?

Slide 7

   - Where did we land on the ownership of Direct selling?

Where
   sellers/brands complain that they often get passed around


   - As aligned with local market CB teams, ownership is structured as below


   - Export market CB team: owns seller acquisition and support
         - Import market CB team: owns seller programs (treat direct
         sellers the same way as CN/KR/JPCB MP sellers)
         - Reg SIP team: reg direct selling PMO


   - Seller/brand enquiries SOPs are now established in MY/SG/ID/VN/TH as
      below


   - Managed sellers: seller’s local RM >> export market CB team >> reg SIP
         team [channel: seatalk group chat with local RMs]
         - Unmanaged sellers: seller’s local CS >> export market CB team >>
         reg SIP team [channel: CS IHS system]





This is as good as saying there is no owner.



   - How are we tracking the use of paid ads right now for direct selling?

- Reg direct selling PMO owns the tracking of paid ads spending and
      shares the insights with export market PICs to drive seller investment
      - At current stage, we focus on pushing sellers to start investing on
      ads, thus key metrics we track are 1) # sellers on ads; 2) ads
spending; 3)
      ads GMV; 4) ROAS. An example of IDMY tracking as below



Do we have the breakdown by lane…adoption, tr% etc.



   - How are returns being handled right now for SIP and Direct Selling?

Is
   it a blocker for sellers to join?

- Pls see the summary below for short-term/as-is. Variation in practices
      for direct selling mainly due to 1) export market request; 2) feasibility
      of establishing a RTS channel.

o   Short-term, RR scheme is not a key blocker for ID/VN/MY sellers to join
SIP/Direct, based on seller feedback. We will monitor seller sentiment with
local market PICs and enhance seller education to help them price in the
potential RR cost.



Why do we choose for local disposal instead of RTS?

content.answer:
rebate 50% of ads top up through platform sponsored vouchers; 3) provide
   adxpress service
   -

   Data validated with both SGBI and CB FP&A


   -

   How about ADO coverage? Why so high for VN?
   -

      ADO coverage = lane level Ads ADO / total ADO
      -

      Higher ADO coverage for VNMY direct at 46% due to lower ads CPO at
      $1.1 vs all other lanes (e.g., IDMY $4.6). VNMY lower ads CPO
driven up by
      top 10 shops 100% ads adoption with low ads CPO; IDMY out of 10
top shops,
      6 without ads adoption.
      -

      Next step: working with ID team to drive up top sellers ads investment

 [image: image.png]



Slide 18

   -

   Do not understand this very well. How does CN sellers use paid ads right
   now? How does it compare to what SEA Direct Sellers have?

[image: image.png]

   -

   CNCB sellers primarily use overseas shop balance to purchase ads, i.e.,
   deduct the cost from their shop earnings. Under existing Shopee system
   design, this payment method is only possible when seller’s overseas shop is
   linked to a 3PP wallet (e.g. Payoneer).
   -

   For SEA Direct sellers as-is
   -

      MY export sellers are able to purchase ads the same way as CNCB
      sellers, as they are using 3PP wallet (Payoneer), continuing since MYSG
      Direct launch last Mar
      -

      For ID/VN export sellers at launch, aligned with Christin/Anh to 1)
      launch without 3PP to improve seller adoption; 2) improve system
to enable
      shop balance purchase without needing 3PP wallet by EOY; 3)
interim to use
      offline manual adjustment for seller payout - which cannot be
used for ads
      purchase due to system limitations


   -

   And from a product point of view, what are we building to allow them to
   have the same access?
   -

      Product is developing the system feature to enable SEA Direct sellers
      to use overseas shop balance to purchase ads credit v

investment with import markets; ETA end-Nov; 2) long term: System feature
in development to allow seller purchase with shop balance within seller
center, ETA Dec-Mar

o   Reason 3: Lower CR, especially in ID export lanes

the best local price to do the overseas pricing

top 20 sellers, ETA mid-Nov

o   Reg team will focus on ads investment and price comp improvement as P1
in Nov, then move to additional reg initiative push in Dec, including

§  Seller investment: AMS, video, livestream

§  Conversion improvement: CS, key ops metrics



   - For import to SG, @Jason Yu will be the PIC right?

Can remind me where
   we land on MY?

- Yes aligned that @Jason Yu <jason.yu@shopee.com> team will own the
      Direct selling ADO into MY and into SG, this will include


   - topline/PnL targets
         - marketing program/resources
         - Seller/SKU acquisition direction in export market
         - Optimization across import BUs to drive growth


   - Regional direct team will focus on cross-lane efforts including


   - Launch new lanes, secure local resources to start business
         - Align and report regional topline target, track progress across
         lanes, coordinate market resources across lanes
         - Initiate and drive regional initiatives to improve seller
         experience and drive growth across lanes (ads investment, price comp,
         content enablement)
         - Establish and operate inter-market infrastructure and process
         (system, logistics, payment, CS, ops policy)



Slide 17

   - For Ads TR here, what is the denominator?
Total direct GMV ?

Seems
   very high?

o   Denominator = total direct GMV at lane level

o   Oct Ads TR across lane <5%, all below import market CNCB average, and
most lower than their local shop (except MYSG), indicating room to improve

o   MYSG Direct TR higher than seller’s local shop because SG platform has
provided incentives to Direct sellers including 1) 20% off paid-ads top-up;
2) rebate 50% of ads top up through platform sponsored vouchers; 3) provide
adxpress service

o   Data validated with both SGBI and CB FP&A

   - How about ADO coverage?
Why so high for VN?

- ADO coverage = lane level Ads ADO / total ADO
      - Higher ADO coverage for VNMY direct at 46% due to lower ads CPO at
      $1.1 vs all other lanes (e.g., IDMY $4.6). VNMY lower ads CPO
driven up by
      top 10 shops 100% ads adoption with low ads CPO; IDMY out of 10
top shops,
      6 without ads adoption.
      - Next step: working with ID team to drive up top sellers ads
      investment







Slide 18

   - Do not understand this very well. How does CN sellers use paid ads
   right now?
How does it compare to what SEA Direct Sellers have?

o   CNCB sellers primarily use overseas shop balance to purchase ads, i.e.,
deduct the cost from their shop earnings. Under existing Shopee system
design, this payment method is only possible when seller’s overseas shop is
linked to a 3PP wallet (e.g. Payoneer).

o   For SEA Direct sellers as-is

§  MY export sellers are able to purchase ads the same way as CNCB sellers,
as they are using 3PP wallet (Payoneer), continuing since MYSG Direct
launch last Mar

§  For ID/VN export sellers at launch, aligned with Christin/Anh to 1)
launch without 3PP to improve seller adoption; 2) improve system to enable
shop balance purchase without needing 3PP wallet by EOY; 3) interim to use
offline manual adjustment for seller payout - which cannot be used for ads
purchase due to system limitations

   - And from a product point of view, what are we building to allow them
   to have the same access?

- Product is developing the system feature to enable SEA Direct sellers
      to use overseas shop balance to purchase ads credit via seller center.
      Target to roll-out in mid Dec after 12.12. Timeline for each
export market
      as below:


   - ID / VN Export - by Dec 2025
         - MY / SG Export - by Jan 2026
         - Others - by Mar 2026

ERP/SHP Mkt pitching: This is the most successful source with 139
   sellers and a 65% success rate due to SHP Mkt team pitching sellers 1:1
   together with ERP with more credibility.

Shopee-led pitching: Total of 16 sellers with 1% success rate, which is
   lower than Lever 1’s success rate at ~2%. The gap is due to sellers not
   trusting Shopee’s calls/texts as they do not know who we are, and do not
   want to take the time to know.

Mass pitching: Total of 134 sellers success rate at 0.1%, which includes
   various pitching sources such as events / livestreams / PR articles / etc.

contribution more?

and we target to do more moving forward. Will put more details in the
upcoming Reg CB projects monthly, a summary below:

ERP/SHP Mkt pitching: This channel has 85 sellers who listed with 2.2k

SAI pitching: This channel has 10 sellers who listed with 0.4k ADO or ~9%

Mass pitching: This channel has 52 sellers who listed with 1.9k ADO or
   or ~41%


What is the mass pitching blocker?


This refers to the initial expectation that ERPs will show a banner
advertising Shopee’s partnership on their main page which did not
materialise due to fear of offending other e-commerce platforms. Instead,
we pivoted to the 3 sources of acquisition that we shared in the table
above, with the mass pitching instead diverting to other sources such as
livestreams, PR articles and events.


We will not focus on OF. For JST and WDT, what else can we do there?


We target to onboard ~50 sellers moving forward each month, and will place
more focus on mass pitching via events/livestreams/PR articles instead as
it is the quickest to scale. Specifically in Nov, we target to get 33
sellers from mass pitching.


For the other 2 channels referring to ERP/SHP Mkt pitching and SAI
pitching, we will still target to bring in ~20 sellers per month but it is
harder to scale because

(1) ERP/SHP Mkt pitching: a) ERP and their sellers are quite focused on
peak season sales with limited resources on our program b) ERPs require our
orders to be larger to scale this program further.

(2) SAI pitching: is very low efficiency, our success rate is 1.1% vs Lever
1’s ~2% success. The gap is due to domestic TB sellers not knowing who
Shopee is. We suggested trying this later after rounds of PR articles in Q4.

content.answer:
Hi Ian,

Please find our responses below:

I thought WDT, JST were introducing us to their key merchants so that we
could acquire 1 by 1? We only have contacts for ~200 sellers after so long?


For JST, the sellers provided are the top 10% on TB by GMV within their
respective categories. They gave us 221 leads, from which we acquired
sellers one by one. These sellers are generating approximately 4.4K ADO,
accounting for 80% of Swarm, with an SKU efficiency of around 1%. Moving
forward, JST will continue to provide us with 50 sellers per month, and we
are capped to this number.

For the JST one-by-one pitch, we prioritize the quality of sellers over
quantity. These sellers require more effort to pitch and onboard compared
to others, which is why we target 50 sellers per month (around 200 sellers
over the past four months). On average, each seller can bring us around
3,000 SKUs.

In contrast, WDT provided only 4 leads, as they are currently focused on
improving their internal systems in Q4. Without a fully integrated system,
the seller OPS UX is quite messy, so we decided to pause onboarding since
October. We plan to resume onboarding WDT sellers after their internal
system improvements, targeted for completion in Q1 Y26.



For Lever 2, who is governing the inclusion of SKUs right now? To indeed
verify that these are TB skus as compared to SLS sellers trying to pull a
fast one


The local CB team currently manages the process of setting up
Shopee-sponsored shop vouchers for sellers. To ensure that future shop
vouchers are applied only to TB SKUs, we will establish exclusion criteria
for other assortments in the shop that are not part of the program’s focus.

Best Regards,

Max Goh

Address: 1 Fusionopolis Place, #09-10, Galaxis, Singapore 138522

Hi Ian,

Following up on *Action Item #7 *from the Oct 23 Bi-weekly regarding *KRCB
Paid Ads Take Rate*, I’d like to provide clarification on the
TH performance.

First, I’d like to apologize for the confusion caused by our Oct 23 update
— when we mentioned that TH & TW “met 100% Take Rate RR,” it referred to
the *run rate of Push Program sellers as of Oct 19*, not the final
month-end performance.

In reality, the October Net Paid Ads Take Rate for TH closed below target,
mainly due to temporary budget shifts by non-program sellers.

However, we expect recovery in November with:

   -

   *Equalberry’s cross-market budget expansion** (+56K USD across 6
   markets)*, and
   -

   *Continuation of the ongoing TH/TW Push Programs*, both of which have
   shown consistent spending ramp-up since early November.

With these adjustments, *TH’s Take Rate is projected to realign with the
November target level*.
You may refer to the detailed breakdown and outlook in the table below for
more context.

content.answer:
Hi Ian,

Following up on your questions regarding the GMV Max conversion and the
November Paid Ads MTD performance, please find the updates below.


1. GMV Max Conversion — Update Expected by Nov 18

Most KR sellers have already migrated after the manual mode sunset.
To ensure accuracy across both KR and JP markets, we are currently
cross-validating the dataset and will share the consolidated update by
early next week (ETA: Nov 18).

2. Nov. Paid Ads — MTD Performance (Nov 1–13)

Including the 11.11 results, Net Paid Ads Take Rate MTD stands at *5.25%*,
reaching *82% of target*, while spending is tracking at *117% RR*.


[image: image.png]

The current TR RR% is temporarily below target due to:

   -

   *11.11 spike-day distortion* where GMV denominator increased abnormally
   mid-month
   -

   *Other seller push programs (TH, TW, SG, VN, PH)* have not yet been
   reflected in the MTD data

With these factors normalizing in the coming days, we expect TR% to recover
and stay close to the November target toward month-end.


Thank you,

Ka Young Choi

content.answer:
We as a CB team were unable to convince brands in this 1 week because we
were not clear enough in what we can offer for their local OS, when these
brands are clearly against the dual-shop concept and we need to show them
tangible benefits. That's why I think structurally Local Team should be the
main team to onboard local OS as they can better clarify what SHP can offer
for the local shop, OR let our team know local team's offerings and we can
be the comms channel.

It's very regretful how you say this doesn't solve anything. We got seller
commitment on LFF, NPC and SKU gap KPI, and got vendor info & operating
model, and clarified what the next steps can be which will close SHP-TTS
gap. If it's a wholesale model, vendors have good negotiating power when
speaking with the brand. For this 1 week, we talked to the business owner
of the overall market (MY) or at least SHP-MY. And dralthea, we talked to
B2B team who is more keen to increase the local vendors' volume, as opposed
to brand HQ CB shop. The brands we talked to B2C teams include below and we
can follow up to get to the right PIC within their org.

   - Wishtrend : TTS vendor already onboarded to shopee local. We can
   follow up on this shop
   - Grafen : TTS selling only close-to-expiry items with shop ADO of 0-2.
   - Roundlab : But plan to not operate as local vendor model for TTS
   either. All platform shops to run via brand entity
   - Dalba


If our goal is to onboard local OS, and not close the gap with TTS, then
what we did at least provided vendor info to get us started. If this effort
doesn't start solving either local OS open or TTS gap close, then you let
us know what we do.

We as a CB team were unable to convince brands in this 1 week because we
were not clear enough in what we can offer for their local OS, when these
brands are clearly against the dual-shop concept and we need to show them
tangible benefits. That's why I think structurally Local Team should be the
main team to onboard local OS as they can better clarify what SHP can offer
for the local shop, OR let our team know local team's offerings and we can
be the comms channel.

It's very regretful h

content.question: The local vendors need the brands to agree to onboard?

How will your
suggestion solve this?

How will we solve the issue of brands like Torridien
not having TT assortment on our platform?

You mentioned this.



   - Unlike CN or global brands, KR brands open TTS via local vendor
   because they cannot open shop under Korean entity as per TT policy. But
   brands have concerns about the profitability and price control of TTS run
   by local vendors, so they push back on these vendors running SHP local OS

So shall we change our policy so the brands will allow local vendors to
onboard?

content.answer:
Any official shop needs brands' consent and TTS local vendor needs brands'
consent to expand their platform channels. So I suggested we give brands
more reasons to change their mind and open local OS. This needs either (1)
clarification on what SHP can do for local OS which needs local team's
help, or (2) clarification on what the vendor can do for the brands'
business (for SHP local OS) in the local market which needs the vendors'
better terms and conditions with the brand. Both are difficult to come from
only us, the CB team, even if we do represent and negotiate on behalf of
the platform.

For Torriden, our team successfully negotiated with the brand, with the
"push / pressure" of opening local OS, to match the SKUs by Dec and this is
shared in the table.

We can change our policy so that brands need to open local OS through local
entities, like how TTS does. Then brands would likely leverage their
current vendor network  (like TTS vendor) to open local OS. If our goal is
open local OS, this will solve the issue.

Any official shop needs brands' consent and TTS local vendor needs brands'
consent to expand their platform channels. So I suggested we give brands
more reasons to change their mind and open local OS. This needs either (1)
clarification on what SHP can do for local OS which needs local team's
help, or (2) clarification on what the vendor can do for the brands'
business (for SHP local OS) in the local market which needs the vendors'
better terms and conditions with the brand. Both are diffi

more reasons to change their mind and open local OS. This needs either (1)

help, or (2) clarification on what the vendor can do for the brands'
business (for SHP local OS) in the local market which needs the vendors'
better terms and conditions with the brand. Both are diffi

consent to expand their platform channels. So I suggested we give brands
more reasons to change their mind and open local OS. This needs either (1)
clarification on what SHP can do for local OS which needs local team's
help, or (2) clarification on what the vendor can do for the brands'
business (for SHP local OS) in the local market which needs the vendors'
better terms and conditions with the brand. Both are diffi

We have been talking about closing the gap for months, so for the past 2
months, so the team built dashboards for each of NPC, SKU, campaign
participation, and did manual checks to match shop and sku better, because
we understand how inaccurate price data hinders price match progress and
deteriorates SHP-seller relationship.
The NPC, campaign, SKU, we're monitoring and improving without you having
to ask, and we have been sharing in the biweekly to show how much it's
improving for the past months. I believe and I do see how the team did work
to move towards the target and have shown progress, and we don't deserve to
hear that this is not moving anywhere

We show you the NPC, we show you the campaign participation, and we show
you the SKU assortment but if biweekly meeting of Q&A is not sufficient, I
attached dashboard links below that we're using to monitor so you can see
real time as well

   - Campaign participation Oct to Nov KR 84% --> 87%. JP 59% --> 72% (vs
   CN 75% --> 70%) (here
   <https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1TK2R8R6aHpjs-Ay14hJqLdpZHqliaK-M3QKdAbgEc38/edit?gid=927180518#gid=927180518>on
   RM/team level too)
   - SKU exclusive on TTS 108 --> 100 (here
   <https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/14oaoZg6NxM1erH6ufwVuyYXuAbFBtmF83scxNeew-ns/edit?gid=717011053#gid=717011053>
   )
   - NPC 36% --> 9% (here
   <https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1dIpCJsO2Gm_yu0Z_eEPWkjGLcoubeIicQLnJoZfONPc/edit?gid=253123941#gid=253123941>)
   NPC is not improving thus we shared seller nego commitments for EOY
   - Seller mgmt benchmark too, we're making progress with actual
   dashboards and SOPs being launched (here
   <https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1bsglDc5mJax9uC7k66vQ9varTa2taFurak0LuqB6E_A/edit?gid=1286813728#gid=1286813728>
   )

For local OS onboarding, let me breakdown into more granular level and
write down which week/day. I will share this this Thursday.

The newsarticle means TT will allow CB model like ours

requested.

ads-adopted shops (spending > 0)

content.question:
Thanks.



Slide 6

   - Can remind me what was the target for VN before TET?

Will we be able
   to reach TT 40% by then?

Slide 7

   - Where did we land on tightening policy of fake 3PF in the end?

Slide 9

   - For MY and PH, is it possible for FBS  > 3PF?

Feels like FBS can still
   afford to be bigger



Slide 10

   - When will PH fix their WH issue?

Slide 12

   - How are we managing this process in CN right now?

For those sellers in
   our FBS WH, we go seller by seller and attempt to increase their ADO %
   every month?

We look at the SKU mix, ATP % and push them to put more in
   local WH over time?

And then we track seller lens to ensure growth is both
   by new sellers as existing sellers doing more



Slide 13 -14

   - What does this all site RVP mean?

Slide 20

   - Is there a view on cost to seller?

And for SLS, there is also cost for
   seller to pick/pack/store
   - And from a pricing point of view, how will we charge seller?

- We have to go back to why we are doing this
      - It is cheaper for seller to do this
      - It is more profitable for Shopee to do this (including cost savings
      + increase in PC2 difference)
      - Or it is less profitable but it will help buyer experience
   - All the above is still not very clear right now



Slide 26

   - Why will we offer this at true cost to sellers?

We need to make money
   from the businesses that we do for CB..
   - And do we include sellers WH and pick/pack cost?

Others

   - Overall, the flow is not easy to understand and do not think we
   thought this properly through..
   - Still struggle to see the overall P&L impact to the CB business and
   why we want to do this



Slide 31

   - What is Anbo SLS?
- For TH, we should just pitch in advance?

If they go to 3PF, it is hard
   to get them back?
- When do we intend to conclude WIP pitching?

Slide 40

   - For CNLS, do we also pitch SG/MY together?

I guess if they can sell to
   MY, they can also sell to SG



Slide 46

   - Shall we use Taobao program incentives?

Think that is quite aligned
   across all markets?

Too many programs are not good in general
   - But overall, we should definitely align the packages if not very hard
   to pitch. Especially the packages of the key import markets



Slide 60 – 68

   - For this, can coordinate with RBP side, they just did a round of clean
   up. Not sure we should be overly generous just to increase Mall % for the
   sake of



Slide 80

   - Ok. But we need to improve the multiples of the existing categories?

For eg. Fashion and F&B seems to be going down as compared to increasing..



Slide 90

   - For Lever 2, how do we ensure that these are indeed TB SKUs?

And not
   just making the number look good?
- For Lever 3, where did we land on OF?

Slide 96

   - We will always have 2 views. Platform NPC and S2S NPC..
   - What is contribution of seed shops to CB business?

Want to understand

content.question:
Thanks.



Slide 6

   - We need to meet both top and bottom line targets?

Will we meet them
   for Nov?
- Fo Nov, our ABS still does not show signs of improvement?

- In general, Nov should be a good month with good traffic and platform
   support?

But it seems like we are performing worse than Sep..





Slide 10

   - Cannot quite understand how to read the table, it does not add up to
   100%



Slide 11

   - I understand that our FBS rates for heavy items are not competitive
   and this is deliberate, but am not sure how this would impact our sourcing?

Slide 13 -14

   - This is probably still not sufficient?

We moved from 68% lower than CB
   seller to probably 65% lower?

Slide 16

   - Are we also looking at category mix as well?

For eg. I know markets
   are keen to push Taobao stuff >> F&B/Fashion/Beauty/Furniture. Are we
   looking at this angle?

Slide 19

   - For Proya, we can still allow them to have pricing control while
   managing consignment model?

All they need to do is to give us the floor
   price that we cannot go below?

Why do we want to have a fix service fee
   model?

Slide 20

   - How did our UE look like on 11.11 vs BAU day?

Is this something that
   we can do more frequently?
There are more campaigns than just 11.11?

For
   eg. 15th, Payday etc.



Slide 24

   - For VN, where are we on exploring 3PFs?
- For BR, they are pushing FBS, are we intending to be part of the push?

Slide 38

   - Does Jap manga collaboration make sense?

Slide 39

   - Did we look at bag accessories in the end?
- And do we have enough accessories/kids fashion in general for this?
- How will we be marketing this?

Slide 41

   - When will the front end feature be ready to do customized clothes?
- When compared to CB sellers, are we able to do it cheaper than them?

If so, it is probably worth investing in it even though the processes are
   manual?

Slide 43

   - PC3 here is daily number?
- What is the contribution of ADO to total Lovito PH ADO?

Slide 46

   - We need to aim for 1-2 launch per month?
Who do we have for VN?
When are we looking at a new KOL collaboration for PH again?

Slide 48

   - Push would probably come more from SCS side as compared to Fashion



Slide 50

   - Bags are not really growing?
- For Kids, do we sell in different shop?

For men do we sell in
   different shop?
Probably should not be putting everything in lovito shop?

content.question:
as long as we do not use the official brand name to sell, is this something
the brands would be fine with and accept to be on SIP?

content.answer:
--> For SIP name, I'd suggest to use the current P shop's name but just
take out the word "official" and keep the brand name. So for brand shop
example, [torridenofficial.os] will be [torriden.th]. Since sellers will
need to consent in one form or the other, they will want their names in it,
but not project the SIP shop as the flagship and just more sensitive with
the word "official".

Slide 6 Haenle Suh <haenle.suh@shopee.com>

   -

   Is there a reason why JPCB non-ads TR% ~3% lower than KRCB?

->(Please refer to below table pointer #1) When looking at L3M(‘25 Aug-Oct
PnL) actuals, the non-ads TR% gap mainly comes from 3 factors; commission
TR%, mkt pck TR% and handling fee TR%;

Commission TR% for JP is lower vs. KR due to ~10%p lower G2N(e.g for Oct JP
G2N 67% vs KR G2N 76%)  and mkt pck TR% lower due to not having enough big
sellers to buy year long CBP(e.g. KR UST Beauty brands year long CBP
enrollment in all markets)

 [image: image.png]

Slide 8 Haenle Suh <haenle.suh@shopee.com>

   -

   What is the issue regarding G2N?

-> No issue as the commission TR% gap(0.18mil out of 0.55mil PC2 gap as of
11/20 previous sharing) was based on “wrong” G2N data as Reg BI didn’t have
CB BU split. Thus now fixed with KRJP specific G2N data, we do not see much
gap in commission TR% nor significant drop in the G2N

-> For your reference, as of 11/26, KRJP PC2 & SLS PnL is above target with
RR 116%(Total 2.45mil - PC2 missing target by 0.13mil but SLS PnL making up
with surplus of 0.5mil) and majority of the PC2 gap coming from KR ads TR%
gap (please refer to below table blue box)

[image: image.png]


Slide 14 – 16 Yuna Kwon <yuna.kwon@shopee.com> Junho Kim
<junho.kim@shopee.com>

   -

   As discussed please go ahead and I suspect Cosrx and Somebymi fall under
   D as compared to A

→ Cosrx is type A they have local OS (MY)
<https://shopee.com.my/cosrx.my?categoryId=100630&entryPoint=ShopByPDP&itemId=29000297333&upstream

      Comparison summarized in the table below

Launch effort

CB Direct

Local Direct

Remarks

Seller origins

CN/KR/JP

ID/MY/VN/TH/PH

-

Seller communication

Y

Y

   -

   Local direct - local RM pitching/Mass pitching

Logistics channel

   -

   channels/global wallet configuration

content.answer:
For KR managed malls, benchmark is their peer group, meaning we make
   managed brands to spend at least to their peer group’s medianTR which is
   30%. Then on average these sellers will need to increase TR by <1% overall
   so this seems doable. How we do it : we give sellers this peer group median
   TR target, give them one quarter to achieve this TR, and if they fail to do
   so then the next quarter we’ll deduct the gap TR% from their escrow. We
   already ran this concept with UST brands during T2T meetings for the past
   few weeks and their main questions were “are our peer brands spending this
   much? Can we get this data for internal approval? Can you also take into
   account our offline & offsite spending.” So I think this is a
   difficult-but-feasible and good way to motivate sellers’ spending for SHP
   conversion and guarantee certain TR%

Why we benchmark peer group is because this is how most KR malls
      actually strategize, as Korean brands have similar branding and are
      competitors to each other

Why we don’t benchmark CODB of TTS is because out of the 11 UST
      managed brands brands I had T2T meetings with this year-end, all except 1
      brand’s plan next year is to cap or shrink TTS GMV due to its thin margin
      and 2 brand among them even closing TTS shop due to margin, so
it’s not the
      proper benchmark for for KR

For KR unmanaged brands/resellers, the benchmark is also their peer
   group. KR reseller average paid ads TR is 2.2% so making these sellers do
   mandatory ads at 2%. Unlike managed mall, cannot apply median because
   median TR is 0% and irrelevant. How we do it : we use the mandatory ads
   program to uplift the topline using it as tool to push for reseller SIP
   adoption as well (TR increasing due to mandatory ads so more pushed to do
   SIP and not pay the non-SIP penalty)

-> Having said that, with systematic programs in place for ‘26, KR can
achieve “even better” MP PC2 + SLS PnL $ vs. the scenario if we follow the
commission increase of CNCB, and below is the proposal with its impact for
your reference. In the very worst case if Legal team says such escrow
deduct for managed malls’ TR% program and mandatory ads program are not
doable in Korean regulatory environment, then we’ll re-propose with
commissions

[image: image.png]

-> Summary: KR proposes NOT to increase base commission but run
KR-customized TR improvement and ads program and aim KR ‘26 topline to
reach yearly 82k ADO (+40% YoY vs. 2025,  which is +20% YoY higher than the
commission increase only scenario). In terms of MP PC2 + SLS PnL, it will
be increased by $11.8M(vs ‘25) reaching yearly $35.6mil which Program only
case will generate “more” PnL of $0.9mil(+2.51% increase) vs. commission
only case. Also overall TR% reaching 18.97% and PC2% 2.59%(overall PnL %
6.17%)

[image: image.png]

================================================================================================

 For Non-Mall + Beauty, across most markets, there is probably room to
close the gap vs CNCB. The question is how to do it.

 I need to understand what the framework governing the thinking works first

   -

   Then I need a solution for KR Mall, KR Non-Mall, JP Non-Mall.

Do you think we can go with +2% in commissions and +2% in compulsory ads
take rate for JPCB?

-> For

Competitor CODB - for JP sellers in other CB EC PLT is known to be
   roughly 3%p higher vs. SHP

global seller center + global wallet testing (for direct); HQ legal
review/modification of TOS.

supporting VN and TH PMO to prep launch, currently on track.


we think that we can open BN to more SEA markets other than MY? How will

Logistics wise, there are 2 options available for each lane: 1) direct

content.answer: +28k new SKUs from mature sellers
-28k SKUs from CO/CL closure

-23k SKUs from non-performing SKU clean up + seller unlist


[image: image.png]

SKU efficiency dropped vs Oct due to

Marketing budget cut in Nov for mature sellers. Mature sellers’P&L
   +$761k Mom (slide 22 below)

Newer SKUs added in Nov diluting the overall SKU efficiency

[image: image.png]

   -

   In general, there are probably too little new SKUs for JST?


Yes. In terms of SKU# per seller, we have about 3k SKU/seller, and when
benchmarked against traditional sources of acquisition such as Lever 1 of
~0.2k/ seller, we are higher.


However, in an absolute SKU # sense, we are working to increase this
through increasing our seller onboarding speed, which will also be
addressed in the question below. The reason for the slow growth is after
our marketing cuts in November, mature sellers experienced fluctuations in
their topline and many new sellers did not generate orders, and this
sparked concerns in the JST sales team to further push more sellers. In the
meantime, we are discussing with JST on the feasibility of an incentive
program where we give them a referral fee for each seller they successfully
refer to us.



   -

   Can we also track seller numbers? How has this grown over time?


We have 302 JST/WDT sellers in total till date, which grew at an average
pace of ~50 sellers per month over the past 5 months since we started the

content.question:
*Agree. We have clarified legal concerns. Legal guidance is that the
eligibility of seller voucher collection should be based on end-buyer
profile and not SIP decision; and the voucher amount needs to be passed to
end-buyer real-time upon collection; this is to legally protect SIP as a
purchase agent for end-buyers, instead of making purchase decisions.
However it conflicts with the current 2-order system design.*

*With above constraints, we are in progress of exploring short-term
workarounds and long-term product solutions with HQ legal and CB BPM. Will
update progress in next weekly update.*



Let’s solve for this. Seller vouchers are meant for end buyers, and SIP is
just a channel we use to allow end buyers to be able to buy. We should be
able to find a workaround for legal concern.



And ID/VN should not have this concern as well.



*For Swarm Document*



Slide 2

   - Can split out OF from here when we look at active sellers and SKUs?

I
   am not sure they will be a big contributor to the business moving forward.
   How does their SKU efficiency compare with JST?

Slide 3

   - I am more concerned with the growth of JST and WDT over time. The pace
   has been overall slow



Slide 4

   can start investing themselves?

- We will need to set targets for JST and WDT
